  • Ability to quickly grasp and apply complex concepts
  • Strong abilities in problem definition and problem-solving
  • Excellent analytical capabilities and experience using complex modeling and analysis to inform key business decisions
  • Excellent oral, written, and PowerPoint communication skills
  • Proven ability to work effectively, and manage others in diverse and distributed teams
  • Ability to work well with all levels within Bain and at client
  • Ability to lead interactions with senior stakeholders (at Bain or Client)
  • Comfort working independently and/or remotely from supervisors in a fast-paced environment
  • Willingness to travel to client locations (expected 20-40%, potential peak of 60%)

Preferred:

  • MBA, or PhD in a technical field. Advanced understanding of data science methodologies, including machine learning and modeling, with a strong analytical background
  • Formal Agile/SCRUM certification. Experience supporting deployment of SaaS solutions with customers/clients or similar roles. Experience in software customer success roles. Experience in technology product management and project management.

 

U.S. Compensation Information 

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service and Bain’s best in class benefits package (details listed below).

Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range to be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows:

  • In New York, California, Texas, Massachusetts, Illinois, and Georgia, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $128,500-$171,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level  
  • For all other locations, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is commensurate with competitive geographic market rates for this role and will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level  
  • Annual discretionary performance bonus  
    • This role may also be eligible for other elements of discretionary compensation  
  • 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, which increases after 3 years of service and is 100% vested upon start date 

Bain & Company's comprehensive benefits and wellness program is designed to help employees achieve personal independence, protection and stability in the areas most important to you and your family. 

Bain pays 100% individual employee premiums for medical, dental and vision programs, offering one of the most comprehensive medical plans for employees without impacting your paycheck  

  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave, sick leave and paid holidays 
  • Fully vested 401(k) company contribution 
  • Paid Life and Long-Term Disability insurance 
  • Annual fitness reimbursements 

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.
job.title: Expert Consultant, Data Sciences, Coro

job.company_description:
BCD Travel creates connections that move people and ideas forward. Through open technology and trusted human expertise, we help companies and people navigate change, simplify complexity and make confident decisions about how and when they travel. Our intuitive digital experiences for every stakeholder power journeys that fuel success and drive progress.
With 15,000+ dedicated team members serving clients in 170+ countries, BCD is shaping a more sustainable future for business travel. Industry-leading meetings and events management and a global consultancy complete our suite of solutions and services. In 2024, BCD achieved US$22.9 billion in sales.
For more information, visit our website. You can also subscribe to our newsletter for the latest industry insights, thought-leader analysis and top tips to drive your successful travel program. Go to: http://ow.ly/N73i50Mhc9Y
job.job_description:
Start your journey with BCD: Grow, connect, collaborate and celebrate with our global team

Senior Director, Travel & Expense (T&E) Management Integration (Remote)

Full time, Remote United States, UK, Spain, Poland, Netherlands, Germany, Denmark, France, Belgium

The Senior Director of T&E Management Integration is a strategic business leader responsible for shaping and executing the vision for seamless integration between travel booking, payment, and expense management ecosystems. This role focuses on driving partnerships and interoperability with leading expense management platforms to deliver measurable value for clients through improved compliance, cost control, and traveler experience.

You will lead cross-functional initiatives that align product strategy, commercial objectives, and partner engagement to create differentiated solutions that strengthen our competitive position in the global travel and payments market.

As a Senior Director, Travel & Expense (T&E) Management Integration, you will

  • Define and own the global strategy for T&E integration, ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and client needs
  • Develop competitive differentiation through advanced integration capabilities that reduce friction and optimize spend visibility
  • Partner with Finance and Commercial teams to create business cases and ROI models demonstrating value for clients and internal stakeholders
  • Build and maintain executive-level relationships with major expense management providers
  • Establish tiered integration frameworks (basic, advanced, premium) to support diverse client requirements and maturity levels
  • Drive adoption of standardized APIs, connectors, and accelerators to reduce implementation complexity and time-to-value
  • Collaborate with Sales Enablement and Marketing to create integration-focused value propositions, RFP libraries, and executive presentation materials
  • Support strategic client engagements, workshops, and QBRs to position integration as a business enabler rather than a technical feature

About You

  • Extensive experience in strategic product leadership or business integration roles within Expense Management, FinTech, or Enterprise SaaS
  • Proven success in building partnerships with expense management platforms and delivering integrated solutions at scale
  • Strong understanding of expense workflows, compliance frameworks, and financial governance
  • Executive presence with ability to influence C-level stakeholders internally and externally
  • MBA or equivalent experience preferred

About Us

We’re a market leader in travel management: We help people and companies travel smart and achieve more, and our clients include some of the world’s best-known and most innovative business and consumer brands. We operate in more than 100 countries, and the majority of our 10,000+ people work virtually (because a 10-second commute usually makes awesome sense). We’re committed to sustainability and to driving success for our customers, our business and ourselves. Learn more about BCD Travel.

Your life at BCD

Working at BCD means connecting, collaborating and celebrating. You’ll be part of a virtual, dynamic and flexible work environment that fosters a good work-life balance so you can focus on what makes you happy. But don’t just take our word for it! Hear why people join BCD and more about the great perks of Life at BCD.

You’ll be offered

  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home or remote opportunities
  • Opportunities to grow your skillset and career
  • Generous vacation days so you can rest and recharge
  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools
  • Travel industry professional perks and discounts
  • An inclusive work environment where diversity is celebrated

At BCD Travel we value you, our culture and success is defined by you. We carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors, including your professional background and experience. The salary range for candidates in applicable jurisdictions in the US for the position of Senior Director, Travel & Expense (T&E) Management Integration is between $130,000 and $150,000. The actual pay depends on your skills, qualifications, experience and geographical location.

Ready to join the journey? Apply now!

We’re dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about a role, but your experience doesn’t align perfectly, we still encourage you to apply.

We are committed to providing reasonable and necessary accommodations to ensure all employees can perform their roles effectively. For accommodation requests or further information, contact our Talent Acquisition department at careers@bcdtravel.com.

Director, Global Program Optimization (Remote)

Full time, United States

The Director, Global Program Optimization is responsible for driving strategy in successfully leading and retaining a portfolio of primarily medium- to large-market and multi-national accounts. The role will also identify expansion opportunities within the existing client base across Travel, Advito & M&E. You will collaborate with our commercial and program management teams to build and deploy customized approaches in support of our existing customers in both proactive bid avoidance and competitive bid activity. This position should have the ability to participate in client-facing presentations and workshops.

As a Director, Global Program Optimization, you will

  • Maintain and increase revenues through client retention and expansion of current customer programs.
  • Help drive program margins and profitability by optimizing programs and retaining accounts.
  • Look for opportunities for deeper program engagement to assist with cross-selling or up-selling products and services beyond standard travel program support
  • Leverage existing data and information to uncover emergent data and trends to improve client retention.
  • Dive into places for clients to consolidate and expand their travel program with BCD where this makes business sense for the client.
  • Proactively engage with business owners regarding the renewal/bid avoidance/rebid/risk status of each assigned account throughout the life of the account.
  • Facilitate project management for the entire bid process pre/during/post bid closeout.
  • Ensure, oversee, and guide the project management components for the entire retention process, including timelines, resource assignments, document management, pricing, and presentations.
  • Ability to manage third party consultants and strategic procurement led processes and competitive bids.

About You

  • Extensive business travel industry experience with previous sales/program management focus
  • Thorough knowledge of business travel management, the current competitive and supplier landscape, and travel industry technology
  • Proficient in business software applications, including MS Excel and MS PowerPoint

  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools

  • An inclusive work environment where diversity is celebrated

At BCD Travel we value you, our culture and success is defined by you. We carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors, including your professional background and experience. Senior Marketing & Communications Strategy Project Manager - Aerospace & Defense Vertical (Remote)

Full time, United States

We are seeking a dynamic and highly organized Project Manager with knowledge and skills in the sales, marketing, and travel industry, and hands-on experience in social media strategy and execution. This role will support the leadership team responsible for the Aerospace & Defense Government Contractor vertical with program initiatives, customer-facing meetings and events, and drive projects that enhance brand presence, customer engagement, and revenue-generating outcomes.

As a Senior Marketing & Communications Strategy Project Manager - Aerospace & Defense Vertical, you will

Project Management

  • Lead end-to-end project planning, execution, and delivery across sales, marketing, and customer-facing initiatives.
  • Develop project timelines, track milestones, and ensure deliverables are completed on time and within scope.
  • Coordinate cross-functional teams, including Marketing, Sales, Product, Communications, and external partners.
  • Oversight of A&D Client Forum and key industry events
  • Manage A&D vertical business plan and key projects

Sales & Marketing Support

  • Liaison with Sales to develop & maintain an A&D sales pipeline for new business
  • Partner with Marketing to create new material highlighting BCD’s value position in this space.
  • Create and maintain project materials such as presentations, briefs, communication plans, benchmarking materials, newsletters, social media posts, website updates, etc.
  • Provide education and collateral for sales and retention teams
  • Analyze campaign and program performance, identifying opportunities for optimization and increased ROI.

Travel Industry Expertise

  • Leverage knowledge of existing A&D customer base via A&D Program Management Workgroup.
  • Support customer-specific travel initiatives, retention strategies, and operational improvement projects, A&D vertical roadmap initiatives, etc.
  • Track progress on all initiatives

Social Media & Digital Engagement

  • Develop and execute social media plans aligned to marketing objectives and customer engagement goals.
  • Manage content calendars, draft posts, and collaborate with design resources to deliver high-quality assets.
  • Track and report social media metrics, engagement performance, trends, and competitive insights.

About You

  • Degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or related field preferred
  • Several years of experience in project management, sales/marketing operations, or program coordination.
  • Experience in the travel industry (corporate travel, travel management companies, suppliers, or hospitality) preferred.
  • Strong understanding of social media platforms, analytics tools, and digital trends.
  • Exceptional communication, organization, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Proficiency with project management tools (e.g., MS Project, Asana, Trello, Smartsheet).

Regional Program Manager

Full time, United States

As a Regional Program Manager, you are responsible for managing a single client account or portfolio of a regional account(s) to include but not limited to client retention, contract negotiation, implementation, business consolidation, sales of products and services, financials, business planning, and reviews, establishing relationships with key decision makers in multiple regions, and staff development. You’ll report to global program management.

As a Regional Program Manager, you will

  • Oversee the regional coordination of all communication across the region, both BCD Travel and the client’s global initiatives
  • Manage new business implementations, working closely with the implementation team to ensure client expectations are met
  • Help measure and report Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on a regional level and develop deliberate business plans
  • Drive profitability of the contract on a regional basis by increasing revenues through up-selling and cross-selling to meet client’s strategic needs
  • Identify and assist in the selection of technology tools and processes to produce desired results
  • Establish and maintain relationships with key customer decision-makers in the region
  • Perform client business reviews and deliver regular financial reporting to all stakeholders

About You

  • Experience implementing and managing large market or regional clients
  • Thorough knowledge of business travel account management and operations
  • Adept with travel industry technology and processes
  • Demonstrated ability to successfully build, maintain, and grow customer portfolios and client relationships

job.company_description: Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH) is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. The challenger in upscale and a leader in midscale and extended stay, Choice® has nearly 7,500 hotels, representing more than 630,000 rooms, in 45 countries and territories. A diverse portfolio of 22 brands that range from full-service upper upscale properties to midscale, extended stay, and economy enables Choice® to meet travelers' needs in more places and for more occasions while driving more value for franchise owners and shareholders. The award-winning Choice Privileges® rewards program and co-brand credit card options provide members with a fast and easy way to earn reward nights and personalized perks. For more information, visit www.choicehotels.com. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in the Washington metropolitan area (North Bethesda, MD), our IT center in Scottsdale, AZ, and Minneapolis, MN, and through our associates across the globe, we keep those hotels humming by serving as a champion for our franchisees. When you #MakeItYourChoice, you will have the environment, the tools, and the momentum to drive your career and organizational success.
job.job_description:
Job Summary

The Organizational Effectiveness Lead is responsible for managing employee listening strategies, learning platforms, and talent enablement initiatives within the Talent & Culture team. This role ensures systems and programs are aligned with business priorities, easy to use, and deliver meaningful impact on employee engagement and leadership capability.

Key Responsibilities

Employee Listening & Insights

  • Own the employee listening strategy, including survey planning, cadence, execution, and evolution.
  • Serve as the primary administrator and internal expert for the Glint platform.
  • Translate employee feedback into actionable insights that improve engagement, performance, and retention.
  • Partner with HR and business leaders to support thoughtful follow-up and accountability.
  • Ensure data accuracy, confidentiality, and a respectful employee experience.

Learning Platform Management

  • Manage day-to-day operations of the learning experience/learning management platform.
  • Oversee processes including user access, content organization, reporting, and optimization.
  • Ensure systems are simple, intuitive, and operationally effective for an enterprise-wide workforce.
  • Continuously evaluate and improve platform usage to meet evolving business needs.

Talent Enablement & Program Support

  • Support the design, facilitation, and delivery of talent and leadership development experiences.
  • Facilitate select sessions with credibility and practicality, particularly for people leaders.
  • Manage program timelines, logistics, materials, and communication for programs and initiatives.
  • Gather feedback and adjust programs to ensure relevance and impact.

Partnership & Execution

  • Collaborate with HR partners, internal communications, and business leaders.
  • Balance multiple priorities in a fast-paced, service-oriented environment.
  • Demonstrate strong time management, adaptability, and resilience.
  • Take a solutions-oriented approach focused on progress, clarity, and follow-through.

Qualifications

Experience

  • 5-7 years of progressive experience in organizational effectiveness, employee experience, people operations, or talent enablement.
  • Hands-on experience managing people systems (employee listening platforms, LMS/LXP, or similar tools).
  • Exposure to facilitation and talent program delivery.
  • Experience supporting leaders in hospitality, service, or multi-location environments is a plus.

Technical Skills

  • Proficiency in employee listening and learning platforms.
  • Ability to synthesize data and feedback into practical recommendations.
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

Competencies

  • Business and operational mindset.
  • Clear, confident communicator who builds trust with partners and leaders.
  • Comfortable working independently and navigating ambiguity.

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Organizational Development, Business, or related field or equivalent combination of education and work experience.

Certifications

  • Prosci Change Management, PMP, DiSC Assessment, Predictive Index, Hogan Assessment, Korn Ferry and other talent assessment certifications helpful

SALARY RANGE:

The salary range for this position is $99,919 – 117,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

WORK LOCATION:

This position is based at Choice Hotels’ Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland. The selected candidate must be able to commit to this location requirement and adhere to the company’s hybrid work schedule, which consists of four days onsite and one day remote per week.

NOTE:

This position is not eligible for relocation assistance or sponsorship.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver

job.job_description:
Job Summary

The Senior Manager, Credit & Collections provides strategic and operational leadership for Choice Hotels’ Credit and Collections functions. This role is responsible for managing enterprise-wide credit risk, optimizing cash flow, and ensuring consistent adherence to credit policies across the organization. The Senior Manager partners closely with Finance leadership and cross-functional stakeholders to drive strong financial performance while supporting positive franchise relationships and sustainable business growth.

Responsibilities

Collections Strategy & Financial Performance

  • Analyze accounts receivable data to identify collection opportunities, leveraging advanced Excel and analytical tools to drive data-informed decisions.
  • Develop and oversee collection strategies to reduce aged receivables and improve Days Sales Outstanding (DSO).
  • Establish performance metrics and reporting to monitor portfolio health, identify risks, and track progress against targets.
  • Oversee account defaults, terminations, and the write-off process for bad debt expense.
  • Provide senior-level oversight and guidance on franchisee outreach strategies; support resolution of complex delinquency root causes while balancing risk management and franchise relationships.

Strategic Credit Leadership

  • Lead the Credit function, including credit evaluation, approval, monitoring, and ongoing risk management for franchise and corporate accounts.
  • Own and continuously enhance credit policies and procedures, ensuring alignment with business objectives, risk tolerance, and industry best practices.
  • Ensure consistent application and enforcement of credit policy across all brands and portfolios through governance, controls, and ongoing team accountability; review and approve complex or high-risk credit decisions and exceptions.
  • Proactively identify credit risk trends and recommend mitigation strategies to Finance leadership.

People Leadership & Talent Development

  • Lead, coach, and develop high-performing Credit and Collections teams through clear expectations, ongoing feedback, and performance management.
  • Manage hiring, onboarding, training, and succession planning to build a scalable and engaged team.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and collaboration.

Cross-Functional Partnership

  • Partner with Legal, Franchise Operations, Billing, and Finance to resolve complex credit and collection matters.
  • Serve as the escalation point for sensitive, high-impact, or enterprise-risk accounts, including large or multi-property licensees.
  • Communicate effectively with internal stakeholders to balance risk management with business and franchise needs.

Process Improvement & Innovation

  • Drive continuous improvement and automation across credit and collections workflows, including the evaluation and implementation of AI-enabled solutions.
  • Improve efficiency, consistency, and scalability of processes while maintaining strong internal controls.

Reporting & Executive Support

  • Prepare and deliver daily, weekly, and monthly reporting, including executive-level summaries and insights.
  • Escalate high-risk accounts and emerging trends to Finance leadership with clear, actionable recommendations.
  • Support ad hoc analyses and special projects as business needs evolve.

Qualifications

Employment Experience

  • 8+ years of progressive experience in credit, collections, accounts receivable, or financial operations.
  • 5+ years of people leadership experience managing and developing teams.

Technical Skills

  • Strong analytical skills with advanced Excel skills, including VLOOKUPs, Power Pivot, and Macros.
  • Demonstrated experience developing and enforcing credit policies and managing credit risk.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Outlook, PowerPoint, and Word.

Additional Skills & Competencies

  • Proven ability to partner cross-functionally and influence at multiple levels of the organization.
  • Demonstrated success in reducing aged receivables and improving collection outcomes.
  • Strong conflict resolution and negotiation skills.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication abilities.

Education Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or a related field or an equivalent combination of education and work experience.
  • Experience in franchising, hospitality, or multi-unit environments preferred.

WORK LOCATION:

This position is based at Choice Hotels’ Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland. The selected candidate must be able to commit to this location requirement and adhere to the company’s hybrid work schedule, which consists of four days onsite and one day remote per week.

NOTE:

This position is not eligible for relocation assistance or sponsorship.

Salary Range

The salary range for this position is $93,000 to $110,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, qualifications, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver
job.title: Senior Manager, Credit and Collections

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all of our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.

We are seeking a Grade 7 Customer Trip Product Owner to own the context building of trip details and steward the enterprise trip data repository. You will define how trip events, customer and flight attributes, and operational signals are captured at the lowest data level to create a trustworthy, real-time source of truth. This role also enables internal customers to onboard to the platform and partners closely with the Notifications Business Rules Engine to feed timely, accurate payloads for customer communications.

Scope & Impact

  • Owns the trip context domain and the enterprise trip data repository—schemas, events, relationships, and governance.
  • Influences Customer and Flight data product roadmaps to ensure essential context is modeled and maintained at the lowest data level.
  • Enables internal customers (operations, care, digital, analytics) to onboard and build on the platform with reliable APIs and documentation.
  • Feeds the Notifications Business Rules Engine with timely and accurate context to power correct template selection and payload construction.

Tools & Platforms

  • Data: Event streaming, ETL/ELT pipelines, data catalog/lineage, schema registries, monitoring/alerting.
  • Identity & Consent: Customer ID resolution, preference management, consent capture and enforcement.
  • Notifications: Business Rules Engine, template services, push gateways, SMS aggregators, email providers.
  • Delivery & Docs: Backlog/roadmap tools, API gateways, developer portals, documentation platforms.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Roadmap
  • Define a multi-quarter roadmap for trip context, repository capabilities, and platform enablement tied to measurable outcomes.
  • Translate objectives into epics/features with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies across Customer and Flight data products.
  • Prioritize based on customer impact, operational risk, compliance, and technical feasibility; manage trade-offs transparently.
  • Trip Context Data Management
  • Own canonical trip schemas, event definitions, and relationships across bookings, changes, disruptions, and resolutions.
  • Set data quality SLAs and validation rules (freshness, completeness, accuracy, consistency); drive remediation for defects.
  • Ensure context is modeled at the lowest level necessary to support eligibility, personalization, and compliance use cases.
  • Partner with engineering on event-driven architectures and APIs; define contracts and versioning for backward compatibility.
  • Platform Onboarding & Enablement
  • Create onboarding guides, sample payloads, SDKs, and reference integrations for internal customers.
  • Run intake, discovery, and readiness assessments; align on use cases, data needs, guardrails, and service levels.
  • Establish support processes (runbooks, SLAs, office hours) and feedback loops to improve developer experience.
  • Integration with Notifications Business Rules Engine
  • Provide timely, accurate trip context to the rules engine for template eligibility, selection, and payload construction.
  • Co-design suppression, sequencing, and failover logic informed by data quality, customer preferences, and operational realities.
  • Maintain shared observability (dashboards, alerts) for event-to-send latency, payload correctness, and incident response.
  • Architecture & Cross-Domain Integration
  • Collaborate with Customer and Flight data product owners to influence schemas, identifiers, and lifecycle events.
  • Integrate with identity resolution, consent/preference services, and eligibility engines to enable personalization and compliance.
  • Plan cutovers, deprecations, and migration paths that reduce complexity and safeguard data lineage.
  • Compliance, Risk & Controls
  • Ensure adherence to privacy, security, and regulatory requirements (e.g., CAN-SPAM/TCPA for contactability, GDPR/CCPA for data rights).
  • Steward consent, preferences, and retention policies in trip context; maintain audit trails and data governance artifacts.
  • Define incident management runbooks and escalation paths for data issues impacting communications and operations.
  • Stakeholder & Change Management
  • Align cross-functional stakeholders (Ops, Care, Digital, Marketing, Legal, Security) on priorities and release plans.
  • Lead enablement for new capabilities—training, documentation, and change communications for internal customers.
  • Facilitate UAT and incorporate feedback to improve clarity, correctness, and developer experience.
  • Delivery & Execution
  • Maintain an outcomes-focused backlog; provide clear requirements, data contracts, and acceptance criteria.
  • Plan iterative releases; measure impact with dashboards and adjust based on performance and stakeholder feedback.
  • Focus on cross-functional collaboration over ceremony compliance; unblock dependencies proactively.

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 5–7+ years in Product Ownership/Management with experience in data products or platforms.
  • Deep familiarity with data modeling, event-driven architectures, and data quality/governance practices.
  • Experience integrating with messaging/notification systems and consent/preference services.
  • Strong analytical skills; ability to diagnose data issues and reason about lineage, timeliness, and correctness.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management; able to align diverse teams on shared data contracts and outcomes.
  • Working knowledge of privacy, security, and regulatory considerations for customer communications and data handling.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience in travel/transportation or other real-time, operations-heavy domains.
  • Exposure to customer data platforms, identity resolution, and eligibility/personalization engines.
  • Familiarity with experimentation (A/B testing), accessibility, localization, and resiliency patterns.
  • Knowledge of cloud data ecosystems and observability tooling (logs, metrics, tracing, alerting).

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare
job.title: Product Owner, Trip Context and Platform Enablement

job.company_description: Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH) is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. The challenger in upscale and a leader in midscale and extended stay, Choice® has nearly 7,500 hotels, representing more than 630,000 rooms, in 45 countries and territories. A diverse portfolio of 22 brands that range from full-service upper upscale properties to midscale, extended stay, and economy enables Choice® to meet travelers' needs in more places and for more occasions while driving more value for franchise owners and shareholders. The award-winning Choice Privileges® rewards program and co-brand credit card options provide members with a fast and easy way to earn reward nights and personalized perks. For more information, visit www.choicehotels.com. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in the Washington metropolitan area (North Bethesda, MD), our IT center in Scottsdale, AZ, and Minneapolis, MN, and through our associates across the globe, we keep those hotels humming by serving as a champion for our franchisees. When you #MakeItYourChoice, you will have the environment, the tools, and the momentum to drive your career and organizational success.
job.job_description:
JOB SUMMARY:

The Benefits & Wellbeing Communications Manager leads the execution, and continuous improvement of Choice Hotels’ benefits and wellbeing programs. This role is responsible for delivering exceptional associate support through the administration of benefits programs, coordinating wellbeing initiatives, and managing clear, timely communications. This role partners cross-functionally across HR to include Corporate Communications, Belonging and external vendors to deliver a best-in-class associate experience. This role is pivotal in shaping the associate experience through impactful communications and holistic wellbeing initiatives.

Ready to make a real impact? The Benefits & Wellbeing Communications Manager is the driving force behind inspiring our Choice Hotels family to embrace health and wellbeing—for themselves and their loved ones! This isn’t just a role; it’s a mission to energize and empower associates to take full advantage of programs designed to help them thrive.

You’ll lead the charge in delivering exceptional associate support, ensuring our benefits programs run seamlessly while championing wellbeing initiatives that truly make a difference. Through clear, timely, and compelling communications, you’ll spark engagement and create a culture where wellbeing is front and center.

Collaboration is key—you’ll partner across HR, Corporate Communications, Belonging, and external vendors to craft a best-in-class associate experience. Your work will shape how our associates feel, connect, and care for themselves and their families. If you’re passionate about creating impact through dynamic communications and holistic wellbeing strategies, this is your opportunity to shine!

RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Serve as the primary escalation point for complex associate inquiries regarding benefits (medical, dental, vision, life, disability, HSA, FSA, 401(k), etc.) providing exceptional customer service.
  • Oversee benefits onboarding, changes, terminations, and retirement processes, ensuring compliance and accuracy.
  • Lead the design, implementation, and evaluation of company-wide wellbeing initiatives (physical, mental, and financial health).
  • Manage annual health fairs, wellness events, and the ongoing wellness calendar.
  • Develop and execute a comprehensive communications strategy for benefits and wellbeing programs.
  • Partner with Corporate Communications to ensure consistent, clear, and engaging messaging across all channels.
  • Collaborate with Belonging team to integrate Belonging and Wellbeing initiates seamlessly to deliver on Choice’s employer brand, Make if your Choice.
  • Ensure all programs comply with federal, state, and local regulations (HIPAA, PPACA, ERISA, COBRA, FMLA, Section 125, etc.).
  • Manage relationships with benefits and wellness vendors and monitor service levels.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Employment Experience

  • Minimum 5 years of progressive experience in benefits administration, communications, and / or wellbeing program management.
  • Demonstrated experience managing cross-functional projects and leading initiatives.

Technical Skills

  • Proficiency in HRIS systems and Microsoft Office Suite.

Additional Skills & Competencies

  • Strong knowledge of benefits regulations and best practices.
  • Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and project management skills.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, changing environment.
  • Commitment to upholding company values

Education Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, Communications, or related field or equivalent combination of education and work experience.

SALARY RANGE:

The salary range for this position is $112,000 - $125,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

WORK LOCATION:

Candidates must reside in the Washington, D.C. metro area and commit to a hybrid work schedule consisting of four days per week onsite at our Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, and one day working remotely from their home office.

NOTE:

We do not provide relocation assistance or sponsorship assistance for this role.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all of our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.

We are seeking a Product Owner to lead enterprise data management and customer communications for trip experiences. This role owns the strategy and delivery of accurate, timely, and personalized notifications across push, SMS, and email. You will ensure the data that powers these communications is reliable, governed, secure, and integrated across systems—so customers receive clear information at the right moment of their journey.

Scope & Impact

  • Owns the rules engine that will drive accurate, consistent and timely notifications to our customers, interfaces closely with underlying data capabilities.
  • Operates at enterprise scale across multiple systems, channels, and business functions (e.g., Operations, Customer Care, Marketing, Legal).
  • Balances customer experience, regulatory compliance, operational realities, and technical constraints to deliver measurable outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Roadmap
  • Define a customer-centric vision and multi-quarter roadmap for trip communications and the supporting data foundation.
  • Translate outcomes into epics and features with clear success metrics and acceptance criteria.
  • Prioritize the backlog based on customer impact, risk, compliance requirements, and technical feasibility.
  • Customer Trip Communications (Omnichannel)
  • Own trip-lifecycle triggers and content across channels (push, SMS, email) including timing, frequency, and suppression logic.
  • Define personalization rules (identity, trip context, language, preferences) and guardrails (rate limiting, quiet hours, failover).
  • Coordinate with Operations and Customer Care to ensure messages reflect real-time operational realities and support resolution paths.
  • Architecture & Integration
  • Collaborate with engineering on event-driven architectures and APIs that support near real-time communications.
  • Integrate with customer data platforms and messaging providers (e.g., push gateways, SMS aggregators, email service) to ensure deliverability and observability.
  • Manage dependencies across systems (identity resolution, preferences, eligibility engines) and plan cutovers/deprecations to reduce complexity.
  • Compliance, Risk & Controls
  • Ensure adherence to applicable regulations (e.g., CAN-SPAM, TCPA, GDPR/CCPA) and internal policies.
  • Maintain audit trails, data retention practices, and escalation/runbooks for incidents affecting communications.
  • Partner with Legal, Security, and Privacy teams to review new features, templates, and data uses.
  • Stakeholder & Change Management
  • Align cross-functional stakeholders on priorities, trade-offs, and release plans; communicate status and risks clearly.
  • Lead go-to-market readiness for new communication capabilities—training, support materials, and operational runbooks.
  • Facilitate user acceptance testing and incorporate feedback to improve clarity and effectiveness.
  • Delivery & Execution
  • Maintain a transparent, outcomes-focused backlog; provide clear requirements and acceptance criteria.
  • Plan iterative releases and validate impact with data; adjust based on performance and feedback.
  • Participate in core delivery rituals as needed; focus on cross-functional collaboration over ceremony compliance.

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 5–7+ years in Product Ownership/Management with demonstrated experience in enterprise data or communication products.
  • Hands-on familiarity with data governance, data quality, and event-driven architectures.
  • Experience with customer messaging platforms (e.g., push, SMS, email) and preference/consent management.
  • Strong analytical skills—comfort with metrics, dashboards, and diagnosing data/communication issues.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; ability to craft clear requirements and align diverse stakeholders.
  • Working knowledge of privacy, security, and regulatory considerations for customer communications.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience in travel/transportation or other real-time, operations-heavy domains.
  • Exposure to customer data platforms, identity resolution, and personalization engines.
  • Familiarity with experimentation (A/B testing), accessibility guidelines, and localization at scale.
  • Knowledge of cloud data ecosystems and observability tooling (e.g., logs, tracing, alerting).

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together

job.job_description:
Job Summary

The Senior Manager, Analytics & AI Strategy plays a pivotal role in advancing Choice Hotels’ enterprise transformation through analytics, AI, and emerging technologies. Sitting within the centralized Advanced Analytics team, this role ensures that data, analytics, and AI are systematically aligned with Choice Hotels’ most critical business priorities and are adopted across the organization.

As a cross-functional leader, the Senior Manager connects strategy across four essential pillars: decision-centric processes, enterprise data and AI strategy, organizational enablement, and content strategy for emerging platforms. The role will identify and prioritize high-impact opportunities, guide the data and AI strategy needed to support them, lead adoption and fluency programs that drive organizational change, and ensure Choice Hotels’ content and brand are represented effectively within the evolving AI landscape.

The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker and influencer who can operate seamlessly across teams and levels of the organization. They bring strong project management and communication skills, consulting-style problem solving, and the ability to convert complex challenges into actionable strategies that deliver measurable business impact.

Primary Responsibilities

Drive Strategy & Processes for Decision-Centric Analytics & AI

  • Define the strategic framework for how Choice Hotels prioritizes and deploys analytics and AI against the organization’s most critical business decisions.
  • Design and refine operating processes for the Advanced Analytics team to ensure resources are allocated to the highest-value opportunities and away from lower-impact work.
  • Develop standardized methods for scoping, evaluating, and tracking use cases, ensuring clarity on business objectives, success metrics, and ROI.
  • Lead efforts within the Advanced Analytics team to reinforce a decision-centric mindset, ensuring models, dashboards, and analytics are designed with clear business impact in mind.
  • Create processes and/or identify tools that capture, measure, and feedback outcomes of business decisions influenced by analytics, establishing a closed loop for measurement and continuous improvement.
  • Design and implement a portfolio management framework to oversee the lifecycle of analytics data products, ensuring alignment with enterprise priorities.

Data Strategy for Analytics & AI

  • Define the data roadmap for analytics use cases, ensuring that investments in data sources, platforms, and capabilities are aligned with Choice’s highest-value business decisions.
  • Partner with Technology and Data Engineering to improve data accessibility, quality, and readiness for analytics and AI deployment.
  • Establish strategy and processes for customer data activation and monetization, balancing innovation and revenue opportunities with privacy, compliance, and brand protection.
  • Support the launch of the franchisee satisfaction program, ensuring survey design and data capture processes provide the Advanced Analytics team with a robust foundation to deliver actionable owner insights.

Drive AI & Data Enablement

  • Lead the rollout of AI fluency program to the Choice organization in partnership with HR and Technology.
  • Drive enterprise data democratization by supporting the rollout of the data catalog and data dictionaries, making data easier to discover and use across the business.
  • Develop and maintain common data practice guidelines and example queries that promote consistency and quality in analytics work.
  • Refresh and maintain data literacy tools and training (e.g., SQL assessments, practice resources) to elevate foundational skills across the organization.

Lead Content & LLM Strategy

  • Own project management and coordination of the enterprise LLM content strategy, aligning stakeholders across Digital, Marketing, Distribution, Sales, Reputation Management, Analytics, Technology, and Brand.
  • Facilitate alignment across sub-committees of the enterprise content working group, ensuring a consistent approach to LLM content visibility.
  • Monitor generative AI and LLM industry trends, translating insights into actionable strategies for Choice Hotels.
  • Prepare executive-ready materials and recommendations for steering committees and senior leadership to guide decision-making in this evolving space.

Support Advanced Analytics Team Effectiveness

  • Organize and deliver team-wide forums (e.g., town halls, learning sessions) to build engagement and share updates on analytics initiatives.
  • Oversee operational processes including budget tracking, project intake, and knowledge-sharing platforms (e.g., SharePoint).
  • Develop decision-support materials and presentations for senior leaders, ensuring complex analytics initiatives are communicated clearly and effectively.
  • Identify and implement process improvements that enhance team efficiency and strengthen cross-functional collaboration.

Qualifications

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Data Science, Business, or related field or equivalent combination of education and work experience.
  • Advanced degree preferred.

Experience

  • 5+ years of experience across consulting, analytics, AI strategy, and related fields, with a proven ability to lead cross‑functional initiatives and drive data‑informed strategic outcomes.
  • Strong project management, consulting-style problem solving, communication skills.
  • Exceptional storytelling and presentation (PowerPoint) skills.
  • Proven ability to influence stakeholders and drive alignment across diverse groups.
  • Ability to balance strategic vision with operational execution.
  • Experience leading cross-functional initiatives, building trust and alignment across diverse stakeholder groups.
  • Demonstrated expertise in change management, with a track record of driving adoption of new processes, tools, or ways of working.
  • Experience in hospitality, customer experience, or franchisee engagement is a plus.
  • Knowledge of data strategy, architecture, and governance principles is preferred.
  • This role requires on-site work in a designated office cubicle at least four days per week, availability during standard East Coast business hours, and active use of company communication tools, including enabled email and instant message notifications, throughout the workday.

Technical Skills

  • Familiarity with AI platforms, data governance, and analytics tools.
  • Understanding of LLMs and emerging AI technologies.

Competencies

  • Strategic thinking, influence without authority, and ability to drive organizational change.
  • Excellent collaboration and stakeholder management skills.

WORK LOCATION:

This position is based at Choice Hotels’ Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland. The selected candidate must be able to commit to this location requirement and adhere to the company’s hybrid work schedule, which consists of four days onsite and one day remote per week.

NOTE:

This position is not eligible for relocation assistance or sponsorship.

Salary Range

The salary range for this position is $132,000 to $155,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, qualifications, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver
job.title: Analytics & AI Strategy Senior Manager

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

Become a member of Delta’s World Class DOM PRM team as a Domestic Revenue Management Sr Analyst. Delta’s RM professionals are responsible for maximizing the revenue generated on each flight and customer segment by aligning passenger demand with willingness to pay to offer the optimal mix of fare products. Core to Delta’s industry-leading revenue results, each Sr Analyst uses a rigorous, innovative set of demand management and competitive pricing strategies. Every Revenue Management Sr Analyst blends the art of demand management with the science of analytical expertise to create the right price for each customer on every flight. 

As part of the department onboarding process, you will participate in a training cohort with the expectation of successful completion of the training courses and knowledge assessments.

DOM PRM Team Functions

  • If placed in a Pricing role, you will monitor, initiate and communicate industry pricing actions including changes to fare levels, fare rules, policies, and pricing strategy execution
    • Proactively manage Delta’s fare structure including prices and fare rules
    • Identify and analyze market issues and trends, determining appropriate pricing actions, filing of actions, and post-audit assessment of actions
  • If placed in an Inventory management role, you will be responsible for flight level revenue optimization by properly valuing our passengers and forecasting future demand levels
    • Monitor and update the flight level demand forecasts through the analysis of historical and future booking trends, the pricing environment, industry capacity trends, the competitive landscape, and other factors
    • Establish and maintain the flight level overbooking forecasts through the analysis of no-show behavior trends
  • Use various and complex high-level dashboards, prescriptive to descriptive analysis, along with market specific data, to create an appropriate customer offer to best segment customers and maximize revenue
  • Diagnose, interpret, and communicate basic market performance of revenue stats: LF, Yield, RASM, local vs. flow, drivers and actions
  • Evaluate performance and post-departure trends to identify and leverage future opportunities while maximizing the revenue potential for each market
  • Performing frequent ad-hoc analyses related to the assigned markets
  • Communicate market trends, updates, recommendations and overall performance results to Revenue Management leaders and key stakeholders within the Commercial team
  • Provide technical support to other revenue management groups and both internal and external distribution channels and reporting
  • Work cross-functionally with Inventory Management, Pricing, Network Planning, Alliances, Sales, Marketing, and other teams to ensure alignment on market level strategies
What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 2+ years of relevant work experience
  • Detail-oriented, analytical ability are required
  • Strong presentation, public speaking and written communication skills are required
  • Demonstrate ability to manage the frequent market dynamic changes quickly and function in a high pace environment
  • Must be able to set priorities in a time sensitive environment based on revenue impact and be able to manage several responsibilities at once
  • Must be intellectually curious, interested in problem solving where multiple variables are impacting market performance
  • Must be able to work efficiently as part of a team and as an individual contributor, embracing diverse people, thinking and styles
  • Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office software is required
  • Must be performing satisfactorily in current position
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Bachelor's degree-Preferred major in Business, Finance, Economics, Marketing, or Statistics
  • Familiarity with airline economic and industry experience preferred

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare
job.title: Sr Analyst, Domestic Revenue Management
