job.job_description:
Tech that takes you places


We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together.


Drive the strategic initiatives that unlock new growth across CarTrawler’s commercial engine. This role sits right at the intersection of strategy and execution, shaping priorities, building winning go-to-market approaches and turning insight into action across our most important opportunities. Working closely with Commercial Leadership and cross-functional teams, you’ll lead high-impact, cross-functional strategic initiatives from ideation to implementation, ensuring alignment and measurable outcomes while bringing clear, board-ready storytelling to the moments that matter. If you thrive in fast-paced environments, love solving complex commercial problems and enjoy influencing through data and crisp narrative, this is your runway.


This role can be based in Dublin or UK, with periodic travel to our Dublin headquarters to support important in-person meetings and collaboration.


Your Journey starts here!

What you’ll do as a Commercial Strategy Lead

  • Strategic Responsibilities
  • Shape long-term commercial and partner strategies that drive sustainable growth and advance CarTrawler’s overarching strategic objectives.
  • Develop winning go-to-market strategies to enter new segments and secure high-value partners, working closely with Commercial Leadership.
  • Create differentiated business models, value propositions and prospect engagement strategies tailored to partner segments and regions.
  • Partner with cross-functional teams (Partnerships, Trading, Supply, Product, Marketing, Finance) to ensure strategies and GTM plans reflect market trends, prospect needs, and evolving platform capabilities.
  • Embed AI-driven thinking and tooling into commercial workflows – improving decision-making, enhancing commercial modelling, and driving new ways of working across the Commercial organisation.


  • Core Responsibilities
  • Lead high-impact, cross-functional strategic initiatives from idea through to implementation, tracking alignment and measurable outcomes.
  • Partner with Business Development to prioritise high-value opportunities, stress-test negotiation approaches, and help shape winning strategies.
  • Build and refine high-quality commercial proposals with Product Marketing and stakeholders—clear, rigorous and tailored to the partner and segment.
  • Deliver market, competitor, and win/loss insights that sharpen our commercial strategy and improve how we sell.
  • Improve deal governance and commercial frameworks (pricing structures, modelling, rate cards, approvals, and proposition rules by segment/tier).
  • Create concise, insight-led updates for the Leadership Team and Board on pipeline performance, major opportunities, and strategic progress.


What you’ll bring to the team

  • 3–5 years’ experience in strategy, commercial strategy or management consulting—ideally in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
  • Strong commercial and financial acumen, including pricing, commercial modelling and evaluating revenue/profitability trade-offs.
  • Excellent analytical problem-solving skills, able to turn complex data into clear, actionable recommendations.
  • Confident communicator and storyteller, comfortable presenting to senior executives
  • Proven ability to influence and align stakeholders across a matrixed organisation, challenging constructively and driving decisions forward.
  • A pragmatic, ownership mindset, happy to work independently end-to-end and excited by using AI, data and automation to improve ways of working.


Go on, give it a go!

We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected.

At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity, or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here.


Perks for the journey

· Competitive pay and benefits, including a performance-based annual bonus, fully subsidised VHI health insurance and pension matching up to 4%.

· Generous time off and flexible working, including 25 days’ annual leave, extra company days, monthly Friday Unplugged afternoons and the option to work abroad for up to 20 days a year.

· Comprehensive wellbeing support, including access to health and mental health programmes (HeadsUp and EAP) and paid leave for marriage, volunteering and personal wellbeing including menstrual, menopause, and fertility leave.

· Growth and recognition culture with development opportunities through training, coaching and study support plus programmes that celebrate individual and team achievements.

· Everyday perks that make a difference - subsidised canteen (KC Peaches), on-site car parking, Bike to Work and TaxSaver schemes plus an active Sports & Social Club.
job.title: Commercial Strategy Lead

job.company_description:
BCD Travel creates connections that move people and ideas forward. Through open technology and trusted human expertise, we help companies and people navigate change, simplify complexity and make confident decisions about how and when they travel. Our intuitive digital experiences for every stakeholder power journeys that fuel success and drive progress.
With 15,000+ dedicated team members serving clients in 170+ countries, BCD is shaping a more sustainable future for business travel. Industry-leading meetings and events management and a global consultancy complete our suite of solutions and services. In 2024, BCD achieved US$22.9 billion in sales.
For more information, visit our website. You can also subscribe to our newsletter for the latest industry insights, thought-leader analysis and top tips to drive your successful travel program. Go to: http://ow.ly/N73i50Mhc9Y
job.job_description:
Start your journey with BCD: Grow, connect, collaborate and celebrate with our global team

Customer Success Consultant (Remote)

Full time, Spain

This position will proactively manage a portfolio of clients using the GetGoing platform, which is BCD Travel’s all-in-one digital travel & expense management solution. The primary objective is to grow the revenue of our client's portfolio by turning customers into advocates. This includes, but is not limited to, client onboarding, adoption, expansion, retention, as well as client portfolio profitability management.

You will establish collaborative relationships with our clients and act as the Voice of the Customer for your portfolio within our organization to ensure that GetGoing delivers the most value to clients.

As a Customer Success Consultant, you will

  • Lead the onboarding activities for new clients with the objective to drive speed-to-revenue
  • Support clients’ desired outcomes through advice, education, and digital resources
  • Identify growth and expansion opportunities, and work to expand client user engagement
  • Work with clients to create a plan that aligns with their overall strategy
  • Coordinate communication between GetGoing/BCD Travel and clients
  • Be responsible for the profitability and organic growth of your client portfolio

About You

  • Prior client facing account management business travel experience required
  • Global-mindset with the ability to communicate proactively and foster positive business relationships
  • English + French, both fluently spoken and written. German language skills in addition is a plus
  • Sales perspective with willingness to dig into technical details when needed
  • Analytical skills and the ability to suggest ways to improve processes

About Us

We’re a market leader in travel management: We help people and companies travel smart and achieve more, and our clients include some of the world’s best-known and most innovative business and consumer brands. We operate in more than 100 countries, and the majority of our 10,000+ people work virtually (because a 10-second commute usually makes awesome sense). We’re committed to sustainability and to driving success for our customers, our business and ourselves. Learn more about BCD Travel.

Your life at BCD

Working at BCD means connecting, collaborating and celebrating. You’ll be part of a virtual, dynamic and flexible work environment that fosters a good work-life balance so you can focus on what makes you happy. But don’t just take our word for it! Hear why people join BCD and more about the great perks of Life at BCD.

You’ll be offered

  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home or remote opportunities
  • Opportunities to grow your skillset and career
  • Generous vacation days so you can rest and recharge
  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools
  • Travel industry professional perks and discounts
  • An inclusive work environment where diversity is celebrated

Ready to join the journey? Apply now!

We’re dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about a role, but your experience doesn’t align perfectly, we still encourage you to apply.

We are committed to providing reasonable and necessary accommodations to ensure all employees can perform their roles effectively. For accommodation requests or further information, contact our Talent Acquisition department at careers@bcdtravel.com.
job.title: Customer Success Consultant

job.job_description:
Tech that takes you places

We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together.


In this role, you’ll shape how CarTrawler’s story is visually experienced by partners around the world. Leading a small, high-impact design team, you’ll define the creative vision and strategic direction for how we present our propositions, pitches and partner experiences ensuring every touchpoint reflects our innovation and brand strength. Acting as a bridge between Commercial, Product and Marketing, you’ll translate complex ideas into compelling visual narratives that drive connection, clarity and growth.


Your Journey starts here!


What you’ll do as Head of Value Creation:

  • Play a central role in ensuring that CarTrawler’s enterprise strategy and M&A activity combine to unlock sustainable, long-term value.
  • Reporting directly to the Chief Value Creation Officer, the role supports the shaping and delivery of CarTrawler’s value creation agenda across both organic and inorganic growth. Working closely with Corporate Development, Finance, and broader Cross Functional Leaders within the business, the Head of Value Creation helps translate strategic priorities and acquisition rationales into clear value drivers, aligned plans, and measurable outcomes.
  • The role has particular exposure to M&A value creation, including supporting the transition from deal rationale to post-acquisition value delivery, while maintaining a broader enterprise perspective across strategy, performance, and transformation. Operating in a highly cross-functional environment, the role brings analytical insight, structure, and alignment to CarTrawler’s most critical strategic initiatives, and provides a strong development platform for a high-potential leader.


Main Accountabilities and responsibilities:

  • Enterprise Value Creation
  • Support the CVO in the development and ongoing refinement of CarTrawler’s enterprise strategy and Value Creation Roadmap.
  • Help translate strategic priorities into clearly articulated value drivers, initiatives, and success measures across the organisation.
  • Partner with the Leadership Team and Finance to ensure alignment between strategy, value creation priorities, and functional plans.
  • Provide analytical insight and structured challenge to strategic decision-making at enterprise level.


  • M&A Value Creation & Post-Deal Integration
  • Work closely with the Corporate Development team to support the assessment of acquisition opportunities, with a focus on strategic fit, value drivers, and integration considerations.
  • Contribute to investment discussions by articulating potential value creation levers and key risks from a post-deal perspective.
  • Support the transition from acquisition rationale to post-deal value delivery, helping to shape integration priorities and value realisation plans.
  • Provide oversight and tracking of post-acquisition value creation initiatives, ensuring leadership visibility on progress, risks, and outcomes.
  • Act as a strategic point of connection between CarTrawler and acquired businesses, supporting alignment and focus on priority value drivers.


  • Value Tracking, Governance & Executive Insight
  • Support the design and operation of governance, tracking, and reporting frameworks to monitor delivery of the Value Creation Roadmap.
  • Work with Finance to track progress against key financial, operational, and strategic value drivers.
  • Develop high-quality materials and insights to support executive and board-level discussions.
  • Help ensure focus remains on initiatives with the greatest potential value impact.


  • Strategy & Transformation Support
  • Contribute to enterprise-wide strategic and transformation initiatives, working in partnership with functional leaders.
  • Provide structured problem-solving and analytical support on complex, cross-functional topics.
  • Support the prioritisation and sequencing of strategic initiatives across the organisation.


  • Team & Capability Development
  • Work as part of a small, high-performing Value Creation team, contributing to a culture of high standards, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
  • Help embed value creation and integration best practices across the organisation.
  • Build strong relationships across functions to enable effective collaboration and delivery.


What you’ll bring to the team:

  • Minimum 5 - 7 years of experience in strategy, management consulting, private equity, corporate development, professional services or a related role.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Strategy, Finance, or a related field
  • Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA), Chartered Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) or Certified Management Consultant (CMC) certification an advantage, but not essential.
  • At least 3 years in a senior or lead role supporting executive-level decision-making or strategic delivery.
  • Experience supporting or contributing to enterprise strategy development, including translating strategic priorities into structured initiatives and value drivers.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple stakeholders and priorities simultaneously.
  • Exposure to M&A activity, including:
  • Supporting acquisition assessments and diligence, and/or
  • Contributing to post-acquisition integration and value realisation efforts.
  • Demonstrated success in strategic portfolio management, performance tracking, or transformation delivery, ensuring initiatives translate into measurable business outcomes.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate complex data and insights into clear strategic recommendations.
  • Evidence of execution focus, capable of turning strategy into action, maintaining momentum, and ensuring delivery of high-quality outputs under tight timelines.
  • Experience working cross-functionally with senior leaders, influencing without direct authority, and navigating ambiguity with sound judgment.
job.title: Head of Value Creation

job.job_description:
Tech that takes you places
We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters.

Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together.

The VP Business Development is a senior IC role responsible for owning the end-to-end sales process for high value prospects, maximising the commercial value of signed partnerships while progressing multiple complex deals at pace. You will lead commercial negotiations whilst working cross-functionally to define and scope complex partner commercial and technical proposition requirements, to deliver high-performing partnerships which meet CarTrawler and partner expectations, and which drive significant growth for the CarTrawler business.

This role can be based in Dublin, the UK or New York, with a preference for regular availability to work from our Dublin headquarters.

Your Journey starts here!
What You’ll Do As a VP Business Development

  • Partner Acquisition & Pipeline Management
  • Scaling and growing CarTrawler partner opportunities and building a diversified pipeline of prospects aligned with the company strategy.
  • Developing and building relationships with key decision makers and C-Suite representatives involved in any prospective opportunities.
  • Influencing prospective partners at all levels and selling consultatively.
  • Representing CarTrawler at industry and or other networking events
  • Commercial Strategy
  • Working with Commercial Strategy team to evolve our commercial propositions to meet the requirements of the ever-changing partner set and competitive landscape.
  • Developing a clear understanding of prospective partners’ business and technology requirements and articulating these back to the business.
  • Working on complex deals that require more customization and are not necessarily out-of-the-box solutions and working cross-functionally to scope, agree and deliver against these requirements.
  • Deal Execution & Negotiation
  • Accountable for preparation, submission, and presentation of proposals including tender responses to the highest professional standard ensuring the CarTrawler brand and commercial guidelines are adhered to.
  • Through active and effective pipeline management and commercial execution, ensuring commercial targets are achieved on an ongoing basis.
  • Closing deals that maximise contribution for CarTrawler through working within the commercial framework(s) to deliver and exceed targets.
  • Cross-Functional & Team Leadership
  • Playing an active role in supporting key internal stakeholders (e.g. Product, Commercial and Technical Teams) with a thorough understanding of market demands and partner requirements.
  • Collaborating with other key departments (e.g. Go-Live, Product & Technology, Legal, Finance, Partner Management) throughout the sales process, particularly during competitive tenders and responding to RFPs.
  • Support other team members in their development through knowledge sharing and mentoring.
  • Governance & Reporting
  • Managing and progressing all commercial opportunities through Salesforce ensuring completeness & accuracy of relevant engagements and providing comprehensive updates for senior management and CarTrawler Leadership Team.




What You’ll Bring To The Team

  • 10+ years in senior commercial roles within travel, mobility, airline, or related sectors, with direct ownership of enterprise-level deal cycles
  • High attention to detail with ability to rapidly develop subject- matter expertise and become an authority on our business
  • Strong influencing and relationship management skills with the ability to engage and build relationships with all stakeholders including c-suite inside and outside of CarTrawler
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, with curiosity to resolve problems, identify emerging business trends, be creative, and find innovative solutions
  • Constructively challenge both internal and external stakeholders whilst still maintaining excellent relationships
  • Experienced negotiator with extensive business development experience and the ability to influence
  • Excellent commercial acumen with direct experience of preparing, submitting, and winning complex bids and/or tender processes
  • Highly professional, with strong personal and professional integrity, who can successfully represent CarTrawler both internally & externally
  • Excellent knowledge of the marketplace and overall travel industry
  • Technology savvy with ability to communicate technical product features effectively to both commercial and technical audiences
  • Ability to work independently, manage multiple concurrent projects and initiatives in a cross-functional environment
  • High proficiency in Word, Excel and PowerPoint




Go on, give it a go!

We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected.

At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here.

Perks for the journey


  • Competitive pay and benefits, including a performance-based annual bonus, fully subsidised VHI health insurance and pension matching up to 4%
  • Generous time off and flexible working, including 25 days’ annual leave, extra company days, monthly Friday Unplugged afternoons and the option to work abroad for up to 20 days a year
  • Comprehensive wellbeing support, including access to health and mental health programmes (HeadsUp and EAP) and paid leave for marriage, volunteering and personal wellbeing including menstrual, menopause, and fertility leave
  • Growth and recognition culture with development opportunities through training, coaching and study support plus programmes that celebrate individual and team achievements
  • Everyday perks that make a difference - subsidised canteen (KC Peaches), on-site car parking, Bike to Work and TaxSaver schemes plus an active Sports & Social Club




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

job.job_description:
Tech that takes you places
We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together.

We are looking for an experienced Content Designer to join Product & Design and shape how CarTrawler communicates within our products, with a particular focus on localised, multi-language experiences.

Sitting at the intersection of product content and localisation, you will:

Design and refine content for key product journeys (for example, search, booking and rental conditions).

Ensure copy is clear, consistent and translation-ready, so it can be localised accurately and efficiently.

Help operationalise our content strategy and support delivery of our product and customer experience goals.

You will work closely with Product, Design, Engineering and Commercial/Partner teams, acting as a go-to expert for product copy and localisation and helping improve the standards and workflows that keep it consistent across markets and partners.

Your Journey starts here!
What You’ll Do As a Content Designer

  • Core Content Design Responsibilities
  • Design and write clear, concise, user-centred product content for key journeys and UI surfaces.
  • Create content that is structured and translation-ready, and partner with Translations to ensure it works across priority languages.
  • Maintain and apply the product style guide, terminology glossary and content patterns.
  • Audit and improve existing content across our systems, using data, research and experiments to refine and optimise copy.
  • Localisation & Translation Operations
  • Shape and improve how product teams brief localisation, including templates, checklists and context expectations.
  • Prepare and structure source text, clarify variables/placeholders and add contextual notes before content goes to translation.
  • Support ongoing content management across our product content systems (for example, structure, naming and clean-up of outdated content).
  • Partner with translation providers, providing structured context and strengthening LQA feedback loops into style guide and glossary updates.
  • Process, Systems & Enablement
  • Embed content into upstream product, partner and commercial initiatives, including partner-facing messaging and merchandising strategies.
  • Help teams plan and communicate content changes, especially when they impact multiple languages or partners.
  • Act as a go-to content and localisation partner for product teams, providing light coaching on writing clear, translation-ready copy.
  • Contribute to guardrails and workflows for AI-assisted copywriting tools powered by our style guide and glossary.




What You’ll Bring To The Team

  • Third-level qualification in Communications, English, Journalism, Marketing, UX/HCI, Digital Media or a related discipline, or equivalent relevant industry experience.
  • A portfolio of relevant content design / UX writing work is required (for example, product flows, UI copy, content patterns, before/after examples and experiments or A/B tests you’ve contributed to).
  • 3–5+ years of experience in content design, UX writing, content strategy, copywriting or a related role, preferably within a digital product environment; experience in the travel or travel technology industry is a plus.
  • Proven ability to design and write clear, concise and user-centred product content, demonstrated via a strong portfolio.
  • Experience working in cross-functional product teams alongside designers, product managers and engineers, and understanding of UX principles and user journeys.
  • Experience collaborating with localisation/translation teams and using TMS/CMS tools, including providing clear context for translators.
  • Comfortable using data and research (quantitative and qualitative) to inform content decisions and prioritisation.
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with attention to detail and a commitment to delivering high-quality work.
  • Ability to adapt to changing priorities and work in a fast-paced, iterative environment.




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
job.title: Content Designer

job.company_description:
Lufthansa is one of the world’s leading airlines, connecting passengers to over 200 destinations across 74 countries from our hubs in Frankfurt and Munich.
As an industry pioneer, we are committed to shaping the future of sustainable aviation, investing in next-generation aircraft, cutting-edge technology, and digital solutions. Our goal is to drive excellence: not just in the sky, but across every touchpoint of the travel experience.
With Lufthansa Allegris, our new long-haul experience, we are setting a new standard in premium travel, introducing a completely redesigned First, Business, Premium Economy, and Economy Class for ultimate comfort and choice. We continue to enhance the travel experience with state-of-the-art lounges, award-winning service, and onboard broadband Wi-Fi (FlyNet) across our entire long-haul fleet. Follow us for updates on how we're shaping the future of aviation.
For more information, reservations or customer service, visit lufthansa.com or follow us on Facebook, X and Instagram. We look forward to welcoming you onboard.
This LinkedIn page is maintained by Deutsche Lufthansa AG. We will not answer specific Customer Service queries here on LinkedIn. If you have an immediate concern or need to contact us please visit http://www.lufthansa.com/us/en/imprint
job.job_description:
Do you want to help shape Lufthansa Group’s flight operations and optimize affordable ancillary services across multiple airlines? Do you have initial experience in sales, product management, or marketing and want to expand it internationally? Then join our team and help us improve the customer journey!

Tasks

Dive into the world of ancillary services at Lufthansa Group – from upgrades to onboard Wi-Fi. We manage all revenue budgets and optimize profitability, sales, and customer value using the marketing mix (4Ps). You’ll take on diverse tasks, especially including:

  • Organizing and facilitating workshops with internal and external colleagues
  • Conducting market and data analyses, e.g., creating benchmarks
  • Supporting the preparation of argumentation and decision documents for management
  • Implementing creative ideas into practice

Benefits

  • Flexible working hours
  • Free parking
  • Special deals with selected partners
  • Cafeteria
  • Subsidized public transport

Requirements

At the time of the internship, you should be at least in your third semester of study and ideally meet the following requirements:

  • Enrollment throughout the entire duration of the internship (a current enrollment certificate and a written proof of mandatory internship must be included with the application) or internship during a gap year between Bachelor’s and Master’s degree
  • Ongoing studies in a relevant field (e.g., Economics, Marketing, Sales, Communication, or a comparable degree program)
  • Analytical skills and enjoyment of working with numbers and research
  • Strong communication skills and creative ideas
  • Independent and proactive working style
  • Very good knowledge of MS Office (especially PowerPoint & Teams)
  • Fluent German and English skills

Further information can also be found in our FAQs.
job.title: Internship Ancillary Category Management Start date: by arrangement. Duration: 6 months

job.job_description:
Du willst den Flugbetrieb der Lufthansa Gruppe mitgestalten und bezahlbare Zusatzservices über mehrere Airlines hinweg optimieren? Du bringst erste Erfahrungen in Sales, Produktmanagement oder Marketing mit und willst diese international ausbauen? Dann werde Teil unseres Teams und verbessere mit uns die Customer Journey!

Aufgaben

Tauche ein in die Welt der Zusatzservices der Lufthansa Group – von Upgrades bis WLAN an Bord. Wir steuern alle Umsatzbudgets und optimieren entlang des Marketing-Mix (4Ps) Rentabilität, Umsatz und Kundenwert. Dabei erwarten dich vielfältige Aufgaben, insbesondere:

  • Organisation und Begleitung von Workshops mit internen und externen Kolleg:innen
  • Durchführung von Markt- und Datenanalysen, z. B. Benchmark-Erstellung
  • Unterstützung bei der Erstellung von Argumentations- und Entscheidungsvorlagen für das Management
  • Umsetzung kreativer Impulse in die Praxis

Benefits

  • Zuschuss ÖPNV
  • Kantine
  • Discounts bei ausgewählten Partnern
  • Kostenloses Parken
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten

Voraussetzungen

Zum Zeitpunkt des Praktikums solltest Du mindestens im 3. Semester Deines Studiums sein und idealerweise folgende Voraussetzungen mitbringen:

  • Immatrikulation über den gesamten Zeitraum des Praktikums (aktuelle Immatrikulationsbescheinigung und ein schriftlicher Pflichtpraktikumsnachweis sind der Bewerbung beizufügen) oder Praktikum im Gap Year zwischen Bachelor und Master
  • Laufendes Studium in einem relevanten Fachgebiet (z. B. Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Marketing, Sales, Kommunikation oder vergleichbarer Studiengang)
  • Analytische Fähigkeiten und Freude an der Arbeit mit Zahlen und Research
  • Ausgeprägte Kommunikationsfähigkeit und kreative Ideen
  • Selbstständige und proaktive Arbeitsweise
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse in MS Office (insbesondere PowerPoint & Teams)
  • Fließende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse

Weitere Informationen auch in unseren FAQs.

Über uns

Die Lufthansa Group ist ein weltweit operierender Luftverkehrskonzern. In ihrem Heimatmarkt Europa nimmt sie eine führende Rolle ein. Mit ca. 100.000 Mitarbeiter:innen erzielte die Lufthansa Group im Geschäftsjahr 2024 einen Umsatz von 37.581 Mio. EUR. Die Deutsche Lufthansa AG ist in den Geschäftsfeldern Network Airlines, Point-to-Point Airlines, dem Bereich Aviation Services mit den Geschäftsfeldern Logistik, Technik sowie den weiteren Gesellschaften und Konzernfunktionen organisiert.
job.title: Praktikum im Bereich Ancillary Category Management Praktikumstart: nach Absprache, Dauer: 6 Monate

job.company_description:
Lufthansa is one of the world’s leading airlines, connecting passengers to over 200 destinations across 74 countries from our hubs in Frankfurt and Munich.
As an industry pioneer, we are committed to shaping the future of sustainable aviation, investing in next-generation aircraft, cutting-edge technology, and digital solutions. Our goal is to drive excellence: not just in the sky, but across every touchpoint of the travel experience.
With Lufthansa Allegris, our new long-haul experience, we are setting a new standard in premium travel, introducing a completely redesigned First, Business, Premium Economy, and Economy Class for ultimate comfort and choice. We continue to enhance the travel experience with state-of-the-art lounges, award-winning service, and onboard broadband Wi-Fi (FlyNet) across our entire long-haul fleet. Follow us for updates on how we're shaping the future of aviation.
For more information, reservations or customer service, visit lufthansa.com or follow us on Facebook, X and Instagram. We look forward to welcoming you onboard.

job.job_description:
Are you open-minded and eager to learn? You want to be part of a fun team and get insights in the Lufthansa Group Brand Management department? Are you characterized by strong communication skills, a passion for brand-related topics and exceptional organizational talent?

That's great! Then we invite you to join our team as an intern.

Tasks

We manage the Lufthansa Group's branding, both internally and externally. Our team collaborates closely with brand strategists, brand design team and various agencies to ensure cohesive and effective messaging.

You want to get working experience in an international team? Then we are looking for you!

As an intern you will drive with us the following tasks:

  • Get insights and assist with brand agency support
  • Support daily operations of the Lufthansa Group Brand Hub
  • Take over smaller brand management projects
  • Prepare strategic management meetings and support brand management operations
  • Support cross-functional communication between different teams

Benefits

  • Free parking
  • Employee events
  • Cafeteria
  • Special deals with selected partners
  • Flexible working hours
  • Subsidized public transport

Requirements

You are a team player with a high level of initiative who feels comfortable in a dynamic and international environment with a wide range of tasks. In addition, you bring the following requirements with you:

  • At the time of the internship at least in 3rd semester of a degree in the fields of marketing, tourism, design, brand or media management, business administration, communication or similar
  • Enrolled for the entire period of the internship (current certificate of enrollment and a written proof of mandatory internship must be enclosed with the application) or the internship between the Bachelor's and Master's degree program (Gap Year)
  • High degree of commitment and independence
  • Very good knowledge of German and English, both spoken and written
  • Analytical and conceptual thinking as well as flexibility and quick comprehension skills

Further information can also be found in our FAQs.
job.title: Internship Lufthansa Group Brand Management Start of the internship: April 2026, planned for 6 months

job.job_description:
Communication and creativity are among your strengths? Do you have a talent for organization and enjoy coordinating events? Would you like to take on responsibility and gain an insight into the marketing and sales processes of an international company?

Cool, then an internship in B2B marketing is your chance to get a taste of your dream job.

Tasks

We are responsible for all B2B marketing measures in Germany - with a focus on events and digital marketing - and work closely with the A-CH-BE markets. No two days are the same for us.

We look forward to your help in a virtual team:

  • You create content for advertising with our cooperation partners
  • You will be part of the team in the organization, preparation and follow-up of events, have your own areas of responsibility and are jointly responsible for the success and participation in the events
  • The evaluation of marketing success is part of this
  • You will actively participate in creative brainstorming processes
  • You will get to know and work with various marketing tools
  • You will take on tasks and smaller projects independently and deal with new products and sustainability
  • You contribute to our day-to-day business and administrative tasks
  • You will provide support in all areas and gain a comprehensive overview of what we do

Benefits

  • Special deals with selected partners
  • Subsidized public transport
  • Flexible working hours
  • Employee events
  • Free parking
  • Cafeteria

Requirements

Your recipe for success:

  • At the time of the internship, you are at least in the 3rd semester of a degree course
  • Ideally, you have gained initial experience in the working world through internships or similar in the marketing or event sector
  • Enrollment for the entire period of the internship (current certificate of enrollment and written proof of mandatory internship must be enclosed with the application) or internship between the Bachelor's and Master's degree program (Gap Year)
  • MS Office, especially MS Excel and MS PowerPoint skills
  • Your fluency in writing texts as well as your flexibility and quick comprehension skills characterize you
  • Knowledge of general business administration and marketing
  • Strong customer orientation
  • Analytical and conceptual thinking skills
  • Ability to work independently, high motivation and initiative
  • Solution and team-oriented
  • Very good German and good English skills

Further information can also be found in our FAQs.
job.title: Internship Marketing Internship start: August 2026, duration: 6 months.

job.job_description:
Are you passionate about people and leadership? Would you like to gain insights into the exciting world of Lufthansa Airlines and make a real contribution through your commitment during your internship? Then the Leadership Development team is the right place for you!

Tasks

Our team is responsible for management development at Lufthansa Airlines with a focus on managers in the operational areas.

In addition to initiatives to strengthen the feedback culture and the creation of tailored development programs for managers, our services also include the implementation of the annual employee survey and many other exciting projects.

  • Project work in the role of PMO, i.e. support with project planning, operational management, follow-up and tracking
  • Collaboration on the conceptual design and further development of management formats and concepts
  • Organization, preparation and evaluation of workshops and training courses as well as support in their implementation
  • Management of offer platforms (e.g. SharePoint)
  • Event planning and administration, communication and internal marketing activities

Requirements

You are open to a wide range of tasks from concept to administration, share our "we get things moving!" mindset and receive structured training and support. If we have capacity, we offer insights into other exciting areas.

  • At the time of the internship, you must be at least in the 3rd semester of a degree course in human resources, psychology, business administration, economics/social sciences or similar
  • Enrolled for the entire duration of the internship (current certificate of enrollment and written proof of mandatory internship must be enclosed with the application) or the internship between the Bachelor's and Master's degree program (gap year)
  • Ideally initial experience in HR and/or project management
  • Organizational talent and strengths in reliably juggling between tasks

Further information can also be found in our FAQs.
job.title: Internship Leadership Development Internship start: April 2026, duration: 6 months

job.job_description:
Kommunikation und Kreativität gehört zu deinen Stärken? Du bist ein Organisationstalent und hast mega Spaß am Koordinieren von Events? Du möchtest Verantwortung übernehmen und einen Einblick in die Abläufe des Marketings und Vertriebs eines international operierenden Konzerns bekommen?

Cool, dann ist ein Praktikum im Bereich B2B Marketing deine Chance, mal in deinen Traumjob reinzuschnuppern.

Aufgaben

Wir verantworten alle B2B Marketingmaßnahmen in Deutschland – mit Fokus auf Events und Digital Marketing – und arbeiten eng mit den Märkten A-CH-BE zusammen. Bei uns ist kein Tag wie der Andere.

Wir freuen uns sehr auf deine Mithilfe in einem virtuellen Team:

  • Du erstellst Content für Werbung mit unseren Kooperationspartnern
  • Bei der Organisation, Vor- und Nachbereitung von Events bist du im Team dabei, hast eigene Aufgabenbereiche und bist mitverantwortlich für den Erfolg und bei den Veranstaltungen dabei
  • Die Auswertung des Marketingerfolgs gehört dazu
  • Bei kreativen Ideenfindungsprozessen nimmst du aktiv teil
  • Du lernst unterschiedliche Marketingtools kennen und arbeitest damit
  • Du übernimmst eigenständig Aufgaben und kleinere Projekte, beschäftigst dich mit neuen Produkten und Sustainability
  • In unserem Tagesgeschäft und bei administrativen Aufgaben bringst du dich ein
  • In allen Bereichen unterstützt du und bekommst einen umfassenden Überblick über das, was wir tun

Benefits

  • Discounts bei ausgewählten Partnern
  • Mitarbeiterevents
  • Kostenloses Parken
  • Kantine
  • Zuschuss ÖPNV
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten

Voraussetzungen

Dein Erfolgsrezept:

  • Zum Zeitpunkt des Praktikums bist du mindestens im 3. Semester eines Studiums
  • Idealerweise konntest du durch Praktika o.Ä. im Marketing oder Event-Bereich erste Erfahrung in der Arbeitswelt sammeln
  • Immatrikulation über den gesamten Zeitraum des Praktikums (aktuelle Immatrikulationsbescheinigung und ein schriftlicher Pflichtpraktikumsnachweis müssen der Bewerbung beigefügt werden) oder Praktikum zwischen dem Bachelor- und Masterstudium (Gap Year)
  • MS-Office, insbesondere MS Excel und MS PowerPoint Kenntnisse
  • Deine Sprachgewandtheit beim Verfassen von Texten sowie deine Flexibilität und schnelle Auffassungsgabe zeichnen dich aus
  • Kenntnisse in allgemeiner BWL sowie im Marketing
  • Ausgeprägte Kundenorientierung
  • Analytisches und konzeptionelles Denkvermögen
  • Selbstständiges Arbeiten, hohe Motivation und Eigeninitiative
  • Lösungs- und teamorientiert
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse

Weitere Informationen auch in unseren FAQs.

Über uns

Die Lufthansa Group ist ein weltweit operierender Luftverkehrskonzern. In ihrem Heimatmarkt Europa nimmt sie eine führende Rolle ein. Mit ca. 100.000 Mitarbeiter:innen erzielte die Lufthansa Group im Geschäftsjahr 2024 einen Umsatz von 37.581 Mio. EUR. Die Deutsche Lufthansa AG ist in den Geschäftsfeldern Network Airlines, Point-to-Point Airlines, dem Bereich Aviation Services mit den Geschäftsfeldern Logistik, Technik sowie den weiteren Gesellschaften und Konzernfunktionen organisiert.

job.job_description:
Are you curious, dynamic, and eager for new challenges in a global company surrounded by a great team? With your innovative ideas and your drive, you can actively participate in shaping the price products of LH Airlines & help optimize our revenue result. If working with colleagues from other hub locations excites you, then take off with us!

Tasks

Our Offer Pricing teams shape LHG Airlines’ commercial offer portfolio and corresponding pricing to optimize revenues. During this internship you will get to know the exciting field of revenue management and gain diverse insights into the commercial heart of an airline.

  • Support in maximizing revenues, taking into account willingness to pay, opportunity and displacement costs
  • Support in development and definition of price and offer strategies as well as price portfolios for certain customer groups, such as business or private travelers, corporate customers, tour operators, or individual distribution channels
  • Continuously monitor and analyse market and revenue trends, derive insights and present key findings/ recommendations for strategic actions
  • Conduct benchmark analysis of relevant market players to ensure optimal positioning of price offer
  • Monitor and assess planned pricing activities and coordinate success control (e.g. marketing campaigns)
  • Closely cooperate and exchange with interfaces within Lufthansa Group Revenue Management

Benefits

  • Employee events
  • Flexible working hours
  • Subsidized public transport
  • Free parking
  • Cafeteria

Requirements

At the time of the internship you are required to be at least in your 3rd semester in economics or comparable field of study like Tourism Management, Aviation Management, etc.

  • Enrolled for the entire period of the internship (current certificate of enrollment and a proof of mandatory internship must be enclosed with the application) or the internship between the Bachelor's and Master's degree program (Gap Year)
  • Fluent in written and spoken English; German knowledge would be an asset
  • Very good knowledge of Microsoft Office products, especially Excel and Power Point; other statistical software for analysis of data would be a plus
  • Interest in working in an analytical, number & data driven context
  • High analytical skills, technical understanding of IT systems
  • High degree of flexibility, organizational talent, entrepreneurial and conceptual thinking, precise presentation of complex issues
  • Excellent presentation skills

Further information can also be found in our FAQs.
job.title: Internship Revenue Management Pricing Internship start: April 2026, duration: 6 months

job.job_description:
Do you want to help shape Lufthansa Group’s flight operations and optimize affordable ancillary services across multiple airlines? Do you have initial experience in sales, product management, or marketing and want to expand it internationally? Then join our team and help us improve the customer journey!

Tasks

Dive into the world of ancillary services at Lufthansa Group – from upgrades to onboard Wi-Fi. We manage all revenue budgets and optimize profitability, sales, and customer value using the marketing mix (4Ps). You’ll take on diverse tasks, especially including:

  • Organizing and facilitating workshops with internal and external colleagues
  • Conducting market and data analyses, e.g., creating benchmarks
  • Supporting the preparation of argumentation and decision documents for management
  • Implementing creative ideas into practice

Benefits

  • Flexible working hours
  • Free parking
  • Special deals with selected partners
  • Cafeteria
  • Subsidized public transport

Requirements

At the time of the internship, you should be at least in your third semester of study and ideally meet the following requirements:

  • Enrollment throughout the entire duration of the internship (a current enrollment certificate and a written proof of mandatory internship must be included with the application) or internship during a gap year between Bachelor’s and Master’s degree
  • Ongoing studies in a relevant field (e.g., Economics, Marketing, Sales, Communication, or a comparable degree program)
  • Analytical skills and enjoyment of working with numbers and research
  • Strong communication skills and creative ideas
  • Independent and proactive working style
  • Very good knowledge of MS Office (especially PowerPoint & Teams)
  • Fluent German and English skills

Further information can also be found in our FAQs.
job.title: Internship Ancillary Category Management Start date: by arrangement. Duration: 6 months

Die Deutsche Lufthansa AG ist in den Geschäftsfeldern Network Airlines, Point-to-Point Airlines, dem Bereich Aviation Services mit den Geschäftsfeldern Logistik, Technik sowie den weiteren Gesellschaften und Konzernfunktionen organisiert.
job.title: Praktikum im Bereich Marketing

job.company_description: dnata is a leading global air and travel services provider. Established in 1959, the company offers quality and safe ground handling, cargo, travel, catering and retail services in 34 countries across six continents.
job.job_description:
Who are we?

Dnata is one of the world’s largest air and travel services providers. We provide a comprehensive range of inflight catering, onboard retail, supply chain and logistics services at all major and regional airports across the UK.Dnata Catering provide expertly crafted inflight catering services, delivering fresh and high-quality meals at major and regional airports across the UK.As the leading Halal caterer in the UK, we also provide tailored experiences to meet each customer’s unique needs.We’re a leader in VIP catering, offering bespoke inflight to private and charter clients.

The Role

The dnata global retail business is rapidly expanding, building off successful operations in the UK, Europe, Middle East and Australia. As the business expands into new market territories with new clients, the need has arisen to recruit a Retail Category Management & Merchandising lead to support this expansion to provide a range of services aligned to business readiness to provide a turnkey approach for category management and merchandising to meet client needs long term. The role is designed to ensure for each new business implementation, we have clear leadership for the entire category strategy across the full range of products right through to the actual acquisition of goods within those categories. In addition, the role will ensure that the business will be using ready-made processes, templates and applications upon deployment in a new territory or for a new start-up business without having to invest the time and resources required to develop their own from scratch. The role will also play a pivotal role in the development of new products and services pertinent to the retail business proposition.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the Sourcing, Ranging and Procurement of applicable product ranges for new business implementations
  • Develop clear category and range strategies appropriate for the territory and market for the new businesses being implemented
  • Margin optimisation in the sourcing process – optimising price and investments to drive the best trading value for the launch assortments
  • Develop trading and supply chain forecasts and intake plans - developing appropriate opening stock levels, ordering new products, and managing warehousing requirements to avoid shortages or overstocking upon transition and launch
  • Create merchandising plans to align stock loading levels on the aircraft to onboard service routines and trading forecasts
  • Prepare all master data for range initiation to be input in the applicable retail or ERP systems
  • Assessments of existing Ranging, Category Management and Merchandising processes with identification of gaps and actionable improvement points
  • Create a core set of “white label” processes, templates and applications applicable to Retail Sourcing, Category Management and Merchandising
  • Supporting the global Sales team with new business implementations – providing guidance for prospective product offerings and start-up processes
  • Developing turnkey merchandising and bar design processes, that align with the new business start-up IT solution
  • Support the recruitment of local teams for new start-up airline customers – using the turnkey approach to embed processes and templates into the new teams’ ways of working
  • Engage the suppliers into the strategies and white-label approach and align them culturally to the objectives being driven

Required Skills

  • Evidence of previous retail buying & category management - a minimum of 5 years
  • Experience of working within an aligned buying and merchandising environment
  • Strong leadership and management skills- Good interpersonal, communication, presentational and teamwork
  • Able to work under pressure – plan, organise and delegate
  • Attention to detail and accuracy
  • High level analytical and numerical skills
  • Enthusiastic, self-motived, proactive and persuasive

What'sin it for you?

  • An opportunity to be part of the Emirates global family.
  • Free breakfast, lunch and dinner on site
  • Free tea and coffee
  • Simply health Plan
  • Refer a Friend Scheme
  • Discounted gym membership at over 2000 gyms nationwide
  • Cycle to Work Scheme
  • Opportunity to enhance your learning through our inhouse apprenticeship programme

If you're invited to interview, please let us know if there are any adjustments we can make to ensure you're cleared for take-off and able to perform at your best. We’re committed to making the recruitment journey as smooth and inclusive as possible.
job.title: Retail Category Manager

job.company_description: dnata is a leading global air and travel services provider. Established in 1959, the company offers quality and safe ground handling, cargo, travel, catering and retail services in 34 countries across six continents.
job.job_description:
We’re En Route. We’re a welcoming bunch, with a down-to-earth outlook that helps build lasting relationships with customers and colleagues alike.

As global specialists in passenger solutions, supply chain services and sourcing for airlines and airline caterers, we operate globally as part of dnata, one of the world’s largest combined air services providers.

En Route’s expertise is one of the many reasons why we’ve enjoyed more than 20 years as a global food solutions partner, with offices in the UK, USA, Australia and UAE.

Are you ready to become part of our story?

Created by En Route colleagues, our values sit at the very heart of our business.

  • Birthday day off
  • 25 days off a year
  • Company bonus scheme
  • Hybrid Working (3 days in office)
  • Medical Insurance
  • Referral Scheme
  • Social Events
  • Flexi Time

The Food Development Manager is responsible for leading the innovation, development and launch of high-quality food and beverage products. Providing expertise across En Route’s core categories, ensuring all project tasks are completed within agreed timescales and to the required standards, taking project ownership where applicable.

Working closely with the Head of Category to shape the category plans and their tactical execution. In addition to having product responsibility this role will also support strategic projects as required.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the end-to-end development of new food concepts and product ranges
  • Conduct tastings, presentations and menu showcases for customers and internal stakeholders
  • Work closely with customers to understand their brand positioning, route profiles and passenger expectations and translate this into detailed product briefs for suppliers
  • Understand the wants and needs of both our customers and the end consumer
  • Build strong relationships with customers, establishing self as subject matter expert
  • Partner with suppliers and technical colleagues on first trials, first production and oversee implementation of new products ensuring compliance with food safety standards
  • Monitor culinary trends, competitor activity and passenger feedback to drive continuous improvement and innovation
  • Ensure that the product portfolio is refreshed, reviewed and updated in line with Category plan to ensure it is relevant and contemporary
  • Avoid unnecessary spread in range eliminating obsolescence and optimising inventory
  • Act as an En Route ambassador at PR and Marketing events as and when required
  • Collaborate with a broad range of stakeholders to ensure Innovation, ingredient quality, cost effectiveness and sustainability where appropriate
  • Working with Technical to ensure product safety and adherence to guidelines
  • Work with supply chain to ensure effective mobilisation
  • Support commercial account managers during client pitches and tenders
  • Ensure any direct reports have clear performance goals that are regularly reviewed throughout the year
  • Deputise for Head of Category as required
  • Travel overseas as required for customer and supplier meetings
  • To undertake any other duties, which may from time to time be assigned.
  • Experience in food product development or manufacturing, ideally within airline catering or food service.
  • Experience managing client‑facing projects and working effectively with cross‑functional teams.
  • Strong project management capability with a hands‑on, flexible approach to leading work at pace.
  • Excellent culinary awareness, ideally across multiple product categories.
  • A strong passion for food, product innovation, and understanding emerging trends.
  • Commercial acumen with the ability to balance creativity and practicality.
  • Excellent communication skills and strong stakeholder engagement.
  • Ability to build and maintain strong business relationships.
  • A customer‑focused mindset and a clear sense of accountability- both personally and in enabling others.
  • Confidence in making data‑driven decisions and collaborating effectively across internal and external partners.
  • Experience in food manufacturing or food retail.
  • Experience using a retail category approach to product development.
  • Chef background with expertise across multiple categories.
  • Experience working in a matrixed, international organisation.
  • People management experience.
  • Negotiation skills.
  • Advanced understanding of food manufacturing processes.
  • Confident user of Microsoft Office applications (PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook).
  • Strong presentation skills.
  • Competence in specification design and detailed product costing.
  • Ability to interpret and analyse market trends.

If you’re interested in applying for this role, please send a copy of your most recent CV to EnRouteRecruitment@en-route.com

Please no agencies.

We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable, and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences, and we are dedicated to ensuring a fair and accessible recruitment process for all candidates. If you require any adjustments or support during the application or interview stages, please let us know – we’re here to help!

Regrettably, due to the high volume of applications we receive, we are unable to respond individually to every candidate. If you have not heard from us within 10 working days of submitting your application, unfortunately you have not been successful on this occasion.

We take your privacy seriously. Any personal data you share with us during the application process will be handled in accordance with applicable data protection laws and used solely for recruitment purposes. Your information will be stored securely and retained only for as long as necessary.
job.title: Food Development Manager

job.company_description: dnata is a leading global air and travel services provider. Established in 1959, the company offers quality and safe ground handling, cargo, travel, catering and retail services in 34 countries across six continents.
job.job_description:
At Travelbag, we make travel a piece of cake. Our knowledgeable experts live and breathe holidays, so they’re always on hand with tips and advice, to help our customers discover somewhere new. Whether they want a relaxing week away or a month-long adventure, we’ve got it covered. We’re owned by dnata Travel, part of the Emirates Group.

We are now recruiting for experienced Travel Consultants to join our team of experts at our store in Knutsford.

To be considered, you need to have experience of selling long haul destinations and tailor-made holidays. You need to have exceptional customer service skills, be driven by your passion for travel and motivated to meet and exceed your targets. GDS experience would also be an advantage.

If you have experience of working in a target driven environment as well as experience of travelling long haul, we'd be happy to hear from you too.

We have a lucrative uncapped commission scheme with average commission of £24K on top of your base salary, however our top performers average £55K in commission!

Depending on experience in your 1st Year you can expect a minimum OTE of £35K rising to £39K in Year 2.

Thanks to Travelbag’s regular marketing activity, you’ll always have plenty of customer enquiries to follow up. And you’ll build fantastic relationships with your customers, whether face to face or over the phone, so they come back to you time and time again. Our impressive number of repeat customers is something we’re incredibly proud of.

When you join Travelbag, you’ll have an initial 4-week interactive training period with our inhouse training team. You’ll then join our ‘academy’ with dedicated support alongside support and coaching from your manager.

You will have lots of opportunities to enhance your product and destination knowledge – including weekly product training from our destination team and supplier hosted events where you can meet others in the industry as well as learn about a product or destination – and there are usually prizes up for grabs!

Our Travel Consultants work 37.5 hours per week 5 days over 7, 9am to 6:00pm. In peak periods we can be open 9am to 9pm to meet our customer demands.

We have some fantastic benefits, making us a great place to work:

  • Uncapped commission scheme.
  • Fam Trips and Incentives - you will visit a key Travelbag location in your first year, in partnership with our suppliers we had 62 spaces on Fam Trips last year!
  • Travel discounts – we have a dedicated Staff Travel Team to help you plan your next adventure.
  • 25 days annual leave, increasing to 28 days after 5 full years of service (+ bank holidays).
  • Additional annual leave purchase scheme – so you can pursue your love of travel.
  • Company pension scheme.
  • Life assurance.
  • Employee Assistance programme.
  • Mental Health First Aiders
  • Belong programme - Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that play a crucial role in implementing our DEI strategy
  • Ongoing development – we have an in-house People Development Team.
  • Industry/social events – including supplier events.
  • Annual conference where we bring the whole Travelbag Team together.
  • Cycle to work scheme.
  • Long service awards.
  • Reward & recognition programme
  • Volunteer days – one day’s paid leave each year to volunteer with a registered charity.
  • dnata4good – supporting the local communities we work, live and thrive in.
  • Refer a friend scheme.
  • Free eye care tests.
  • Free Taste Card membership– offering discounts on dining out, movies, attractions, takeaways and online shopping. Free tea and coffee in all our shops
job.title: Travel Consultant

job.job_description:
We’re En Route. We’re a welcoming bunch, with a down-to-earth outlook that helps build lasting relationships with customers and colleagues alike.

As global specialists in passenger solutions, supply chain services and sourcing for airlines and airline caterers, we operate globally as part of dnata, one of the world’s largest combined air services providers.

En Route’s expertise is one of the many reasons why we’ve enjoyed more than 20 years as a global food solutions partner, with offices in the UK, USA, Australia and UAE.

Are you ready to become part of our story?

Created by En Route colleagues, our values sit at the very heart of our business.

  • Birthday day off
  • 25 days off a year
  • Company bonus scheme
  • Hybrid Working (3 days in office)
  • Medical Insurance
  • Referral Scheme
  • Social Events
  • Flexi Time

This is an exciting opportunity to lead logistics coordination for a major customer account, primarily managing high-volume container shipments from GB to the Middle East, as well as movements to the US, Australia, and Asia. The role focuses on freight forwarding activities, predominantly sea freight with some air freight, ensuring timely, cost-effective delivery and exceptional service.

You will act as the key liaison between suppliers, carriers, and internal teams, managing international transport flows and documentation while maintaining operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

  • Logistics Coordination
  • Manage international shipments (sea and air) with freight forwarders and carriers.
  • Book shipments, schedule deliveries, and prepare all required shipping documents (invoices, packing lists, customs paperwork).
  • Monitor container movements and proactively resolve delays or issues.
  • Track and report logistics performance metrics.
  • Communication and Collaboration
  • Act as the main point of contact for freight forwarders and carriers.
  • Provide clear updates on shipment status and transport issues to internal teams and customers.
  • Collaborate with purchasing, sales, and warehouse teams to ensure smooth operations.
  • Data Analysis and Reporting
  • Generate reports on shipment performance and cost efficiency.
  • Maintain accurate records of all logistics transactions.
  • Inventory Management
  • Support stock accuracy through remote audits and cycle counts.
  • Assist with inventory control procedures as required.
  • Background in freight forwarding or international logistics, with hands-on experience in sea freight and some air freight.
  • Strong understanding of Incoterms, transport flows, and international shipping processes.
  • Operational, hands-on mindset with ownership of shipments and problem-solving skills.
  • Comfortable working in fast-paced, deadline-driven environments.
  • Excellent communication and organisational skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel and familiar with ERP systems (Business Central/Navision/SAP preferred but not essential).

Please note this is a Hybrid role based out of Windsor, Berkshire.

If you’re interested in applying for this role, please send a copy of your most recent CV to EnRouteRecruitment@en-route.com

Please no agencies.

We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable, and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences, and we are dedicated to ensuring a fair and accessible recruitment process for all candidates. If you require any adjustments or support during the application or interview stages, please let us know – we’re here to help!

Regrettably, due to the high volume of applications we receive, we are unable to respond individually to every candidate. If you have not heard from us within 10 working days of submitting your application, unfortunately you have not been successful on this occasion.

We take your privacy seriously. Any personal data you share with us during the application process will be handled in accordance with applicable data protection laws and used solely for recruitment purposes. Your information will be stored securely and retained only for as long as necessary.
job.title: Logistics Coordinator – Freight Forwarding (Sea & Air)

job.company_description:
Based in Dubai, the Emirates Group employs over 120,000 staff from more than 160 nationalities. The Emirates Group’s extensive and diverse international portfolio includes the world’s largest international airline, Emirates, and one of the largest combined air services provider in the world, dnata. Within the Group there are a diverse range of businesses which offer a wide spectrum of career opportunities, all of which can be explored through the Group's dedicated careers website, emirates.com/careers
Essential to the Group’s ongoing success is the employment of high-quality people who benefit from living and working in Dubai, a modern cosmopolitan city offering one of the most desirable lifestyles in the world. The Emirates Group employees come from over 160 nationalities, receive tax-free salary and benefits package, and are offered professional development opportunities to further their careers with the organisation. If you are a high-performer, seeking a career challenge, personal and professional development, and reward and recognition for your contribution, then the Emirates Group is the perfect opportunity for you. To find out more about the career opportunities the Emirates Groups offers and how to become part of our future growth and rapid success, visit emirates.com/careers
job.job_description:
Job Purpose

A world leader in Air Cargo. We possess a geographical advantage that enables us to reach over 150 destinations across 6 continents. We are able to cater to two-thirds of the world’s population in just 8 hours and serve major international trade regions quickly and efficiently. Our SkyCargo group works in partnership to facilitate global trade and connect customers with their destinations. We have a young, efficient, wide-bodied fleet, with ample capacity to transport cargo through our state-of-the-art, multi-airport hub in Dubai and adapt to our customers ever-changing needs.

Develop corporate initiatives by structuring annual express / courier operating plans, supporting the development of business plans for a defined region. In collaboration with the global team drive the development of commercial sales, operational and marketing initiatives to promote express an courier cargo products and services to achieve regional targets. Provide objective analytical input and advise corrective measures to support the formulation of long and short term strategies to achieve target KPIs and drive business development in the region. In collaboration with the appointed GSA’s and operational partners drive the development of commercial sales and operational excellence to promote products and services to achieve regional targets / product soft / hard targets.

  • Collaborate with headquarter stakeholders to develop a comprehensive annual express/courier revenue plan to target each country / region based on market trends. Ensure revenue plans generate optimal returns for each location with an optimal set of destination and product mix. Support the global team in recommending corrective actions on their business plans to develop new business.
  • Drive commercial and operational partnerships to promote sales in new markets. Establish a market leading sales approach, measure service and promote sales in new markets, ensuring adequate measures of success are in place to measure rate of success.
  • Evaluate performance against revenue and operational / service performance plans at each location. Undertake country reviews and approve incentive / development plans in conjunction with the global team to identify areas for improvement and develop new business opportunities. Pro-actively engage with customers, peers and other commercial stakeholders to ensure product mix and segment/agent mix is optimal and track and measure actions to achieve desired impact. Based on market/country reviews, establish new traffic flows and new markets (offline points) to develop new business.
  • Participate along with the global team in identifying, shortlisting, conducting financial evaluation of new GSSA/CSSA for new markets. Recommend ORC (cost of sale) terms and conditions and recommend commercial KPIs to selected GSSA/CSSA. Measure rate of attainment to set targets and take corrective actions to ensure market presence and business development opportunities are not compromised.
  • Collaborate with Capcon, express pricing, Regional Global Accts team in RFQ reviews, strategically and tactically decide price position for RFQ and other targeted business to develop new traffic flows, improve market share and contribute to incremental revenue generation.
  • Interact with customers, Trade delegations and the Product Development team to develop new initiatives and measure rate of success after ensuring all marketing and sales support are provided.
  • Support the development of a comprehensive marketing plan for the region, collaborate with Product Development team on focus areas and recommend any specific promotional, collateral and advertising material specific to region in collaboration with Corporate Communications to ensure maximum market exposure and marketing activities support revenue generation. Drive comprehensive project plan for new station start-ups in the region defining station targets, ensure potential customers needs are established, price positioning and allocations secured as needed, to ensure business development activities are well supported.
  • Act as a change agent under guidance of the global team and other headquarters stakeholders to facilitate operational initiatives required for new business processes and systems rollout. Responsible for evaluating and scoring regional commercial and operational partners using the developmental score cards on pre-selected criteria.
  • In coordination with the global team, create and manage comprehensive project plans for new station start-ups in the region, defining station targets, and ensuring potential customers needs are established. Also ensuring price positioning is appropriate and operational compliance is in place.
  • Provide support and direction to operations managers in respect of service performance support and analytical work and regional training needs plans to achieve regional objectives and provide direction and guidance on expenditure budget guidelines to ensure partner productivity is improved and stations are well resourced.

Qualification

Qualifications & Experience

  • A minimum of 8 years experience gained in Logistics, Express or Courier Cargo.

Knowledge/Skills

  • Degree preferably in a Commercial discipline with emphasis on sales, marketing and business administration related subjects.
  • Experience in an operational / service performance environment within the courier / express industry, preferably on a multi-country basis.
  • Experience in a sales, marketing or business development role in the express / courier logistics industry.
  • Must have considerable sales experience, be an organised and strong negotiator, be aware of and responsive to economic and industry trends and business/market fluctuations.
  • Specific experience in the sales and operational environment within the express / courier industry , including an understanding of operations, customs, marketing and pricing.
  • Excellent presentation, analytical and report writing skills.
  • Fluent in written and spoken English.

Information

  • Applications from staff who have not completed 12 months’ service in their current position will not be considered.
  • Candidates must have the legal right to live and work in the U.K. The Company will not assist in obtaining visas/work permits.

Salary & benefits

Competitive Salary

At Emirates, we believe it is important that we reward our colleagues for helping us to be the largest and one of the most successful airlines in the world. Some of the benefits you can look forward to include a competitive basic salary along with allowances including shift pay, transport and meal allowances. Every November we will reward you with a guaranteed bonus and each May we operate a non-contractual profit share scheme.

Of course we want to make sure are happy and healthy so we have a host of benefits, such as private medical insurance, a health cash plan, employee assistance programmes available to you from day one. You will also be able to spend your time off travelling the world as you will have access to discounted flights and hotels around the world.
job.title: Regional Express Manager - U.K.
