job.job_description:
As you contemplate your future, you might be asking yourself, what’s the next step? Start your journey with us!

We’re seeking an experienced Java Software Engineer to join our Accommodation team in Barcelona, Madrid or Alicante to develop and leverage the best e-commerce technology to create a one-stop-shop for our customers, where they can find the best hotel for their needs and experience amazing post-booking service in our best-rated mobile app. If you’re ready to soar, we’re ready to take you there

Why eDreams ODIGEO

Join the world’s leading travel subscription platform.

Millions of customers every year across 44 markets – 5 brands – 7.7million Prime members since launching in 2017

More than 100 million searches per day on our websites - more than 6 billion AI daily predictions

Over 1,700 employees – More than 60 different nationalities from all continents – 99% permanent contracts

We're the world’s leading travel subscription platform. We pioneered Prime, the first and largest travel subscription programme, which has topped over 7.7 million members since launching in 2017. Prime members are subscribed to global travel, gaining access to a comprehensive multi-product offering for all their travel needs—including hotels, rail, flights, dynamic packages and car rental, among others— compounded by industry-leading flexibility features and exclusive, member-only benefits. This entire Prime experience is powered by a proprietary, industry-leading AI platform that delivers a smarter, hyper-personalised service and comprehensive travel experience globally to its members.

What you will do:

The Role's Key Responsibilities and Tasks

As an eDOer, you will have clear objectives, great challenges and a clear overview of how your work contributes to the global company project and its customers.

As a Java Software Engineer in the Accommodation area, you will be in charge of:

  • Work with a top-notch tech stack such as Java 17, Maven, TestNG, Cucumber, Mockito, Oracle, ElasticSearch, BigTable, Redis, Kafka,, Datamesh, Datadog, Kibana; Docker, Kubernetes, Google Cloud Platform, Sonarqube and soon Spring Boot;
  • Create distributed, scalable microservices platforms, where software gets deployed daily, with high quality, clean code, SOLID principles, design patterns and TDD;
  • Be independent when working with the modules on your area of expertise, in any phase of the SDLC;
  • Design & develop advanced systems and innovative solutions that help us solve big problems related to the sales life cycle of a hotel booking. Share them with both technical and non-technical audiences;
  • Code the solutions with the highest quality through good design, attention to detail and test quality. Be a referral of best practices within the team.


You will work in a fully agile organisation, using Kanban practices to deliver business value continuously. You will actively participate in team ceremonies and events, as well as the product definition and ideation processes, as part of a high-performing cross-functional team, working with other top engineers, product managers, architects, UX designers to design, build, operate and evolve the Hotels Domain microservices platform. You will receive mentorship and support to help you develop your career super fast.

What you will do:

  • Deep understanding of software engineering concepts such as OOP, functional programming, concurrency, data structures, design and architecture patterns, databases, APIs etc.;
  • Experience working with the entire software development cycle, including deploying to production and testing (both unit and integration), as well as Cloud and Continuous Delivery environments;
  • Strong data-driven capabilities, analytical skills, design pattern skills and creativity that will allow you to solve complex business and customer problems;
  • Ambition to be the best and provide resilient, high-performing, well-dimensioned solutions to complex problems;
  • Strong focus on quality, and a technical excellence mindset;
  • Excellent communication skills and an open mind to reach agreements for the benefit of the team and your stakeholders;
  • Ability to visually represent your solutions in standard diagrams that can be shared with both technical and non-technical audience;
  • Strong organisational skills. Ability to organise, prioritise and delegate tasks to overcome deadlines in an uncertain environment
  • Fluent English level that will allow you to work in a multicultural environment and communicate at all levels;
  • A strong mindset for continuous learning and improvement, growth and innovation;
  • A team player attitude with a focus on knowledge sharing within a dynamic team;
  • Passion for what you do, willing to improve every day and apply the latest state-of-the-art technology, when possible, to your solutions.


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with Applied AI Tools: Demonstrated comfort using practical AI tools such as GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, or other AI-powered coding assistants.
  • Experimentation Mindset: Curiosity and eagerness to explore, experiment with, and integrate emerging AI-driven solutions into software development workflows.
  • AI-Enhanced Problem Solving: Ability to effectively leverage AI tools to debug code, streamline development processes, and enhance productivity.
  • Adaptability and Learning Agility: Enthusiastic about continuously learning and quickly adapting to new AI features and capabilities.
  • Collaboration with AI: Experience or interest in collaborating closely with AI tools as “pair programmers” to complement traditional software development practices.


What’s in it for you?

The best talent deserves the best benefits

At eDO, we want you to be a part of our success story and great culture.

Here’s what we offer:

  • A rewarding Compensation package! 💥Prime Plus membership, competitive salary and benefits package, including flexible benefits, performance-based bonuses, birthday day off, discounts and partnerships, relocation support and premium equipment with role-based selection options and device ownership through our equipment lifecycle program when it reaches its refresh cycle.
  • Continuous learning to fuel your growth and explore new horizons! 📚 Learn and grow with free Coursera access, soft skills workshops, tech training, leadership development, and more. Plus, enjoy a great onboarding program.
  • Grow opportunities to empower your career, and unleash your potential! 🚀Personalised career paths and the eVOLVE Program will help you discover, grow, and thrive. Internal mobility opportunities let you pursue horizontal career changes and promotions.
  • Your Well-being is our Priority. Embrace Freedom and Flexibility! 💙 At eDO, we value flexibility, employee care, and transparency. We offer a hybrid home-office model focused on outcome. You’ll be able to find the right work-personal life balance that suits you best.
  • Work hard, party hard! We believe in having fun and connecting with colleagues! 🎉 Join eDO for after-work events, padel tournaments, parties, and more. Create communities based on your passions, like sports and music. Come to work as you are, with no dress code, and enjoy free fruit, coffee, and tea at our offices.
  • Enjoy a dynamic and healthy environment! 💫 Be innovative, take risks, and share your ideas. Our diverse and open-minded teams support high performance, learning, and growth. You’ll work in an Agile mindset environment with recognition at our core.


Wanna take a peek into what it’s like to work at eDO? Follow us on LinkedIn and discover more about life at eDO!

If you are ready for a career opportunity with unmatched benefits, continuous learning, and a supportive work-life balance, look no further! Take your career to new destinations by applying now and help our diverse, inclusive, and passionate team shape the future of travel.

Apply now!

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status. Our people are the key to our success. Each one of them is unique in their own way and the respect for their uniqueness is and will always be a passion and motivation. Don’t meet every requirement? job.company_description: dnata is a leading global air and travel services provider. Established in 1959, the company offers quality and safe ground handling, cargo, travel, catering and retail services in 34 countries across six continents.
job.job_description:
We’re En Route. We’re a welcoming bunch, with a down-to-earth outlook that helps build lasting relationships with customers and colleagues alike.

As global specialists in passenger solutions, supply chain services and sourcing for airlines and airline caterers, we operate globally as part of dnata, one of the world’s largest combined air services providers.

En Route’s expertise is one of the many reasons why we’ve enjoyed more than 20 years as a global food solutions partner, with offices in the UK, USA, Australia and UAE.

Are you ready to become part of our story?

Created by En Route colleagues, our values sit at the very heart of our business.

  • Birthday day off
  • 25 days off a year
  • Company bonus scheme
  • Hybrid Working (3 days in office)
  • Medical Insurance
  • Referral Scheme
  • Social Events
  • Flexi Time

The Senior Account Manager (SAM) is responsible for leading and exceeding sales & profit targets for the region through the development and execution of strategic business strategies. Working within company guidelines and investment parameters, the SAM will drive growth by acquiring new net sales, increasing sales to existing customers, and maintaining customer retention.

Collaboration with operational and cross-functional teams is essential to ensure exceptional service delivery and customer satisfaction. Additionally, the SAM may have line management responsibilities, overseeing a team of account managers to achieve collective goals and objectives.

Key Responsibilities

  • Take ownership of sales budget performance and engage in contract negotiations with key stakeholders across the UK/EU to impact the departmental P&L significantly.
  • Expand market share, achieve, and surpass sales & gross margin objectives.
  • Lead negotiations and strategy with key prospective accounts, making critical decisions.
  • Conduct quarterly meetings with key prospects and customers to review performance and strategize.
  • Cultivate and manage commercial relationships for En Route in the region.
  • Develop in-depth insights into customer businesses and establish strategic partnerships with decision-makers up to the main board level.
  • Understand customer business development plans and align En Route’s support to enable their success.
  • Conduct periodic product/range reviews to ensure offerings meet account needs and drive growth and profitability.
  • Generate market activity reports, analyse trends, and present findings to the senior team as needed.
  • Provide accurate and timely performance, financial, and volume forecasting and reporting for accounts, considering market dynamics and business fluctuations.
  • Collaborate with Marketing to analyse competitor activities and propose strategic changes based on data insights.
  • Develop and implement a strategic sales plan in coordination with Commercial, NPD, and Creative teams to achieve sales and margin targets.
  • Contribute to business development initiatives in the region, including tender processes (RFQ/RFP, etc.).
  • Ensure adherence to approved business case and pricing models as per company standards.
  • Manage project implementations in collaboration with the operational team to ensure a seamless transition from concept to operation.
  • Inspire and motivate the local team to enhance performance and meet customer expectations.
  • Serve as a role model for the team, offering coaching and support to the operational team as needed.
  • Provide monthly reports to the senior management team.
  • Maintain up-to-date customer and prospect records in En Route systems, ensuring accurate CRM data management for effective opportunity tracking.
  • Establish measurable goals for short- and long-term sales and overall growth aligned with En Route’s vision and targets, regularly evaluating performance against set standards to drive profitable growth.
  • To undertake any other duties, which may from time to time be assigned. This job description is not intended to be either prescriptive or exhaustive, but it is issued as a general framework at the time of writing.
  • Sales and/or Airline Catering experience
  • Proven success within strategic sales / commercial account management / business development
  • Strong commercial awareness (essential)
  • Experience in regional catering/food/logistics markets
  • Highly numerate (to interpret and produce financial plans, account profit, margins, etc.)
  • Good data and business analysis and reporting skills
  • Able to construct and deliver influential commercial selling presentations
  • Superior negotiation skills
  • PC literate including Excel, Word, PowerPoint, etc.
  • Native speaker level English (spoken/written), plus one European language

If you’re interested in applying for this role, please send a copy of your most recent CV to EnRouteRecruitment@en-route.com

Please no agencies.

We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable, and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences, and we are dedicated to ensuring a fair and accessible recruitment process for all candidates. If you require any adjustments or support during the application or interview stages, please let us know – we’re here to help!

Regrettably, due to the high volume of applications we receive, we are unable to respond individually to every candidate. If you have not heard from us within 10 working days of submitting your application, unfortunately you have not been successful on this occasion.

We take your privacy seriously. Any personal data you share with us during the application process will be handled in accordance with applicable data protection laws and used solely for recruitment purposes. Your information will be stored securely and retained only for as long as necessary.
job.title: Senior Account Manager – Commercial

job.job_description:
We’re En Route. We’re a welcoming bunch, with a down-to-earth outlook that helps build lasting relationships with customers and colleagues alike.

As global specialists in passenger solutions, supply chain services and sourcing for airlines and airline caterers, we operate globally as part of dnata, one of the world’s largest combined air services providers.

En Route’s expertise is one of the many reasons why we’ve enjoyed more than 20 years as a global food solutions partner, with offices in the UK, USA, Australia and UAE.

Are you ready to become part of our story?

Created by En Route colleagues, our values sit at the very heart of our business.

  • Birthday day off
  • 25 days off a year
  • Company bonus scheme
  • Hybrid Working (3 days in office)
  • Medical Insurance
  • Referral Scheme
  • Social Events
  • Flexi Time

The Accounts Receivable Assistant will support the Finance department assisting in transacting of accounts receivable related balances and reporting aspects of the En Route Group.

The AR Assistant will report into the AP/AR Manager.

This is a 12 month full-time contract.

Key Objectives

  • Co-responsible for AR reporting across the group of companies
  • Overseeing and managing day-to-day accounts receivable transactions including adherence to group guidelines
  • Assisting on any audit AR related queries for the UK entity and overseas units as required
  • Assisting with the AR customer master data on Business Central
  • Supporting improved cash flow processes, and ongoing cash forecasting for all entities
  • Supporting improved ongoing sales forecasting for all entities
  • Drive improved efficiency & accuracy in reporting through process improvements
  • Assist with maintaining internal controls, processes and procedures
  • Undertake any other duties (e.g. supporting AP team during times of reduced capacity) as required by management, commensurate with the grade of the post
  • Assist in raising all intercompany, related party and 3rd party customer sales invoices/credit notes for all En Route entities
  • Assist in monthly customer statement issuance and reconciliation of balances
  • Assist in chasing debt and resolving customer queries
  • Assist in month-end tasks for the reporting team from AR related balances including AR KPIs
  • Assist in daily update of sales forecast tracker
  • Assist in weekly update of cashflow tracker
  • Assist in updating the relevant AR information within the monthly MBR report for dnata
  • Assist in AR projects with IT and BC consultant with a view to enhance the AR reporting
  • Attend all relevant internal and external training
  • Assist in any adhoc requests from management
  • Assist in resolving year end AR audit related queries to external auditors
  • Ensure existing process notes are updated for any changes

Please note this is a hybrid role based out of Windsor, Berkshire.

If you’re interested in applying for this role, please send a copy of your most recent CV to EnRouteRecruitment@en-route.com

Please no agencies.

We are committed to creating an inclusive, equitable, and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences, and we are dedicated to ensuring a fair and accessible recruitment process for all candidates. If you require any adjustments or support during the application or interview stages, please let us know – we’re here to help!

Regrettably, due to the high volume of applications we receive, we are unable to respond individually to every candidate. If you have not heard from us within 10 working days of submitting your application, unfortunately you have not been successful on this occasion.

We take your privacy seriously. Any personal data you share with us during the application process will be handled in accordance with applicable data protection laws and used solely for recruitment purposes. Your information will be stored securely and retained only for as long as necessary.
job.title: Accounts Receivable Assistant – FTC

job.company_description:
GetYourGuide is a leading global online marketplace to discover and book experiences worth traveling for. Travelers can use GetYourGuide to find things to do in more than 18,000 cities, including tours from local experts, exclusive access to must-see attractions, as well as immersive bucket-list experiences. More than 50,000 supply partners leverage GetYourGuide’s easy-to-use platform to grow their businesses, offering 200,000 experiences to travelers around the world.
If you share our passion for travel and tech, check out our open positions at careers.getyourguide.com or take a look behind the scenes of GetYourGuide on our blog: inside.getyourguide.com.
Important notice: Protect yourself from recruitment scams.
To ensure your safety during the application process, please be aware of potential scammers impersonating GetYourGuide recruiters. Remember, GetYourGuide will never request your payment or sensitive personal information during recruitment. All official job opportunities are exclusively posted on our Careers page. If you suspect fraudulent activity, report it to jobs@getyourguide.com.
job.job_description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

The Supply Analytics team defines how GetYourGuide measures and grows the supply side of our marketplace. We answer the strategic questions that shape our strategy: Where do we have gaps in our marketplace? How can we help suppliers be more successful? What product innovations would unlock the most value for partners?

Our team builds the analytics foundation, metrics, and frameworks that power decision-making across Product, Sales, and Operations. We translate complex supply dynamics into actionable insights that drive resource allocation, product roadmaps, and business priorities—helping 50,000+ suppliers grow their businesses on our platform.

Your mission

  • Shape supply strategy with data: Help shape the culture of our supply teams to be more data-driven, and guide strategy by answering the critical questions—which markets to expand in, which suppliers to prioritize, where sales resources drive the most value, and what product changes matter most to partners
  • Build measurement systems from scratch: Create the metrics, frameworks, and primitives that help us understand the supplier lifecycle & experience
  • Lead, strengthen, and scale a high-performing team: Manage and develop a team that delivers with technical excellence and operates as thought partners
  • Influence at the executive level: Translate complex analyses into crisp, strategic recommendations that shape resource allocation, product roadmaps, and business priorities across the Supply organization
  • Partner across Product, Sales, and Operations: Work directly with senior leaders to align analytics work with business impact, balancing competing priorities and driving proactive, strategic insights
  • Stay hands-on when it matters: Be curious and go hands-on, developing your own analytical insights, turning that into action and recommendation, and review analysesto set a high bar for excellence
  • Mature analytics best practices: Partner with the VP of Data to establish frameworks, processes, and standards that elevate how GetYourGuide uses data across the company

Your toolkit

  • Proven leadership at scale: You’ve hired, developed, and scaled teams in fast-growth environments. You’ve managed managers - and are great at it.
  • Strategic partnership: You’ve shaped roadmaps, influenced resource allocation, and built measurement frameworks that change how businesses make decisions - not just reported on them.
  • Hands-on technical fluency: You’re a hands-on leader proficient with SQL, experimentation design, metric definition, and strategic analytics. You can review a complex analysis, spot the flaw, and jump into the data as needed. You know how to delegate and run a scaled team - but you enjoy going hands-on and will use your skills to up-level the team.
  • Influence executives: You translate complex analyses into crisp recommendations. You know when to push back on ideas and when to say “we don’t know yet, but here’s how we’ll find out.”

Extras that give you an edge

  • Two-sided marketplace experience
  • Experience working with operations, sales, and product teams

This is a critical hire for GetYourGuide as we continue to strengthen this essential pillar of the business and unlock new opportunities. The right leader will be a catalyst for impact—raising the bar for analytics and empowering the Supply cluster to better understand their impact and ecosystem.

How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary

  • Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
  • Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
  • A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
  • Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
  • Monthly transportation and fitness budget
  • Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
  • Language reimbursement program
  • Health and wellness benefits

And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.

We’re an equal opportunities employer

Our commitment is that every qualified person will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion. Please refrain from including your picture and age with your application.
job.title: Senior Analytics Manager, Supply

Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

For our travelers: Ensure an outstanding customer experience across all touch-points of the booking process, offering a reliable and seamless API service.

For our Connectivity Partners: Create a comprehensive platform that streamlines and facilitates their integration process and provides a top-tier service supporting their expansion goals and processes.

For internal teams: Develop robust centralized communication and innovative solutions to assist their projects, and educate global teams on API and partner relationships.

As a Connectivity Project Manager, you will be a key execution partner within cross-functional connectivity teams, working closely with Product Managers, Engineering, Support, Sales, and external partners. You will own key steps in the end-to-end delivery of multiple complex outbound integrations in parallel as well as the ongoing monitoring and operational stability of our existing outbound connectors. Acting as the central point of coordination, you will ensure that integrations move from feasibility to go-live in a predictable, transparent, and scalable way.

You will also serve as the voice of the Connectivity Business Operations teams towards product development, helping shape future connectivity capabilities by feeding back operational and analytical insights, partner needs, and delivery constraints.

Successful Connectivity Project Managers at GetYourGuide are strong coordinators, trusted partners to suppliers, and champions of clarity and accountability across highly technical, multi-stakeholder initiatives.

Your mission

  • Manage the execution of multiple complex connectivity projects, from partner onboarding and feasibility assessment through, testing, go-live, and post-go-live phases.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for external connectivity partners and their engineering teams, ensuring clear alignment on timelines, responsibilities, technical and business expectations.
  • Coordinate closely with Product Managers on the development and long-term evolution of outbound connectors and our Connectivity Hub, representing delivery realities and operational feedback into product discussions.
  • Closely collaborate with the Engineering team to manage dependencies and external stakeholders, and support driving predictable delivery across parallel integrations.
  • Lead cross-functional stakeholder alignment across Engineering, Product, Commercial, Sales, Support, and external partners.
  • Serve as a point of contact for commercial and sales enablement related to our outbound connectors, supporting partner conversations and readiness.
  • Drive continuous improvement of connectivity processes, documentation, monitoring and ways of working to increase operational excellence and scalability.
  • Identify risks early, proactively manage issues, and ensure clear communication and clean escalation where needed.

Your toolkit

  • Experience as a technical or digital Project Manager planning and delivering multiple, parallel, complex, cross-functional projects in a technology-driven environment, ideally involving API-based integrations or platform capabilities
  • Strong understanding of API technologies, web services, and marketplace or partner integrations, with the ability to read and interpret technical specifications and collaborate effectively with engineering teams
  • Strong organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize effectively and operate autonomously in a fast-paced environment
  • Proactive risk and issue management skills, including early identification, mitigation, and clear escalation when needed
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with confidence engaging internal and external stakeholders across technical, commercial, and operational contexts
  • Strong analytical and BI skills, with the ability to interpret data, identify patterns, and support operational and delivery improvements
  • Structured problem-solving skills, with the ability to develop effective solutions in complex and ambiguous situations
  • Customer-centric mindset with a strong focus on delivering high-quality, reliable integration outcomes
  • Ownership-driven and improvement-oriented mindset, with the ability to adapt ways of working and continuously raise operational standards
  • Fluency in English both written and spoken

Extras that give you an edge

  • Industry experience in Shows and Events or Tours and Attractions is a plus

How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary

  • Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
  • Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
  • A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
  • Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
  • Monthly transportation and fitness budget
  • Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
  • Language reimbursement program
  • Health and wellness benefits

And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.

We’re an equal opportunities employer

Our commitment is that every qualified person will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion. Please refrain from including your picture and age with your application.

job.job_description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

Within Supply Data Engineering - part of the strategic Flywheel Data Engineering group - our mission is to build the trusted data foundations and self-serve capabilities for the supply side of our marketplace. We ingest, integrate, and structure critical internal and external datasets into reliable tables, metrics, and data products that help GetYourGuide onboard suppliers, support their success, and scale supply operations. We achieve this through close partnership with Supply, Product and Data, enabling teams to move faster with confidence.

Your mission

  • Build and lead a high-performing team: Hire, coach, and support data engineers to do their best work through clear expectations, direct feedback, and performance management.
  • Set direction and deliver outcomes: Shape the roadmap, strategy, and long-term vision for Supply Data Engineering - aligned with the priorities of Product, Sales, and Data partners working in the space
  • Be a trusted thought partner: Act as a strategic advisor to stakeholders across Supply, Product and Data, building strong relationships and driving adoption of trusted datasets and self-serve capabilities.
  • Raise the bar on technical excellence: Ensure strong data architecture, reliability, and data quality. Establish effective engineering practices and standards while balancing hands-on technical leadership with people leadership.
  • Power critical decision-making and initiatives: Lead work that integrates and operationalizes internal and external datasets (including competitive signals) to enable initiatives such as dynamic/competitive pricing, availability and inventory optimization, and quality improvements - so Supply can move faster with confidence.

Your toolkit

  • Experience: 3+ years in a relevant data management role with hands-on experience.
  • Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, able to explain technical concepts clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Strategic leadership + business acumen: You translate strategic needs into crisp priorities, measurable outcomes, and executable roadmaps.
  • Cross-functional influence: You align stakeholders across Supply, Product and Data - navigating trade-offs and keeping delivery focused on impact.
  • Player-coach leadership: You combine strong coaching with technical depth - reviewing designs and code, improving engineering standards, and mentoring engineers.
  • High-trust team leadership: You build an inclusive, high-trust team with strong engagement and retention, develop high performers, and address performance issues promptly and fairly.
  • Data engineering fundamentals: You’re proficient in SQL and Python and bring strong grounding in data modeling, pipeline design, orchestration, observability, and data quality. You’re comfortable engaging directly with production systems.
  • Pragmatic, self-serve mindset: You value automation, documentation, and enablement to drive scalable self-serve adoption.
  • AI-first mindset: You leverage modern AI tools and techniques to boost engineering productivity and accelerate solving business needs - while maintaining strong standards for quality, security, and governance.

Extras that give you an edge

  • Relevant domain experience: You’ve worked in a marketplace business before, ideally working with seller/supplier-side data (e.g., pricing, availability, inventory, quality). You thrive in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
  • Modern data stack exposure: You have experience with tools such as Databricks/Spark, dbt, and Looker (or comparable technologies).
  • Strength with challenging external data: You’re curious and resilient with real-world data constraints, especially integrating frequently changing external/competitive sources - while maintaining reliability, governance, and trust.

How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary

  • Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
  • Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
  • A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
  • Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
  • Monthly transportation and fitness budget
  • Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
  • Language reimbursement program
  • Health and wellness benefits

And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.

We’re an equal opportunities employer

Our commitment is that every qualified person will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion. Please refrain from including your picture and age with your application.

Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

As the Business Operations & Strategy team, our mission is to give our customer—the Modern Explorer—unparalleled access to worldwide experiences, offering the best selection, availability, pricing, quality, while fostering shared growth with our experience partners. Our goal is to empower every supplier to thrive on GetYourGuide by providing them with advanced tools, actionable insights, tailored support, and seamless connectivity at every stage of their journey with us.

We are looking for a highly analytical, strategic, action-oriented, and collaborative (Senior) Business Operations & Strategy Manager to join our dynamic Business Operations & Strategy (Optimization) team and help us to take our experiences to the next level!

As a (Senior) Business Operations & Strategy Manager – Optimization (Pricing & Commission Operations), you are responsible for turning our pricing and commission strategy into action. Acting as the bridge between strategy, data and daily execution, you will ensure that commissioned and pricing initiatives are operationalized at scale—enabling Account Management to drive commercial results with their supplier partners and working cross-functionally with our Pricing team, Supplier Marketing, Product, Analytics. You will create the operational processes, toolkits, and playbooks that make pricing and commission actionable, supporting supplier success.

Your mission

  • Design and Optimize Commercial Processes: Build, implement, and continuously improve scalable workflows and best practices that empower Account Management teams and cross-functional partners to execute pricing and commission strategies effectively and drive operational excellence.
  • Global Enablement & Harmonization: Drive strategic programs to align, harmonize, and operationalize Account Management and Price & Commission Execution activities, ensuring consistency and localized adaptation for supply growth, engagement, and retention.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration & Stakeholder Management: Work closely with our Pricing Team, Product, Marketing, Analytics, and regional Account Management leads to identify pricing and commission challenges, extract and roll out best practices, and co-create impactful, actionable solutions.
  • Data-driven Price & Commission Optimization: Leverage data and analytics to identify and implement operational optimization opportunities for pricing and commission, increasing supplier satisfaction, commercial effectiveness, and performance.
  • Operational Tooling & Reporting: Develop and deploy tools, dashboards, and reporting solutions to enable transparent tracking, data-driven decision-making, and continuous improvement in pricing and commission operations for Account Management.

Your toolkit

  • 3+ years of experience in Revenue, Commercial or Sales Operations, Strategy, Business Process Management, or similar; experience in a fast-paced tech, marketplace, or SaaS environment strongly preferred.
  • Proven expertise in operationalizing commercial strategies for pricing, commissions, or incentives at scale.
  • Experience building and rolling out processes, playbooks, and enablement resources for Account Management or Sales.
  • Highly collaborative, with a track record of effective cross-functional partnering and influencing.
  • Highly analytical with strong business acumen; skilled at using data to solve problems, identify opportunities, and inform strategic decisions (experience with SQL, BI tools like Looker, Power BI, Tableau a plus).
  • Strong project management skills, with experience leading complex, cross-functional initiatives from concept through to delivery and adoption.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the confidence to drive alignment and influence globally diverse stakeholders.
  • Comfortable working in high-ambiguity, high-growth environments; entrepreneurial mindset, self-starter, and driver of change.
  • Fluent in English; other languages are an advantage.

How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary

  • Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
  • Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
  • A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
  • Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
  • Monthly transportation and fitness budget
  • Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
  • Language reimbursement program
  • Health and wellness benefits

And more…

Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

Our FP&A team is all about empowering sustainable growth through strategic financial planning and data-driven insights, as a trusted business partner. As a key member of this team, you'll be at the forefront of shaping our strategy, leading planning efforts, and influencing critical financial decisions that propel our company toward its objectives.

Responsibilities

Based in our Berlin headquarters and reporting directly to the Head of Operations FP&A, you will partner with the Operations team, overseen by our COO & Co-Founder, which encompasses our Sales & Supply and Customer Care organisations. Your role is to facilitate timely and effective decision-making processes. You should be analytically and strategically sharp, focused on results, and capable of providing clear, concise recommendations crucial for the company's success.

Key Responsibilities Include

  • Insight Generation: Develop actionable, data-driven insights that challenge and elevate the Sales & Supply team's approach to onboarding the most sought-after tours and activities, driving sustainable growth and operational efficiency.
  • Metrics Analysis and Reporting: Define, monitor, and analyze key financial and operational metrics in a high-growth environment. Build adaptable reporting frameworks and dashboards to meet evolving business needs, ensuring leadership has timely insights to guide strategic decisions and optimize performance.
  • Customer Satisfaction Enhancement: Partner with the Customer Care team to analyze KPIs and deliver concrete insights, aiming to significantly enhance customer satisfaction while maintaining efficiency and cost targets.
  • Business Partnering: Partner with Operations teams to provide financial guidance and support for evolving business priorities. Facilitate cross-functional collaboration to align operational expenditures and financial strategies with company objectives.
  • Planning and Forecasting: Lead the forecasting process to deliver business-owned budgets and forecasts that adapt to changing business needs. Drive innovation in business planning and forecasting by identifying key metric drivers and streamlining processes, enhancing overall business agility and foresight.
  • Investment Strategy: Evaluate investment opportunities independently, creating robust business cases. Identify and articulate necessary trade-offs to inform strategic financial planning and resource allocation.

Your Profile

  • Experience: 5+ years of relevant experience and qualifications in FP&A, strategy, consulting, or a similar field within a fast-paced, dynamic business environment. Experience in a senior or leadership capacity is beneficial.
  • Communication: Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to present complex financial insights clearly and persuasively to stakeholders at all levels, including senior leadership.
  • Strategic Thinking: Strong strategic mindset, leveraging data-driven insights and robust business acumen to influence key business decisions and drive organizational impact.
  • Analytical Skills: Strong analytical abilities and a data-driven mindset, with proficiency in financial modeling and forecasting techniques.
  • Collaboration: A proactive and collaborative approach to driving work forward, with a focus on fostering teamwork and achieving impactful results in dynamic environments.
  • Personal Attributes: Intellectual curiosity, adaptability, and a passion for problem-solving. Comfort with ambiguity and openness to learning new tools and approaches as the role evolves.
  • Technical Skills: Experience with BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker) and a basic understanding of data warehouse architecture and database querying (e.g., SQL) preferred. Advanced proficiency in Google Suite, MS Office, and financial modeling, with experience in ERP or CPM systems highly beneficial.

How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary

  • Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
  • Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
  • Flexible working arrangements to support work-life balance (Monday, Tuesday and Thursdays in the office)
  • Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events

Change the way the world travels

Be part of the GetYourGuide journey and connect people with unforgettable travel experiences worldwide. Since 2009, millions of travelers have booked unique activities with us in over 12,000 cities. Our headquarters in Berlin is supported by local offices across the globe, from New York to Bangkok.

Ready to join a diverse community of over 850 fellow explorers dedicated to revolutionizing the travel experience industry? Check out getyourguide.careers to learn more.

Team mission

Our mission is to offer the world's most comprehensive, curated selection. Pricing is a key lever for this. The pricing team will drive profitable, sustainable growth by owning and optimizing GetYourGuide's global pricing and inventory strategy.

This role is responsible for establishing a data-driven framework for pricing and commission decisions, ensures highly competitive positioning, and balances the core objectives of operator partnership, customer acquisition, and margin health. We will transform pricing from a function into a powerful, strategic asset that defines our market leadership.

Your mission

  • Define and lead the commercial pricing strategy, turning company growth objectives into actionable pricing levers
  • Design and evolve commission structures, discount programs, and incentive models that align supplier success with marketplace goals
  • Establish and oversee the collection and use of competitive pricing insights, ensuring we have the right coverage across markets and categories
  • Build scalable playbooks that enable Sales and Account Management teams to engage suppliers effectively on pricing topics
  • Develop frameworks for ensuring fair and competitive pricing, balancing customer trust with strong supplier relationships
  • Partner with Product and Data leaders to connect commercial priorities with the pricing platform and tools
  • Act as the commercial voice of pricing in the organization, providing insights and influencing senior stakeholders

Your toolkit

  • 10+ years of experience in marketplaces, e-commerce, or travel, with a strong track record in pricing, revenue management, or commercial strategy
  • 5+ years track record working on marketplace pricing
  • Strong commercial acumen and ability to balance supplier revenue goals with competitive customer pricing
  • Experience working cross-functionally with Product, Data, and Sales in a fast-paced environment
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills; able to influence senior leadership and external partners
  • Based in or willing to relocate to Berlin

job.job_description:
Change the way the world travels

GetYourGuide is the global leading marketplace for unforgettable travel experiences. Travelers use GetYourGuide to discover the best things to do in a destination. Since its founding in 2009, people from all over the world have booked more than 150 million tours, activities, and attraction tickets through GetYourGuide. Powered by a global team of over 800 travel experts and technologists, we are headquartered in Berlin and have 17 local offices around the world. Visit our careers website to learn more

The Role

We are searching for an outstanding Lead Strategic Partnerships Manager to establish, maintain, and grow partnerships with some of the world’s leading brands in EMEA. The role reports to the Head of Partnerships, EMEA, and will play a pivotal role in growing GetYourGuide's market share in EMEA, our fastest-growing region. Please note this is an individual contributor position.

Your Mission

  • Strategically expand our EMEA portfolio while owning, managing, and growing a multi-million dollar portfolio of enterprise accounts and industry relationships
  • Build a strong pipeline of exceptional partnership prospects that offer the growth potential to grow EMEA GMV contribution by 5X
  • Contribute to the design of, and execute, playbooks, sales, and growth systems needed to penetrate strategic verticals
  • Develop strong internal partnerships and collaborate cross-functionally to maximize revenue opportunities to improve deal progressions
  • Be accountable for selling to existing customers and net new customers
  • Work closely with Legal, FP&A, Partner Tech, Supply, Accounting, Product, and Brand and Growth Marketing Teams to build feasible partnership structures, ensure compliance with contractual terms and achievement of partnership milestones, and maximize the impact of the GetYourGuide brand.
  • Regularly communicate strategies, impact, learnings, and next steps to GetYourGuide leadership via weekly, monthly, and quarterly business updates.
  • Represent GetYourGuide by interfacing with business leaders in an acute business setting (sales pitches, ongoing partner meetings, etc) and at industry events such as conferences, trade shows, and through participation in local trade associations.

Your Toolkit

  • 7+ years of experience working in sales, key account management, or business development functions at high-growth marketplace, SaaS, e-commerce, airline or online travel tech companies
  • Connected, have a good network and reputation
  • Proficient and have an understanding of affiliate marketing and Strategic Partnership
  • Good business acumen and commercial instincts, specifically in contract negotiation and relationship management
  • An understanding of online marketplace dynamics, with demonstrated success in driving growth, tangible results, and KPI improvements
  • Entrepreneurial attitude to win despite all challenges, thriving in a fast-paced environment, a growth mindset, and learner's attitude
  • Highly collaborative, outstanding communication, negotiation, persuasion, and interpersonal skills with a strong win-win mentality and natural ability to build relationships
  • Data-driven, results-oriented, and accurate
  • Excited to spend up to 30% of your time traveling
  • Model GetYourGuide’s in-person first value and work from the office, min 3+ days per week.
  • Proficiency in English is a requirement, and proficiency in French or Spanish is a plus.

How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary

  • Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
  • Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
  • A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
  • Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
  • Monthly transportation and fitness budget
  • Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
  • Language reimbursement program
  • Health and wellness benefits

And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.
