job.job_description:
Join our Movement and Champion Restaurant Culture! 💚

At TheFork we believe that the best things in life happen around the table.

As the leading restaurant booking platform in Europe, we connect the broadest community of loyal diners with the world’s favorite restaurants. Powered by innovation and a deep passion for the restaurant industry, we create unique dining experiences across 11 countries.

We’re part of the Tripadvisor Group and proud to be building a diverse, people-first culture where “respect”, “ownership”, “growth” and “better together” values thrive.

If you’re passionate about food, technology, and making a real impact, your seat at the table is ready.

👉 Discover life at TheFork

What You Will Do

  • Grow revenue from your restaurant portfolio month over month by selling and activating TheFork solutions.
  • Act as the main point of contact for your restaurants and build strong, long-term partnerships.
  • Support your portfolio mainly by email and phone, advising on positioning, revenue management and online marketing.
  • Define and execute action plans in collaboration with Sales and Marketing teams to drive performance.
  • Work closely with the Marketing Restaurant Offer team to create and manage promotional plans, including Festivals, special offers, YUMS and SUPER YUMS.
  • Negotiate fair and sustainable commercial conditions that improve restaurant margins and increase seat occupancy.
  • Monitor compliance with TheFork terms and conditions and quality standards to ensure an excellent B2C user experience.
  • Provide onboarding, training and ongoing support to ensure effective use of TheFork tools.
  • Share restaurant feedback and user needs with internal teams to continuously improve TheFork products and platforms.

Key performance indicators (KPIs)

  • Monthly revenue generated by restaurants in your portfolio.
  • Number and quality of promotions available across the portfolio.
  • Level of B2C issues or customer complaints.
  • Number of restaurants participating in the YUMS and SUPER YUMS programmes.
  • Number and quality of restaurants taking part in TheFork Festivals.

Who You Are

You Must Meet These Critical Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience in account management or a similar commercial role.
  • Fluent French and fluent English, both written and spoken.
  • Fully computer literate, with experience using MS Office and Salesforce or a similar CRM.
  • Comfortable having sales conversations and negotiations over the phone.
  • Experience managing a high volume of outbound and inbound calls (20–50 per day) and large account portfolios (600–1,000 accounts).
  • Self-starter with a strong results focus and experience working with KPIs and targets.
  • Ability to manage several priorities or projects at the same time.

You Can Impress With These Additions

  • Experience in the Internet or technology sector.
  • Experience pitching and selling promotions, deals or flash sales.
  • Experience exceeding sales or performance targets on a regular basis.
  • Knowledge of revenue management concepts or commercial optimisation.
  • Strong analytical and creative thinking, with the ability to propose tailored customer solutions.
  • Ability to quickly build trust and develop long-term business relationships.
  • High level of autonomy and confidence working independently.

What We Offer You

😄 An awesome team

🏠 A permanent contract (that can be useful in life)

⚖️Flexible working environment (2 days home office per week + up to 4 total weeks additional flexibility during the summer period and in December to work fully remotely)

💸 Competitive salary & monthly bonus

🍕 Lunch vouchers available for each working day (because yes, we like to try our best restaurants)

🌎 International teams and a multicultural environment spanning 10 offices across Europe

🤝 Highly inclusive working environment

🤸‍♀️ Lifestyle benefits that can be used to reimburse expenses related to physical and leisure activities, family support, travel etc

🎓 Continuous learning and development programs

😌 Free access to the Calm app to help you build resilience wherever you are in your mental health journey

🐣Dedicated parental leave and caregiver leave policies (12 weeks fully paid)

🏥Health insurance fully covered by the company

👩‍🦽 Life & Disability Insurance at no cost to the employee

💗 Welfare allowance school costs, elderly care, babysitting costs, transportation, travel leisure

🍴 Amazing offices with dining, coffee points and leisure area

🎤 Team building events

All hiring happens through our careers site and official email. We do not text or ask for payment during the hiring process. Please report any suspicious messages immediately.

We believe that we are better together, and we welcome you for who you are. We endeavor to ensure that everyone - regardless of ability, age, socio-economic & cultural background, ethnicity, faith, gender, gender expression, gender identity, ideology, national origin, race, sexual orientation, marital status, or any characteristic protected under applicable law -has the opportunity to reach their full potential. At TheFork, we want you to bring us your unique perspectives and experiences, so we can collectively continue disrupting the restaurant industry and go from good to great.

TheFork is committed to a fair recruitment process. If you have special needs and require reasonable support during your application, interview, or participation in the selection process due to health conditions or disability, please direct your inquiries to AccessibleRecruitment@TheFork.com. Our HR team will review the request and respond accordingly.
job.title: Sales Account Manager

job.company_description:
Tripadvisor, the world's largest travel guidance platform*, helps hundreds of millions of people each month** become better travelers, from planning to booking to taking a trip. Travelers across the globe use the Tripadvisor site and app to discover where to stay, what to do and where to eat based on guidance from those who have been there before. With more than 1 billion reviews and opinions of nearly 8 million businesses, travelers turn to Tripadvisor to find deals on accommodations, book experiences, reserve tables at delicious restaurants and discover great places nearby. As a travel guidance company available in 43 markets and 22 languages, Tripadvisor makes planning easy no matter the trip type. The subsidiaries of Tripadvisor, Inc. (Nasdaq: TRIP), own and operate a portfolio of travel media brands and businesses, operating under various websites and apps, including the following:
www.bokun.io, www.cruisecritic.com, www.flipkey.com, www.thefork.com, www.helloreco.com, www.holidaylettings.co.uk, www.jetsetter.com, www.niumba.com, www.seatguru.com, www.singleplatform.com, www.vacationhomerentals.com, www.viator.com.
* Source: SimilarWeb, unique users de-duplicated monthly, September 2023 ** Source: Tripadvisor internal log files
job.job_description:
Join our Movement and Champion Restaurant Culture! 💚

At TheFork we believe that the best things in life happen around the table.

As the leading restaurant booking platform in Europe, we connect the broadest community of loyal diners with the world’s favorite restaurants. Powered by innovation and a deep passion for the restaurant industry, we create unique dining experiences across 11 countries.

We’re part of the Tripadvisor Group and proud to be building a diverse, people-first culture where “respect”, “ownership”, “growth” and “better together” values thrive.

If you’re passionate about food, technology, and making a real impact, your seat at the table is ready.

👉 Discover life at TheFork

What You Will Do

As part of the commercial team, you will manage and grow relationships with top-tier, premium restaurant partners. You will:

  • Develop and manage a targeted pipeline of high-end and fine dining restaurants.
  • Engage directly with owners, chefs, and senior decision-makers to build trusted, long-term relationships via virtual and face to face meetings.
  • Position the platform as a strategic growth and visibility partner, using a consultative, insight-led approach.
  • Negotiate tailored commercial terms that respect brand positioning while driving sustainable revenue growth.
  • Advise partners on positioning, demand generation, and selective promotional strategies aligned with premium brand standards.
  • Overcome objections through credibility, market knowledge, and a nuanced understanding of the premium dining landscape.
  • Represent the brand with a high level of professionalism in all interactions, including industry events and partner meetings.
  • Consistently deliver against monthly and quarterly targets across a variety of KPIs such as yield management and retention.
  • Collaborate closely with Sales Operations, Marketing, Legal and Finance teams to ensure a seamless partner experience.
  • Travel to attend selected industry events, tastings, and high-profile partner engagements as required.

Who You Are

You Must Meet These Critical Qualifications

  • You are fluent in English; additional languages are highly appreciated.
  • You have at least 3 years of experience in sales or account management, ideally within a digital, online, or tech-led business.
  • You have experience managing territories, pipelines, and KPIs using Salesforce or a comparable CRM.
  • You are a self-driven, results-oriented professional who consistently strives to meet and exceed targets.
  • You are confident and comfortable conducting negotiations and commercial conversations over the phone.
  • You have a strong understanding of revenue management–related topics and commercial performance levers.
  • You can quickly establish trust and build credible, long-term business relationships.
  • You work autonomously and are comfortable managing KPIs, targets, and performance metrics.
  • You are able to work on a hybrid basis, with three days per week in the London office and availability to travel 20% of the time.

You Can Impress With These Additions

  • You have experience within the Internet, digital, or tech sector.
  • You have a proven background in pitching and selling promotions, deals, or flash sales.
  • You thrive in fast-paced environments and remain organised under pressure.
  • Hunter mindset, with the drive to identify and convert new business opportunities.

What We Offer You

  • 😄 An awesome team
  • 🏠 A permanent contract
  • ⚖️ Flexible working environment (2 days home office per week + up to 4 total weeks additional flexibility during the summer period and in December to work fully remotely)
  • 💸 Competitive fixed salary and bonus
  • 🍕 Lunch vouchers available 3 working days per week (delivered to the office only)
  • 🌎 International teams and a multicultural environment spanning 11 offices across Europe
  • 🤝Highly inclusive working environment
  • 🤸‍♀️ Lifestyle benefits that can be used to reimburse expenses related to physical and leisure activities, family support, travel etc
  • 🎓 Continuous learning and development programs
  • 😌 Free access to the Calm app to help you build resilience wherever you are in your mental health journey
  • 🐣Dedicated parental leave and caregiver leave policies (12 weeks fully paid)
  • 🏥 Health insurance
  • 👩‍🦽 Life & Disability Insurance at no cost to the employee
  • 👴 Attractive pension plans
  • 🍴 Amazing offices with dining, a coffee point on each floor, and leisure area
  • 🎤 Team building events

All hiring happens through our careers site and official email. We do not text or ask for payment during the hiring process. Please report any suspicious messages immediately.

We believe that we are better together, and we welcome you for who you are. We endeavor to ensure that everyone - regardless of ability, age, socio-economic & cultural background, ethnicity, faith, gender, gender expression, gender identity, ideology, national origin, race, sexual orientation, marital status, or any characteristic protected under applicable law - has the opportunity to reach their full potential. At TheFork, we want you to bring us your unique perspectives and experiences, so we can collectively continue disrupting the restaurant industry and go from good to great.

TheFork is committed to a fair recruitment process. If you have special needs and require reasonable support during your application, interview, or participation in the selection process due to health conditions or disability, please direct your inquiries to AccessibleRecruitment@TheFork.com. Our HR team will review the request and respond accordingly.
job.title: Key Account Manager Top Chef (Michelin Restaurants)

job.company_description:
We are a technology company that powers the global travel industry. By leveraging next-generation technology, we create global technology solutions that take on the biggest opportunities and solve the most complex challenges in travel.
Every day, millions of consumers and employees interact with our technology worldwide. We power mobile apps, airport check-in kiosks, online travel sites, airline and hotel reservation networks, travel agent terminals, and scores of other solutions.
Positioned at the center of travel, we shape the future by offering innovative advancements that pave the way for a more connected and seamless ecosystem – empowering both clients and travelers with greater control and convenience.
Want to learn more? Visit www.sabre.com
job.job_description:
The Account Manager EMEA will roll up his or her sleeves and commit to serving the needs of our hospitality customers around the region. We are looking for a dedicated, innovative team member who is curious, can produce excellent work and take enthusiastic ownership of results. This is an exciting opportunity to join us and be a part of the journey we are on.

Team Description

The sales and account management team is a highly demanding team, driven by their eagerness to increase SOW, operates fast and efficiently in order to meet targets and deliver great service to our customers.

This role will involve managing a varied portfolio of hotels and chains, you will be responsible for setting the vision and strategy for each account, identifying opportunities to deepen partnerships with our clients. Together, we will focus on deploying superior account planning and a consultative sales approach to maintain a high level of service and increase penetration in existing accounts.

Role And Responsibilities

  • Serve as a strong advocate for clients within Aven, effectively navigating the complexities of a large, virtual organization to identify and collaborate with the right stakeholders, ensuring timely execution on behalf of the client
  • Evolve annual territory sales strategy and execution; perform ongoing market landscape analysis and market research to identify top opportunities and risk
  • Implement and maintain effective sales management practices to deliver accurate and up-to-date revenue projections and pipeline forecasts, supporting business growth
  • Extensive understanding of market landscape, including knowledge of key players, knowledge of the competitive landscape, key trends, opportunities, and challenges
  • Proven experience selling and driving negotiations to a successful close
  • Truly understand our customer needs and consult with them on the most effective solutions Aven can offer
  • Follows internal processes to ensure margins are positive and pricing is in-line with Aven strategy
  • Demonstrate a professional presence and business acumen, coupled with articulate and consultative oral and written communication skills.
  • Critical thinking skills with the ability to anticipate potential issues and suggest creative alternatives to overcome barriers
  • Ability to interpret analytics to highlight actionable data and tell a compelling story around performance and results
  • Ability to lead and direct multiple projects and initiatives simultaneously
  • Understand client overall performance and strategy to facilitate future budgeting of anticipated revenues:
  • Negotiate, develop, interpret and execute on client contract
  • Make decisions based on keeping the three main pillars of our team always in balance: client satisfaction, employee satisfaction and fiscal fortitude
  • Monitor customer performance and proactively provide clients with recommendations to optimize results based on their goals and objectives
  • Be savvy in creating presentations that tell a compelling story, be engaging and effective in communicating a strong, productive end in mind
  • Be comfortable with playing a wide range roles based on what the client needs from Aven including Project Manager, Researcher, Technical Expert, Innovator, Collaborator, Entrepreneur and Relationship Lead.
  • Hold both the client and Aven accountable for deliverables and decisions to keep projects and programs moving forward
  • Have a finger on the pulse of all major client/Aven activities and initiatives and find new opportunities with the client
  • Attend industry functions and lead in person meetings with clients; travel in line with business requirements
  • Log and manage all opportunities via Salesforce for accurate forecasting; utilize available tools and resources to be self sufficient

Roles Requirements

  • 2+ years of experience in Hospitality is essential, ideally in Revenue Management or hotel technology
  • Degree in hospitality/business or equivalent and desire to grow within the technology and hospitality sector.
  • Italian language fluency is essential
  • Excellent ability to collaborate and work effectively in a team environment, including both regional teams and global teams. Ability to forge solid internal relationships.
  • Experience managing multiple projects.
  • Self-directed with a start-up mentality.  Resourceful, innovative, and adaptable. Comfortable in a rapidly evolving environment, and skilled in change management
  • Advanced Excel, (inc. Pivots, VLOOKUP & Conditional Formatting)
  • Advanced Word, and PowerPoint
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Fluency in English and Italian is a must.
  • Presentation skills – translating numbers & trends for wider audience.

Personal Skills

  • Unafraid to challenge others, to hold themselves and others to account
  • Set and maintain high standards
  • Analytical mindset, excellent data skills
  • Detail driven
  • Ability to work under pressure and at a high pace, empowered to work on their own initiative
  • Can tell a compelling story and have great presentation skills
  • Good prioritization & time management skills
  • An ability to work cross-functionally and influence decision making
  • Take personal responsibility to meet commitments and actively seeks additional responsibility
  • Team player with exceptional communication skills
  • Desire to grow and evolve
job.title: Account Manager (Italian Speaker) - Aven Hospitality

job.company_description:
We are a technology company that powers the global travel industry. By leveraging next-generation technology, we create global technology solutions that take on the biggest opportunities and solve the most complex challenges in travel.
Every day, millions of consumers and employees interact with our technology worldwide. We power mobile apps, airport check-in kiosks, online travel sites, airline and hotel reservation networks, travel agent terminals, and scores of other solutions.
Positioned at the center of travel, we shape the future by offering innovative advancements that pave the way for a more connected and seamless ecosystem – empowering both clients and travelers with greater control and convenience.
Want to learn more? Visit www.sabre.com
job.job_description:
We’re looking for a curious, energetic, and customer obsessed marketer to join our Commercial Marketing Team. Based in Richmond, London, you’ll be part of a truly global team with colleagues across The Americas, EMEA and APAC, working together to help our customers succeed and shape the future of travel.

In this role, you’ll recommend and deliver smart, insighted communications and campaigns that support commercial priorities in key markets around the world. You’ll partner closely with Commercial teams to execute account-based marketing, using Sabre’s brand tone of voice and visual guidelines to ensure our message is consistent and compelling.

You’ll juggle multiple stakeholders, projects and deadlines — and you’ll thrive here if you’re a strong communicator, great at self-prioritising, and comfortable navigating ambiguity with optimism and a solution-based mindset. If that sounds like you, we’d love to talk.

Role & Responsibilities

  • Develop a clear understanding of key customer segments and turn insight into actionable marketing intelligence.
  • Translate Sabre’s industry, category and product positioning into customer centric value propositions that lead with customer needs.
  • Build and execute account-based strategies with clear marketing objectives and measurable commercial outcomes.
  • Produce compelling customer presentations, value stories and content that shows real business impact.
  • Champion customer success by embedding authentic customer voices, outcomes and proof points in everything you create.
  • Create and curate multichannel content—digital, events, email, web and more—to support account plans.
  • Measure, analyse and report on marketing performance, demonstrating how your work accelerates engagement, sales velocity and pipeline outcomes.

What’s In It For You

  • A marketing role with real variety—from content creation to campaign execution to delivering customer experiences.
  • The chance to collaborate with our Brand and Events Marketing teams to build campaigns that drive meaningful business results.
  • A front row seat during an exciting transformation for Sabre and the travel industry. You’ll help articulate and amplify Sabre’s vision, technology and customer value in bold, impactful ways.

Qualifications & Requirements

Must have skills

  • 3+ years of campaign marketing experience.
  • Experience delivering B2B campaigns with strong project management discipline.
  • Ability to collaborate effectively across multiple teams and regions.
  • Strong copywriting skills across different channels and formats.
  • Understanding of digital marketing techniques, including content marketing and automation.
  • Detail oriented, creative, and grounded in modern B2B marketing best practices.
  • Strong understanding of campaign measurement and performance tracking
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to partner with Sales and Marketing teams.
  • A proactive self-starter comfortable working independently or within virtual, cross functional teams.

Nice to have

  • Experience in enterprise or SaaS marketing environments.
  • Experience operating in a matrixed global organization.
  • Familiarity with marketing automation tools (e.g., Pardot) and CRM platforms (e.g., Salesforce).
  • Experience briefing and managing agencies or creative freelancers.
  • Ability to think strategically and adapt content to regional market needs.

Benefits

  • Highly competitive compensation package
  • We offer a comprehensive medical, dental and wellness program
  • 25 vacation days annually plus an extra week of vacation from December 27 to 31 every year as year end break globally
  • Formal and informal reward, recognition and acknowledgement programs
  • Lots of fun in a globally set team with a global onboarding program
  • Local events and celebrations
  • 4 volunteering days annually, to use with your charity of choice
  • High-end IT equipment to support you from day one
  • Ample employee development events, incl. learning platforms / tools
  • Diversity and Inclusion programs worldwide
job.title: Commercial Marketing Specialist

job.company_description:
Lighthouse is the leading commercial platform for the travel & hospitality industry. We transform complexity into confidence by providing actionable market insights, business intelligence, and pricing tools that maximize revenue growth.
Trusted by over 70,000 hotels in 185 countries, and supported by 700+ employees, Lighthouse is the only solution that provides real-time hotel and short-term rental data in a single platform. We strive to deliver the best possible experience with unmatched customer service. We consider our clients as true partners — their success is our success.
job.job_description:
At Lighthouse, we’re on a mission to disrupt commercial strategy for the hospitality industry. Our innovative commercial platform takes the complexity out of data, empowering businesses with actionable insights, advanced pricing tools, and cutting-edge business intelligence to unlock their full revenue potential.

Backed by $370 million in series C funding and driven by an unwavering passion for growth, we’ve welcomed five companies into our journey and have surpassed $100 million in ARR in 2024. Our 850+ teammates span 35 countries and represent 34 nationalities.

At Lighthouse, we’re more than just a workplace – we’re a community. Collaborative, fun, and deeply committed, we work hard together to revolutionize the hospitality sector. Are you ready to join us and shine brighter in the industry’s most exciting rocket-ship? 🚀

What You Will Do

We are expanding rapidly and are looking for full-time Sales Development Representatives - (Balkans) based in Barcelona, Spain. Reporting to the SDR Team Leader, the Sales Development Representative (SDR) primary responsibility will be to drive growth through qualifying leads for the Business Development Managers.

As an SDR, you will be the first impression between key hotel brands and Lighthouse. You will have the business-critical role of spreading awareness of Lighthouse products across the industry and getting a foot in the door with prospective clients, ultimately building our sales pipeline. You will also have the opportunity to help Lighthouse refine and expand upon our Sales Development function. We are looking for creative go-getters to join us during this exciting time!

Where you will have impact

  • Conduct research on key contacts in the hospitality industry, specifically hotel chains and their management companies.
  • Use news, industry trends, and company updates to put together well-crafted messaging for key contacts.
  • Engage with inbound leads to effectively schedule product demonstrations.
  • Make outbound calls, send LinkedIn messages, and craft highly personalized emails to prospective clients. This role will require maintaining a high volume of activities while maintaining the highest quality of outreach.
  • Proactively seek new business opportunities for Lighthouse.
  • Identify the needs of prospective clients, and qualify both inbound and outbound leads.
  • Get prospects excited to meet with Account Executives, as well as executing a strong handoff to the sales team.
  • Work closely with the marketing team to generate leads and follow up with prospective clients after trade shows, webinars, and other events and campaigns.
  • Prepare and manage administrative tasks, including invoice preparation using CRM tools like HubSpot and Salesforce.
  • Work cross-functionally with Regional Sales Managers, Account Executives, and the marketing team to optimize Lighthouse’s sales process and win rate.

About Our Team

Join our dynamic Revenue team of over 130 sales professionals globally, represented in 24 countries, who are passionate about driving revenue and expanding our market share in the hospitality industry. As a large department with various roles, our team offers many opportunities for growth and career development. Our team works closely with customers, (including hotels, chains, STR and more) to help them achieve their revenue goals. As a member of our team, you'll have the opportunity to work with a dynamic group of professionals, learn from experienced leaders, and contribute to the success of our organization.

What's in it for you?

  • Flexible working environment: Work from home or at one of our global offices or Co-working spaces.
  • Flexible time off: Autonomy to manage your work-life balance.
  • Collaborative team: High-bar, friendly, creative, and passionate colleagues.
  • Career development: Workshops, frameworks, tools, training, and processes to realize your full potential.
  • Impactful work: Shape products relied on by 85,000+ users worldwide.
  • Data culture growth: Opportunity to evolve data culture in a fast-growing scale-up.
  • Competitive compensation: Proactively maintained to value your work.
  • Referral bonuses: Earn rewards for bringing in new talent.

Who You Are

  • High energy with a passion for building relationships and connecting with people.
  • Professional communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Highly organized, with a strong attention to detail.
  • A desire to pursue a career in sales.
  • Strong prioritization skills with a demonstrated ability to effectively manage multiple activities simultaneously.
  • Self-motivated team player with a proven ability to work both as an individual and as part of a team.
  • Demonstrated coach-ability and a desire for continuous improvement.
  • Fluent in Croatian or Polish.
  • A Bachelor's degree in communication, business management, or equivalent 1-2 years experience in an outbound sales position.

Thank you for considering a career with Lighthouse. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace that values equal opportunity for all. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds, regardless of age, gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, and disability. We actively encourage applications from individuals with disabilities and are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process and during employment to ensure all qualified candidates can participate fully. Our commitment to equality is not just a policy; it's part of our culture.

If you share our passion for innovation and teamwork, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of the hospitality industry. At Lighthouse, our guiding light is to be an equal opportunity employer, and we encourage individuals from all walks of life to apply. Not ticking every box? No problem! We value diverse backgrounds and unique skill sets. If your experience looks a little different from what we've described, but you're passionate about what we do and are a quick learner, we'd love to hear from you.

We value the unique perspective and talents that you bring, and we're excited to see how your light can shine within our team. We can't wait to meet you and explore how we can grow and succeed together, illuminating the path towards a brighter future for the industry.
job.title: Sales Development Representative, Balkans

job.company_description:
SAP is the leading enterprise application and business AI company. We stand at the intersection of business and technology, where our innovations are designed to directly address real business challenges and produce real-world impacts.
Our solutions are the backbone for the world’s most complex and demanding processes. SAP’s integrated portfolio unites the elements of modern organizations — from workforce and financials to customers and supply chains — into a unified ecosystem that drives progress.
SAP privacy statement for followers: www.sap.com/sps
Our Community Guidelines At SAP, we're committed to fostering meaningful conversations that respect everyone in our community. To maintain a positive environment, we moderate comments that: • Target individuals personally, including our employees, customers, or partners • Contain discriminatory, harassing, or threatening language/content • Share personal information without consent • Promote misinformation or spam or 3rd-party links
We believe in open dialogue and constructive feedback, but we will remove content that violates these guidelines without notice. We appreciate your understanding and contribution to a respectful community.
For questions about our moderation practices, please DM or contact us at brandsocial@sap.com.
job.job_description:
Nous aidons le monde à mieux fonctionner

Chez SAP, nous faisons simple : vous apportez le meilleur de vous-même, et nous ferons ressortir ce que vous avez de meilleur. Nous sommes des bâtisseurs qui touchent plus de 20 secteurs et 80 % du commerce mondial, et nous avons besoin de vos talents uniques pour façonner l’avenir. Le travail est exigeant – mais il a du sens.

Vous trouverez ici un endroit où vous pouvez être vous-même, où votre bien-être est une priorité, et où vous aurez réellement votre place.

Qu’est-ce que cela vous apporte ? Un apprentissage constant, le développement de vos compétences, d’excellents avantages, et une équipe qui veut vous voir évoluer et réussir.

Votre Mission

À propos du poste

Le BTM (Business transformation management) MU (market unit) Demand Manager pilote la mise en œuvre locale du Plan de Demande Régional BTM. Ce poste exige une personne organisée, proactive et dotée d’un excellent esprit collaboratif, capable d’influencer les parties prenantes et d’assurer l’exécution optimale des initiatives de génération de demande à travers les cinq sources de demande : Ventes, Marketing, Digital Hub Sales Development Executives, Partenaires, Customer Success Manager & Services.

Responsabilités Principales

  • Santé et Gouvernance du Pipeline
  • Suivre et contrôler la création de nouveau pipeline selon les différents axes de vente et sources de demande
  • Garantir une couverture de pipeline suffisante pour atteindre les objectifs de vente
  • Agir en tant que garant de la qualité du pipeline
  • Analyser les mouvements du pipeline et identifier les risques en amont afin de proposer des plans de remédiation
  • Planification et Exécution de la Demande
  • Coordonner localement les activités de génération de demande et de progression pipeline en phase amont
  • Piloter l’exécution du plan de demande BTM sur l’ensemble des sources de demande
  • Revoir et ajuster le plan de demande tout au long de l’année en fonction des besoins business
  • Alignement et Amélioration Continue
  • Garantir l’exécution des programmes de demande régionaux et globaux
  • Partager et promouvoir les meilleures pratiques au sein de l’organisation
  • Collaborer étroitement avec le/la SAP MU Demand Manager et les autres Demand Managers locaux afin d’identifier des opportunités de croissance et de soutenir l’amélioration continue des processus de demande

Votre Profil

Vous êtes autonome, bien organisé(e) et enthousiaste avec un état d’esprit positif. Vous assumez la responsabilité de votre travail, attachez de l’importance aux détails et livrez systématiquement des résultats de haute qualité dans les délais impartis. Vous aimez travailler sur des projets innovants et porteurs de sens, traitez les informations confidentielles de manière responsable et respectez les politiques et normes de l'entreprise.

Qualifications

  • Capacité à influencer et à collaborer avec de multiples parties prenantes
  • Communicateur(trice) efficace, capable de transmettre des messages, une vision et des orientations clairs
  • Solides compétences en prise de décision, résolution de problèmes et exécution
  • Expérience en planification de programme ou dans l’évaluation d’opportunités de développement commercial
  • Expérience de travail dans un environnement multiculturel
  • Maîtrise avancée d’Excel
  • La connaissance du portefeuille et de l’écosystème SAP est un atout majeur
  • Une compréhension du Cloud, du SaaS/PaaS et des modèles commerciaux digitaux serait un plus
  • Maîtrise de l’anglais et du français est requise (niveau B2/C1)

Votre Équipe

Ce poste à durée déterminée est principalement axé sur la gestion de la Demande au sein de l’équipe France BTM. Vous serez rattaché(e) fonctionnellement au BTM Regional Demand Manager ainsi qu’au BTM MU Sales Leader afin d’assurer une parfaite cohérence avec la vision régionale et locale pour une exécution ciblée et efficace. Vous collaborerez également avec l’ensemble de l’équipe commerciale BTM ainsi qu’avec les responsables Marketing, Partenaires, Business Development, Customer Success et Services. Les solutions BTM sont au cœur de la transformation organisationnelle de nos clients et leur permettent de générer une réelle valeur ajoutée pour leur entreprise.

Durée : 6 mois, renouvelable

Date de début : mars 2026 (Q1)

Rémunération en alignement avec la convention collective applicable (Convention SYNTEC) et la grille de salaires SAP.

Faites ressortir le meilleur de vous-même

Les innovations de SAP permettent à plus de quatre cent mille clients dans le monde de travailler ensemble plus efficacement et d'utiliser plus efficacement les informations commerciales. Connu à l'origine pour son leadership en matière de logiciels de planification des ressources d'entreprise (ERP), SAP a évolué pour devenir un leader du marché des logiciels d'application de gestion de bout en bout et des services connexes pour les bases de données, l'analytique, les technologies intelligentes et la gestion de l'expérience. En tant que société en nuage comptant deux-cents millions d'utilisateurs et plus de cent mille employés dans le monde, nous sommes motivés et tournés vers l'avenir, avec une éthique d'équipe hautement collaborative et un engagement envers le développement personnel. Qu'il s'agisse de connecter des industries, des personnes ou des plates-formes mondiales, nous veillons à ce que chaque défi reçoive la solution qu'il mérite. Chez SAP, vous pouvez faire ressortir le meilleur de vous-même.

Nous gagnons grâce à l'inclusion

job.title: CDD: Responsable Demand Management - BTM France F/H

job.company_description:
Lighthouse is the leading commercial platform for the travel & hospitality industry. We transform complexity into confidence by providing actionable market insights, business intelligence, and pricing tools that maximize revenue growth.
Trusted by over 70,000 hotels in 185 countries, and supported by 700+ employees, Lighthouse is the only solution that provides real-time hotel and short-term rental data in a single platform. We strive to deliver the best possible experience with unmatched customer service. We consider our clients as true partners — their success is our success.
job.job_description:
At Lighthouse, we’re on a mission to disrupt commercial strategy for the hospitality industry. Our innovative commercial platform takes the complexity out of data, empowering businesses with actionable insights, advanced pricing tools, and cutting-edge business intelligence to unlock their full revenue potential.

Backed by $370 million in series C funding and driven by an unwavering passion for growth, we’ve welcomed five companies into our journey and have surpassed $100 million in ARR in 2024. Our 850+ teammates span 35 countries and represent 34 nationalities.

At Lighthouse, we’re more than just a workplace – we’re a community. Collaborative, fun, and deeply committed, we work hard together to revolutionize the hospitality sector. Are you ready to join us and shine brighter in the industry’s most exciting rocket-ship? 🚀

What You Will Do

As a Manager, Sales Operations, you will be leading our global team of dedicated tactical specialists and analysts responsible for the data, systems and insights that empower our sales organization and you will be reporting to our Director, Sales Operations. You will be a key player in our commercial success, providing support and strategic guidance to our sales teams. This role requires a leader who can manage day-to-day execution, delegate effectively, and help the team prioritize in a fast-paced environment. Your expertise will be important in shaping sales strategies and capitalizing on revenue opportunities across the global business.

Where you will have impact

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 5 (and growing) sales operations professionals in a supportive environment.
  • Evaluate our current structures, and implement the changes necessary to create a unified, scalable tactical sales operations team
  • Execute the sales operations strategy by identifying process enhancements and translating data analysis into actionable business recommendations to the Revenue Team.
  • Oversee the development and maintenance of key reports and dashboards for sales leadership, including forecasting, pipeline management, and quota attainment.
  • Partner with Sales Enablement to manage and enforce global sales processes, ensuring adherence and a smooth roll-out of new procedures.
  • Act as a key point of contact for the sales team, providing support on the Salesforce lead-to-quote-to-cash process and coordinating with cross-functional teams.
  • Manage stakeholder expectations by effectively prioritizing tasks and communicating progress on key initiatives to senior leadership.
  • You will help define the 'Lighthouse way' of working by integrating AI into your role—whether it’s using AI to prioritize your sales pipeline, automate routine financial reconciliations, or extract faster insights from our commercial data to drive business growth.

About Our Team

You'll be joining our Sales Operations team, reporting to the Director, Sales Operations within the wider Business Operations department. We are a collaborative and essential crew that acts as the operational backbone for our global sales organization. You'll work closely with other teams and senior leadership, providing the insights and support that keep our commercial teams running.

Who you are

Essential

  • Proven experience in a sales operations or revenue operations role.
  • Experience working within a complex sales organization with multiple roles (e.g., SDRs, AEs, AMs).
  • Experience in a high-growth Tech or SaaS company, particularly one that has undergone M&A activity.
  • Demonstrated team management experience, with a passion for leading and developing high-performing individuals.
  • Mandatory, in-depth experience with Salesforce, including reporting and dashboard creation.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data and analysis into actionable business insights.
  • Excellent organizational and time-management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, with a collaborative mindset to work effectively across teams.
  • Fluent in both written and spoken English.
  • You are an AI-curious professional who actively explores how generative AI and automation can optimize your daily workflows. You don't just follow processes; you look for ways to augment them using the latest tech tools to increase your personal impact.

We welcome

  • Familiarity with Looker or other data visualization tools.

What's in it for you

  • Flexible working environment: Work from home or at one of our global offices.
  • Flexible time off: Autonomy to manage your work-life balance.
  • Collaborative team: High-bar, friendly, creative, and passionate colleagues.
  • Career development: Workshops, frameworks, tools, training, and processes to realize your full potential.
  • Impactful work: Shape products relied on by 85,000+ users worldwide.
  • Mobility options: Mobility budget or company car (based on your job category).
  • Competitive compensation: Proactively maintained to value your work.
  • Net allowance: to support home office related expenses.
  • Vouchers: lunch vouchers & eco vouchers.
  • Comprehensive health insurance: Extensive coverage for you and your dependents.
  • Pension funding: Group insurance to secure your future.
  • Referral bonuses: Earn rewards for bringing in new talent.

Thank you for considering a career with Lighthouse. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace that values equal opportunity for all. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds, regardless of age, gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, and disability. We actively encourage applications from individuals with disabilities and are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process and during employment to ensure all qualified candidates can participate fully. Our commitment to equality is not just a policy; it's part of our culture.

If you share our passion for innovation and teamwork, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of the hospitality industry. At Lighthouse, our guiding light is to be an equal opportunity employer, and we encourage individuals from all walks of life to apply. Not ticking every box? No problem! We value diverse backgrounds and unique skill sets. If your experience looks a little different from what we've described, but you're passionate about what we do and are a quick learner, we'd love to hear from you.

We value the unique perspective and talents that you bring, and we're excited to see how your light can shine within our team. We can't wait to meet you and explore how we can grow and succeed together, illuminating the path towards a brighter future for the industry.

Our Domain Advisor is the domain/industry go-to expert on Supply Chain Planning owning the go-to-market strategy and execution for a given (set of) industrie(s) based on deep knowledge on the Supply Chain domain, relevant industry trends and competitive landscape. The domain advisor spends the majority of time on strategic pursuits and develops best practice assets, processes, demand initatives and sales plays. The domain advisor is primarily reponsible for pipeline growth, market development, strategic pursuits and ensuring cross-functional alignment on the growth plan with adjacent SAP teams and partners.

Accountability

  • Responsible for growth in the Supply Chain Planning domain
  • Sales strategy on strategic pursuits
  • Coaches sales teams
  • Oversee the creation of best practise assets (POV, demo, discovery process, etc)
  • Ensures delivery of assets across a cross-functional team
  • Ensures business critical & innovative results
  • Ensures that goals and milestones are met and approved budgets are managed appropriately
  • Manages escalation appropriately
  • Builds strategic partnerships with key decision makers in customer & partner organization

Complexity

  • Takes ownership over the growth of the Supply Chain Planning domain in a matrix organisation
  • Oversees the most complex, strategically important pursuits
  • Owns executive client relationships

Experience

  • 10-15 years of relevant professional experience in the Supply Chain Planning domain/industry
  • Executive presence in commercial organisation
  • Managemenet consulting, value based selling

Communication

  • Represents SAP to customers
  • Finds common ground for cooperation within matrix organistaion and cross-functional teams
  • Formulates clear GTM plans for growth
  • Communicates messages relevant for growth plan
  • Captures opinions and expectations of stakeholder and takes action

Key Responsibilities & Tasks

The Domain Advisor Expert has an in-depth understanding of the Supply Chain Planning domain, has deep industry knowledge and experience making him/her a trusted advisor for buying centers and CSCO/COO. Domain Expert takes ownership of key activities during the 'awareness and (re)consider' phases of the customer lifecycle. Responsible for demand generation, solution/LoB pipeline build strategies and development of scale initiatives resulting in increased solutions/LoB (Line of Business) pipeline, revenue and an elevated SAP leadership position in the solution/LoB segment. Executes solution/LoB segment functional business plans integrating functional and financial objectives. In addition, takes ownership on strategic pursuits alongside the sales team the 'evaluate and select' phases of the lifecycle, extending the expertise of the team.

Tasks: Provide standardized approaches for value-based sales activities for focus solutions/LoB. Enable the field and support roll-out of new field training; conduct in class training for specific solutions/ Lobs and contribute to e-learning content; coach and mentor internal customers on our products and solutions and how to position them with our clients. Provide on demand access for solution/LoB specific expertise; engage with customers on strategic pursuits.

job.job_description:
Nous aidons le monde à mieux fonctionner

Chez SAP, nous faisons simple : vous apportez le meilleur de vous-même, et nous ferons ressortir ce que vous avez de meilleur. Nous sommes des bâtisseurs qui touchent plus de 20 secteurs et 80 % du commerce mondial, et nous avons besoin de vos talents uniques pour façonner l’avenir. Le travail est exigeant – mais il a du sens.

Vous trouverez ici un endroit où vous pouvez être vous-même, où votre bien-être est une priorité, et où vous aurez réellement votre place.

Qu’est-ce que cela vous apporte ? Un apprentissage constant, le développement de vos compétences, d’excellents avantages, et une équipe qui veut vous voir évoluer et réussir.

Votre Mission

En tant que stagiaire au sein de l'organisation commerciale chez SAP, vous serez amené(e) à

Support Commercial

  • Assister les équipes commerciales dans la gestion des comptes clients.
  • Participer à l'élaboration des offres commerciales en collaboration avec les responsables des ventes.
  • Assurer la prise de commande des produits On Premise notamment les upsells sur les produits BI Business Objects.

Analyse De Marché

  • Contribuer à l'analyse du marché et à la veille concurrentielle.
  • Aider à l'identification des opportunités d'affaires et des tendances du secteur.

Gestion De La Relation Client

  • Participer à la gestion de la relation client en assurant un suivi régulier et en recueillant les retours clients.
  • Gérer la mise à jour de la base client et prospect dans le système SAP Harmony

Relation Partenaires

  • Gérer la mise en relation commerciale des partenaires BI On Premise
  • Assurer le suivi des paiements des partenaires pour débloquer les comptes et ainsi permettre les nouvelles commandes.
  • Traiter la gestion de la facturation des contrats partenaires

Formation Et Développement

  • Suivre les formations internes proposées par SAP pour développer vos compétences en vente et en relation client.
  • Participer à l’organisation d’évènements SAP et partenaires : salons...

Votre Profil

  • Étudiant(e) en cours de formation.
  • Maîtrise courante du français et de l'anglais, tant à l'oral qu'à l'écrit.
  • Intérêt marqué pour le domaine commercial et la relation client.
  • Bonne compréhension des solutions logicielles et technologiques, de préférence dans le secteur des technologies de l'information.
  • Excellentes compétences en communication et capacité à travailler en équipe.
  • Dynamique, autonome, et motivé(e) par l'apprentissage continu.
  • Esprit d'analyse et de synthèse.
  • Capacité à s'adapter rapidement à un environnement professionnel exigeant.
  • Maîtrise des outils informatiques et des logiciels de gestion.

Durée du stage : 6 mois

Date de début souhaitée: mars 2026

Votre Équipe

Vous ferez partie de l'équipe Affaires Générales à Levallois, cette équipe est dédiée aux entreprises de taille intermédiaire (moins d'1 milliard de chiffre d'affaires pour les clients existants) et aux comptes Net New Name (clients non SAP ERP), Elle travaille aussi en étroite collaboration avec un écosystème de Partenaires (représentant 80% de notre activité).

De plus, cette équipe travaille avec une équipe élargie de partenaires de distribution, d'avant-vente, d'experts et d'organisations industrielles (tous dédiés à différents produits dans le cadre de l'ERP)

Notre marché est également intégré à l'équipe de vente Digitale basée à Barcelone. Les entreprises de taille intermédiaire se répartissent entre grands comptes à Levallois et volume business à Barcelone. Tous sont sous la responsabilité du Head of Général Business à Levallois-Perret.

Conformément à la législation française, pour toute durée supérieure à 2 mois, ce stage fait l'objet d'une gratification, en alignement avec la grille définie par SAP.

job.title: General Business Sales Expert - F/H

job.company_description:
Lighthouse is the leading commercial platform for the travel & hospitality industry. We transform complexity into confidence by providing actionable market insights, business intelligence, and pricing tools that maximize revenue growth.
Trusted by over 70,000 hotels in 185 countries, and supported by 700+ employees, Lighthouse is the only solution that provides real-time hotel and short-term rental data in a single platform. We strive to deliver the best possible experience with unmatched customer service. We consider our clients as true partners — their success is our success.
job.job_description:
At Lighthouse, we’re on a mission to disrupt commercial strategy for the hospitality industry. Our innovative commercial platform takes the complexity out of data, empowering businesses with actionable insights, advanced pricing tools, and cutting-edge business intelligence to unlock their full revenue potential.

Backed by $370 million in series C funding and driven by an unwavering passion for growth, we’ve welcomed five companies into our journey and have surpassed $100 million in ARR in 2024. Our 850+ teammates span 35 countries and represent 34 nationalities.

At Lighthouse, we’re more than just a workplace – we’re a community. Collaborative, fun, and deeply committed, we work hard together to revolutionize the hospitality sector. Are you ready to join us and shine brighter in the industry’s most exciting rocket-ship? 🚀

What You Will Do

As a Manager, Sales Operations, you will be leading our global team of dedicated tactical specialists and analysts responsible for the data, systems and insights that empower our sales organization and you will be reporting to our Director, Sales Operations. You will be a key player in our commercial success, providing support and strategic guidance to our sales teams. This role requires a leader who can manage day-to-day execution, delegate effectively, and help the team prioritize in a fast-paced environment. Your expertise will be important in shaping sales strategies and capitalizing on revenue opportunities across the global business.

Where you will have impact

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 5 (and growing) sales operations professionals in a supportive environment.
  • Evaluate our current structures, and implement the changes necessary to create a unified, scalable tactical sales operations team
  • Execute the sales operations strategy by identifying process enhancements and translating data analysis into actionable business recommendations to the Revenue Team.
  • Oversee the development and maintenance of key reports and dashboards for sales leadership, including forecasting, pipeline management, and quota attainment.
  • Partner with Sales Enablement to manage and enforce global sales processes, ensuring adherence and a smooth roll-out of new procedures.
  • Act as a key point of contact for the sales team, providing support on the Salesforce lead-to-quote-to-cash process and coordinating with cross-functional teams.
  • Manage stakeholder expectations by effectively prioritizing tasks and communicating progress on key initiatives to senior leadership.
  • You will help define the 'Lighthouse way' of working by integrating AI into your role—whether it’s using AI to prioritize your sales pipeline, automate routine financial reconciliations, or extract faster insights from our commercial data to drive business growth.

About Our Team

You'll be joining our Sales Operations team, reporting to the Director, Sales Operations within the wider Business Operations department. We are a collaborative and essential crew that acts as the operational backbone for our global sales organization. You'll work closely with other teams and senior leadership, providing the insights and support that keep our commercial teams running.

Who you are

Essential

  • Proven experience in a sales operations or revenue operations role.
  • Experience working within a complex sales organization with multiple roles (e.g., SDRs, AEs, AMs).
  • Experience in a high-growth Tech or SaaS company, particularly one that has undergone M&A activity.
  • Demonstrated team management experience, with a passion for leading and developing high-performing individuals.
  • Mandatory, in-depth experience with Salesforce, including reporting and dashboard creation.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data and analysis into actionable business insights.
  • Excellent organizational and time-management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, with a collaborative mindset to work effectively across teams.
  • Fluent in both written and spoken English.
  • You are an AI-curious professional who actively explores how generative AI and automation can optimize your daily workflows. You don't just follow processes; you look for ways to augment them using the latest tech tools to increase your personal impact.

We welcome

  • Familiarity with Looker or other data visualization tools.

What's in it for you

  • Flexible working environment: Work from home or at one of our global offices.
  • Flexible time off: Autonomy to manage your work-life balance.
  • Alan Healthy benefits: 160€/month for food or nursery.
  • Flexible retribution: Optional benefits through tax-free payroll deductions for food, transportation and/or nursery.
  • Wellbeing support: Subsidized up to 80% ClassPass subscription.
  • Comprehensive health insurance: 100% Alan coverage for you, your spouse, and dependents.
  • Collaborative team: High-bar, friendly, creative, and passionate colleagues.
  • Career development: Workshops, frameworks, tools, training, and processes to realize your full potential.
  • Impactful work: Shape products relied on by 85,000+ users worldwide.
  • Competitive compensation: Proactively maintained to value your work.
  • Referral bonuses: Earn rewards for bringing in new talent.

Thank you for considering a career with Lighthouse. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace that values equal opportunity for all. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds, regardless of age, gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, and disability. We actively encourage applications from individuals with disabilities and are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process and during employment to ensure all qualified candidates can participate fully. Our commitment to equality is not just a policy; it's part of our culture.

If you share our passion for innovation and teamwork, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of the hospitality industry. At Lighthouse, our guiding light is to be an equal opportunity employer, and we encourage individuals from all walks of life to apply. Not ticking every box? No problem! We value diverse backgrounds and unique skill sets. If your experience looks a little different from what we've described, but you're passionate about what we do and are a quick learner, we'd love to hear from you.

We value the unique perspective and talents that you bring, and we're excited to see how your light can shine within our team. We can't wait to meet you and explore how we can grow and succeed together, illuminating the path towards a brighter future for the industry.
job.title: Manager, Sales Operations
