شركة ماريوت الدولية هي شركة توفر فرص توظيف متكافئة. نؤمن بتوظيف قوى عاملة متنوعة الخلفيات والحفاظ على ثقافة شاملة تهتم بالأشخاص في المقام الأول. نلتزم بعدم التمييز على أي أساس محمي، مثل الإعاقة وحالة المحاربين القدماء، أو أي أساس آخر يغطيه القانون المعمول

تسعى فنادق ماريوت جاهدة للارتقاء بفن الضيافة والابتكار في كل فرصة مع الحفاظ على راحة الأشخاص المألوفة في جميع أنحاء العالم. بصفتك مضيفًا في فنادق ماريوت، ستساعد في الحفاظ على وعد "الضيافة الرائعة". "دائمًا". من خلال تقديم خدمة مدروسة وصادقة وذات تفكير مستقبلي تدعم هذا الإرث الحي وتبني عليه. مع الاسم المرادف للضيافة في جميع أنحاء العالم، نحن فخورون بالترحيب بك لاستكشاف مهنة مع فنادق ماريوت. عند الانضمام إلى فنادق ماريوت، فإنك تنضم إلى مجموعة من العلامات التجارية مع ماريوت الدولية. كن في المكان الذي يمكنك فيه القيام بأفضل أعمالك، وابدأ هدفك ، وانتمي إلى فريق عالمي مذهل ، لتصبح أفضل نسخة منك.

تعد جي دبليو ماريوت جزءًا من مجموعة ماريوت الدولية الفاخرة وتتكون من أكثر من 100 فندق رائع في مدن مناسبة للإجازات ومواقع المنتجعات المميزة حول العالم. تؤمن جي دبليو بأن شركائنا يأتون أولاً. لأنه إذا كنت سعيدًا، فسيكون ضيوفنا سعداء. يتمتع شركاء جي دبليو ماريوت بالثقة والابتكار والأصالة والبديهية، ويستمرون في إرث العلامة التجارية التي تحمل الاسم نفسه ومؤسس الشركة، جي ويلارد ماريوت.تقدم فنادقنا تجربة عمل لا مثيل لها، حيث ستكون جزءًا من المجتمع وتستمتع بصداقة حميمة حقيقية مع مجموعة متنوعة من زملاء العمل. تخلق جي دبليو فرصًا للتدريب والتطوير والتقدير والأهم من ذلك، مكانًا يمكنك من خلاله متابعة شغفك في بيئة فاخرة مع التركيز على الرفاهية الشاملة. تبدأ معاملة الضيوف بشكل استثنائي بالطريقة التي نعتني بها بشركائنا. هذا هو علاج JW™. عند انضمامك إلى جي دبليو ماريوت، فإنك تنضم إلى مجموعة من العلامات التجارية مع ماريوت الدولية. كن في المكان الذي يمكنك فيه القيام بأفضل أعمالك، وابدأ هدفك ، وانتمي إلى فريق عالمي مذهل ، لتصبح أفضل نسخة منك.
job.title: Dir-Marketing-B

job.company_description:
Lighthouse is the leading commercial platform for the travel & hospitality industry. We transform complexity into confidence by providing actionable market insights, business intelligence, and pricing tools that maximize revenue growth.
Trusted by over 70,000 hotels in 185 countries, and supported by 700+ employees, Lighthouse is the only solution that provides real-time hotel and short-term rental data in a single platform. We strive to deliver the best possible experience with unmatched customer service. We consider our clients as true partners — their success is our success.
job.job_description:
At Lighthouse, we’re on a mission to disrupt commercial strategy for the hospitality industry. Our innovative commercial platform takes the complexity out of data, empowering businesses with actionable insights, advanced pricing tools, and cutting-edge business intelligence to unlock their full revenue potential.

Backed by $370 million in series C funding and driven by an unwavering passion for growth, we’ve welcomed five companies into our journey and have surpassed $100 million in ARR in 2024. Our 850+ teammates span 35 countries and represent 34 nationalities.

At Lighthouse, we’re more than just a workplace – we’re a community. Collaborative, fun, and deeply committed, we work hard together to revolutionize the hospitality sector. Are you ready to join us and shine brighter in the industry’s most exciting rocket-ship? 🚀

What You Will Do

At Lighthouse, the Regional Sales Manager - Business Intelligence takes action to build and inspire a team of Business Development Managers (Account Executives). They will partner closely with Sales Leadership to develop and execute winning sales strategies, set team wide goals, and work towards team and individual quota attainment. As we continue to grow our team, we are looking for a leader available for a hybrid in-office presence in our Madrid or Barcelona office.

Where you will have impact

As a Regional Sales Manager, you are primarily responsible for managing a team of about 7 reps who are able to consistently achieve quota.

  • Develop a best in class sales organisation and get the team of reps to maximize sales revenue and meet or exceed set target
  • Develop specific plans with the reps to ensure sales growth both long and short-term
  • Work closely with reps on deal execution and assist with the closing of key opportunities
  • Assist the team by demonstrating own market knowledge and product expertise on demos with partners or prospects and join various calls with external parties
  • Ensure that reps clearly understand the amount of funnel necessary to sustain success and work closely with reps on demand creation activities
  • Measure and manage key business KPIs and ensure that reps are able to clearly understand the definition of success
  • Run various team calls and other internal calls to ensure team alignment and clear expectations
  • Run weekly forecast calls that allow for an accurate monthly and quarterly sales forecast
  • In absence of Head of Sales for the region, liaise with the sales reps on specific pricing of customised deals by market
  • Identify missing or out-dated processes that need to be developed or redesigned, and create best best practices to improve sales process by working closely with Sales Operations to make it easier for reps to sell
  • Work closely with field marketing and corporate marketing to ensure a strong communication and collaboration between the two teams to maximize the performance of the lead funnel
  • Work in other lines of business (marketing, product, sales operations, customer success, service delivery) to ensure the smooth sales processes, long term success of our customers, maintaining a high retention rate

People Management

  • Work with our in-house talent acquisition manager to recruit, hire, and train new team members
  • Foster your team’s growth through development planning, performance management, collaborative work, and real-time feedback and coaching that promotes individual success and long term retention.
  • Focus on weekly one-on-ones that help sales reps develop in all areas of sales success and personal growth
  • Work closely with Sales Operations and Sales Enablement Manager to identify slow ramping or low performing reps and develop individual plans to improve performance
  • Be a culture carrier and understand and promote the values that have made Lighthouse a long term success
  • Be a motivator, ensure that reps have a clear understanding of our vision, mission and values and that they exhibit them as they work with prospects and customers
  • Be able to effectively and objectively implement performance improvement plans and ensure that reps have a clear understanding of their performance

About Our Team

Join our dynamic Revenue team of over 130 sales professionals globally, represented in 24 countries, who are passionate about driving revenue and expanding our market share in the hospitality industry. As a large department with various roles, our team offers many opportunities for growth and career development. Our team works closely with customers, (including hotels, chains, STR and more) to help them achieve their revenue goals. As a member of our team, you'll have the opportunity to work with a dynamic group of professionals, learn from experienced leaders, and contribute to the success of our organization.

With a mission to expand our market share in EMEA, we focus on both mature and growth markets, constantly seeking out new opportunities to expand our territory. Our team of 36 people is made up of talented and driven individuals from diverse backgrounds, all working together to drive revenue growth and achieve our goals.

Who You Are

  • 5+ years as a sales manager of at least 6 reps (AE/BDM)
  • Leadership skills that motivate reps to go the extra mile for you
  • Proven experience in working in international environment
  • Successful track record of achieving quota as a sales rep
  • Successful track record of target setting and helping a team of reps attain quota
  • Proven experience with SalesForce, or other CRM systems and related sales tools
  • Excellent analytical, problem solving and troubleshooting skills
  • Strong overall business and people skills, including planning, presentation skills, sales skills;
  • Excellent communication skills, including the ability to teach, coach, mentor and motivate individual reps and with others in the company
  • Selling to the hospitality and SaaS industry is a strong plus
  • You are fluent in English

What's in it for you?

  • Flexible working environment: Work from home or at one of our global offices.
  • Flexible time off: Autonomy to manage your work-life balance.
  • Collaborative team: High-bar, friendly, creative, and passionate colleagues.
  • Career development: Workshops, frameworks, tools, training, and processes to realize your full potential.
  • Impactful work: Shape products relied on by 75,000+ users worldwide.
  • Competitive compensation: Proactively maintained to value your work.
  • Referral bonuses: Earn rewards for bringing in new talent.

Thank you for considering a career with Lighthouse. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace that values equal opportunity for all. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds, regardless of age, gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, and disability. We actively encourage applications from individuals with disabilities and are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process and during employment to ensure all qualified candidates can participate fully. Our commitment to equality is not just a policy; it's part of our culture.

If you share our passion for innovation and teamwork, we invite you to join us in shaping the future of the hospitality industry. At Lighthouse, our guiding light is to be an equal opportunity employer, and we encourage individuals from all walks of life to apply. Not ticking every box? No problem! We value diverse backgrounds and unique skill sets. If your experience looks a little different from what we've described, but you're passionate about what we do and are a quick learner, we'd love to hear from you.

We value the unique perspective and talents that you bring, and we're excited to see how your light can shine within our team. We can't wait to meet you and explore how we can grow and succeed together, illuminating the path towards a brighter future for the industry.
job.title: Regional Sales Manager - Business Intelligence EMEA (Team Lead)

job.company_description:
OAG is a leading data platform for the global travel industry offering an industry-first single source for supply, demand, and pricing data. We empower the global travel industry with high-quality, relevant datasets covering the whole journey from planning to customer experience.
Headquartered in the UK, with operations in the USA, Denmark, France, Germany, Singapore, Japan, China, and Lithuania.
For more information visit www.oag.com
job.job_description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY:

OAG is a leading data platform for the global travel industry, offering an industry-first single source for supply, demand, and pricing data. 

We empower the global travel industry with high-quality, relevant datasets that cover the entire journey, from planning through customer experience.  

Headquartered in the UK, with operations in the USA, Denmark, France, Germany, Singapore, Japan, China, and Lithuania. For more information, visit

www.oag.com

ABOUT THE PROJECT/ROLE:

As a Demand Generation Executive at OAG, you’ll own the planning and execution of multi-channel campaigns that drive pipeline growth and customer acquisition across the global travel industry. This is a hands-on role with real ownership from campaign strategy through to optimisation and performance reporting — working closely with sales to turn insight into impact.

YOU WILL: 

  • Own and execute OAG’s demand generation activity, with a clear focus on lead quality, pipeline contribution, and revenue impact
  • Plan and deliver integrated campaigns across SEO, AEO, paid search, paid social, email, and organic social
  • Manage and optimise paid media spend, testing audiences, messaging, and formats to maximise ROI
  • Develop and optimise content and digital journeys to improve search visibility, conversion rates, and lead capture
  • Analyse campaign performance and funnel metrics, turning data into actionable insights
  • Partner closely with sales and marketing colleagues to align campaigns with commercial priorities

WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

  • Experience in demand generation, growth marketing, or digital marketing within a B2B environment (SaaS or technology preferred)
  • Hands-on experience running multi-channel campaigns
  • Strong understanding of SEO, AEO, SEM, and paid media
  • Experience with marketing automation and analytics tools (HubSpot, Google Analytics, etc.)
  • A data-driven mindset with the ability to turn insight into action

IF YOU ALSO HAVE THE BELOW THAT WOULD BE A BONUS:

  • Experience working closely with sales teams in a pipeline-driven environment
  • Familiarity with CRM systems and lead lifecycle management
  • Experience in international or multi-market campaigns

WE WILL OFFER YOU:

  • A dynamic work environment at OAG, fostering innovation in a progressive, non-hierarchical culture, where passionate tech enthusiasts continuously develop new products and solutions with a focus on improvement for the aviation industry.
  • Flexible working arrangements that allow remote work or office attendance.
  • An attractive compensation and benefits package, including private health insurance, a company bonus scheme, and voluntary participation in a company-supported retirement scheme.
  • A generous annual leave policy, growing with each year of service, and a day off during your birthday month.
  • Participation in team-building activities, team workshops, and group learning sessions.

OAG is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We ensure all applicants are considered for employment without discrimination.
job.title: Marketing Executive

job.company_description:
Travelport is a worldwide travel retail platform.
Our next-generation marketplace connects buyers and sellers that share our passion for delivering exceptional travel experiences.
Unconflicted and independent, we are reinventing a simpler future for travel’s complex ecosystem.
job.job_description:
Job Description

ACCOUNT MANAGER | EMEA AGENCY SALES | AUSTRIA

Travelport is a place of opportunity. It’s our incredible team that makes our company great – our people drive our winning culture.

What’s Travelport?

Travelport is a worldwide travel retail platform that provides easy ways for businesses like airlines, hotels, and car rental companies to connect through personalized content to agencies and travelers. The world is changing, and our industry must change with it. We’re leading the change; we are the change.

We are on a mission to power the future of travel; come join us.

What does a great Account Manager do?

A great Account Manager at Travelport is the trusted partner for our customers—someone who knows the Austrian travel landscape inside out and can turn that knowledge into real business impact. You’ll be the face of Travelport for a portfolio of mid-tier agencies and partners, building relationships that go beyond transactions. This isn’t a role where you wait for instructions; it’s about taking ownership, spotting opportunities, and driving growth with confidence and creativity.

You’ll translate complex technology into clear value for customers, helping them succeed in a fast-changing travel environment. Every day, you’ll combine commercial acumen with local market expertise to deliver solutions that matter. If you thrive on autonomy, love solving problems, and want to shape the future of travel in Austria, this is your chance to make a real difference.

Responsibilities include:

  • Own and manage a portfolio of mid-tier travel agency accounts in Austria, driving retention, growth, and profitability.
  • Develop and execute account plans that leverage deep knowledge of the Austrian travel market to deliver customer success and competitive advantage.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for customers, building strong relationships and proactively identifying new business opportunities.
  • Negotiate and renew contracts, ensuring alignment with Travelport’s commercial objectives and customer needs.
  • Operate as a self-starter—independently prioritizing, problem-solving, and mobilizing internal resources to deliver exceptional outcomes.


Who we are looking for in this role:

  • Deep understanding of the Austrian travel industry, including key players, market dynamics, and distribution trends.
  • Proven ability to work independently and take initiative—comfortable operating as a self-starter in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong commercial acumen with experience in account management, contract negotiation, and driving revenue growth.
  • Excellent relationship-building and communication skills, capable of influencing stakeholders at all levels.


Our ambition is to hire for potential not just experience, and you’re likely to succeed if you:

  • Are always ready to give it your all and deliver high quality work
  • Are excited to use your creativity and innovative thinking to try new things
  • Take responsibility and own your work
  • Are courageous and ambitious to challenge what’s been done before


These are just a few of our employee’s favourite benefits/perks…

  • Competitive salary and annual performance bonus.
  • Flexible working arrangements, including hybrid work options.
  • Private health insurance and wellness programs.
  • Generous vacation allowance plus local public holidays.
  • Pension plan and life insurance coverage.
  • Learning and development opportunities, including access to global training resources.


Interested? We’d love for you to get in touch!*

  • Please note that if your application is progressed, you may be asked to complete a self-recorded video interview, at a time that works best for you. This is your chance to bring your skills & experience to life in your own words and style. Ensure you keep an eye on your junk folder just in case our invitation finds its way there.


We are an equal opportunities employer and will consider all qualified applicants purely on their skills and abilities. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation, if needed
job.title: Account Manager I

job.company_description: WEX (NYSE: WEX) is the global commerce platform that simplifies the business of running a business. WEX has created a powerful ecosystem that offers seamlessly embedded, personalized solutions for its customers around the world. Through its rich data and specialized expertise in simplifying benefits, reimagining mobility and paying and getting paid, WEX aims to make it easy for companies to overcome complexity and reach their full potential. For more information, please visit www.wexinc.com.
job.job_description:
Senior Director, International Mobility Products/ Hybrid / London / Competitive Basic Salary Plus Annual Bonus and LTIP / Permanent

About The Team / Role

WEX is an innovative payments and technology company, committed to simplifying the business of doing business for our customers. Our International Mobility product lines focus on helping Fleets and Small Businesses manage cash flow, make payments, and make key business decisions seamlessly – so they can focus on what they love: their business.

As a key member of the International Mobility Senior Leadership team, you will partner with Commercial, Operational, and Technology teams to own and drive our International Mobility product strategy. You will create an integrated product vision, manage a team of product leaders and managers, and deliver innovative products that solve our customers’ most pressing pain points while driving high-value business outcomes.

What's on Offer?

  • Highly competitive salary
  • Annual company bonus
  • 40-hour week – Monday to Friday
  • Hybrid working
  • Industry-leading pension scheme
  • Private medical coverage
  • 25 days holiday plus bank holidays, with the opportunity to purchase additional holidays
  • Life assurance and income protection
  • Gym Flex membership
  • Discounts & perks platform
  • Employee wellbeing programs

How You'll Make An Impact

  • Develop and execute a bold vision and strategy for International Mobility products
  • Conduct market and customer research to identify trends, pain points, and opportunities
  • Drive measurable business outcomes and customer value through product innovation
  • Develop strong go-to-market strategies including pricing, packaging, marketing, and sales enablement
  • Lead and mentor a high-performing team of product managers, fostering a culture of inclusiveness, empowerment, and trust
  • Collaborate across WEX to ensure product success, shared capabilities, and financial optimization
  • Champion data-driven decision-making and customer-centric innovation

Experience You'll Bring

  • Significant experience in product management, including leading complex product portfolios and launching innovative payment or financial technology solutions.
  • Demonstrated product leadership experience, including building and developing high-performing teams.
  • Proven ability to communicate and influence executive stakeholders to drive strategic alignment
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience using quantitative and qualitative data to inform decisions
  • Deep customer-centric mindset with a track record of solving complex customer problems
  • Proven success in delivering high-value business outcomes through new product commercialization
  • Collaborative, inspirational leadership style with experience developing talent and high-performing teams
  • Experience building scalable, shared capabilities and driving cross-functional initiatives

Ready to lead at scale?

If you’re excited by the opportunity to define product strategy within a fast-moving, global fintech, apply today for immediate consideration.

We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified individuals regardless of race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or age. Due to high application volumes, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted within one week. We do not accept speculative agency CVs; PSL agencies should only submit CVs if authorized by the Talent Acquisition team.
job.title: Senior Director, International Mobility Product

job.company_description: WEX (NYSE: WEX) is the global commerce platform that simplifies the business of running a business. WEX has created a powerful ecosystem that offers seamlessly embedded, personalized solutions for its customers around the world. Through its rich data and specialized expertise in simplifying benefits, reimagining mobility and paying and getting paid, WEX aims to make it easy for companies to overcome complexity and reach their full potential. For more information, please visit www.wexinc.com.
job.job_description:
SALES MANAGER/ LONDON OR MANCHESTER / HYBRID/ £COMPETITIVE PLUS UNCAPPED COMMISSION AND BENEFITS / PERMANENT POSITON

About The Team / Role

The Sales Manager at WEX International will proactively drive strategic new business development initiatives to achieve significant growth and market expansion within the EMEA travel vertical. This role is crucial for hitting sales targets and securing new revenue streams specifically from Online Travel Agencies (OTAs), Travel Management Companies (TMCs), and other travel intermediaries.

While this role is anchored in London, we welcome applications from candidates based in our European locations: Dublin, Lille, Berlin, Breda, and Rome.

What’s On Offer

  • Highly competitive salary (dependent on experience)
  • Uncapped Commission
  • 40-hour week – Monday to Friday
  • Hybrid working- from either our Manchester or London Bridge office
  • Industry leading pension scheme
  • Private Medical
  • 25 days holiday plus Bank Holidays – with the opportunity to purchase additional holidays
  • Life assurance
  • Income protection
  • Gym Flex membership
  • Discounts & perks platform
  • Employee wellbeing

How You’ll Make An Impact

  • Develop and execute strategic business development plans to exceed regional sales targets and acquire new clients specifically within the OTA, TMC, and broader travel intermediary sectors.
  • Proactively identify, pursue, and secure new business opportunities to expand WEX International's market share within the travel vertical.
  • Build and nurture a robust pipeline of potential clients, partners, and key decision-makers within the travel industry, converting prospects into loyal customers.
  • Lead negotiations and close deals with travel sector clients, demonstrating strong commercial acumen to maximize profitability.
  • Collaborate intensely with product, marketing, and operational teams to craft and deliver compelling, tailored payment solutions that win new business within the travel industry.
  • Represent WEX International at key travel industry events, actively networking to generate leads and promote our solutions to potential clients.
  • Consistently meet and exceed individual and team sales targets.
  • Engage strategically with cross-functional teams to ensure all client needs are met, facilitating successful onboarding of new accounts in the travel sector.
  • Contribute to mentoring and potentially scaling a high-performing business development team, fostering a sales-driven culture focused on the travel vertical.

Experience You’ll Bring

  • Proven ability to translate sales strategies into actionable plans with a track record of successful execution within the travel industry.
  • Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills with a strong focus on closing deals.
  • Deep understanding of the payments industry, particularly within the EMEA region, with a specialized understanding of the travel vertical (OTAs, TMCs, travel intermediaries).
  • Highly goal-oriented and results-driven, with an unwavering commitment to achieving and exceeding sales targets and business objectives.
  • Demonstrated ability to drive substantial business growth in the EMEA region, specifically within the travel sector.
  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, or a related field.
  • Proven experience in business development or sales within the payments or financial services industry, with a strong emphasis on new client acquisition in the travel vertical (OTAs, TMCs, or travel intermediaries).
  • Fluency in English is essential; additional European languages are a significant asset for market expansion.
  • Willingness and ability to travel extensively throughout the EMEA region to secure new business within the travel sector.

What’s Next?

If you have the skills and passion to take on this Sales Manager we would love to hear from you. APPLY NOW for immediate consideration.

Due to the volume of applications received, unfortunately it is not possible to respond to each one individually, applicants that are shortlisted will be contacted within one week of application

We do not accept speculative CVs. Any CV received by WEX will be treated as a gift and eligible for an agency fee. PSL agencies should only send CVs if authorised to do so by the Talent Acquisition team.

#IRELAND #FRANCE #GERMANY #ITALY # NETHERLANDS
job.title: Sales Manager

job.company_description:
We’re adventure seekers. Smile givers. Impact makers.
We believe in the power of travel. It broadens horizons for our customers, and for our people too. New places to live, new roles to explore, new communities to join. It’s yours for the taking.
We’re TUI, a leading global travel and leisure experience company that makes holiday dreams come true for people around the world.
We are one of the world’s leading tourism groups counting 1200 travel agencies and online portals, five airlines with around 130 aircraft, over 400 hotels, 16 cruise liners, digital platforms for tours and activities and most importantly over 60 000 colleagues around the world. What unites us is creating moments that enrich the lives of our 21 million customers.
Beside the unforgettable experience we provide our customers, we also believe in the good that tourism can bring and we care deeply about our environmental impact. The TUI Care foundation has projects in over 30 countries worldwide, building on the potential of tourism as a global force for development. The Foundation opens up new perspectives through education and training, empowers local communities to benefit from the success of tourism and engages in the protection of nature and the environment.
At TUI we simply say “Let’s TUI it”. For us, that means creating happiness. Tackling challenges every day together, with a positive, can-do attitude, and finding solutions even for the most unexpected situations. Our teams across TUI are just as diverse as our destinations. So whether you have lots of experience or none at all. If you want to work in an office or feel best in the field. No matter if you’re an accomplished tech whizz, an aspiring entertainer, or simply in love with flying.
There’s a place for you here.
job.job_description:
Here at TUI, you’ll find we’re an inclusive company with a culture of care and by joining the Retail Team as an Assistant Manager in our Hastings store, you’ll work alongside your manager to lead, inspire, coach and develop a team to become destination experts to recommend and sell holidays, Cruise, UK Breaks and additional products whilst delivering exceptional sales and customer service. You’ll put all customers at the heart of our business by creating unforgettable holiday experiences that make us the best-loved holiday company.

As part of our dedication to delivering exceptional customer service and flexibility for our customers, you’ll enjoy a varied shift pattern each week including weekends and bank holidays, and evenings at some of our busier stores.

About Our Offer

  • TUI offers much more than just a starting salary of £13.81 per hour
  • Be rewarded for exceeding your targets with our commission scheme
  • Enjoy fantastic holiday benefits, discounts, special offers and the ability to purchase additional TUI time off
  • Benefit from our Health and Wellbeing across key areas – Financial, Health, Social, Community and Career
  • Investment in your development and career progression
  • Pension scheme and life assurance.


About The Job

  • Lead by example and role model company values to inspire your team and deliver excellent customer service
  • Personality and experience will assist you to guide, coach and develop the team to collaboratively reach high performance and standards that drive engagement
  • Assist with developing the team to be proactive and actively investigate and resolve every question or complaint. If things don’t go quite right, you’ll have the confidence to step in and take ownership
  • As technology advances so does TUI, you’ll work with a variety of systems empowering you to enhance your knowledge to actively improve our customer digital journey
  • Monitor and report on customer feedback, spot trends, improve customer satisfaction and loyalty to grow our business
  • Assist with recruiting talent, maintaining high levels of engagement and low levels of attrition.


About You

  • Proven passion for leading by example and delivering excellent customer service
  • Confident and fun motivator with a strong drive to achieve goals and sales targets
  • Skilled at balancing multiple tasks and confident to stay motivated when challenges arise
  • Flexible to embrace change and adapt easily to new ways of working
  • Trusted mentor with a fair and inclusive management style who recognises people are our most valued asset
  • An organised individual who takes pride in their work and able to communicate effectively
  • Able to work a varied shift pattern each week in-line with shop opening and closing times.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We're committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please let us know.
job.title: Retail Assistant Manager

job.job_description:
Here at TUI, you’ll find we’re an inclusive company with a culture of care. By joining the Retail Team as a Deputy Manager at our Canterbury shop, you’ll work alongside the manager to lead, inspire, coach and develop a large team to become destination experts to recommend and sell holidays, Cruise, UK Breaks and additional products, achieving sales and service targets through providing exceptional sales and service. You’ll put all customers at the heart of our business by creating unforgettable holiday experiences that make us the best-loved holiday company.

As part of our dedication to delivering exceptional customer service and flexibility for our customers, you’ll enjoy a varied shift pattern each week including weekends and bank holidays, and evenings at some of our busier stores.

About Our Offer

  • TUI offers much more than just a starting salary of £14.83 per hour
  • Be rewarded for exceeding your targets with our commission and discretionary bonus schemes
  • Enjoy fantastic holiday benefits, discounts, special offers and the ability to purchase additional TUI time off
  • Benefit from our Health and Wellbeing across key areas – Financial, Health, Social, Community and Career
  • Investment in your development and career progression
  • Pension scheme and life assurance.


About The Job

  • Lead by example and role model company values to inspire your team and deliver excellent customer service
  • Personality and experience will assist you to guide, coach and develop your team to collaboratively reach high performance and standards that drive engagement
  • Assist your manager in developing the team to be proactive and actively investigate and resolve every question or complaint. If things don’t go quite right, you’ll have the confidence to step in and take ownership
  • As technology advances so does TUI, you’ll work with a variety of systems empowering you to enhance your knowledge to actively improve our customer digital journey
  • Monitor and report on customer feedback, spot trends, improve customer satisfaction and loyalty to grow our business
  • Assist with recruiting talent, maintaining a high level of engagement and a low level of attrition.


About You

  • Proven passion for leading by example and delivering excellent customer service
  • Confident and fun motivator and driven to achieve goals and sales targets
  • Skilled at balancing multiple tasks, following company policies and procedures and confident to stay motivated when challenges arise
  • Flexible to embrace change and adapt easily to new ways of working
  • Trusted mentor with a fair and inclusive management style who recognises people are our most valued asset
  • An organised individual who takes pride in their work and communicates effectively
  • Able to work a varied shift pattern each week in-line with shop opening and closing times.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We're committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please let us know.

job.job_description:
Bij TUI geloven we dat reizen meer betekent dan simpelweg aankomen op een bestemming. Het draait om het creëren van blijvende herinneringen, het verkennen van diverse culturen en het verrijken van levens. Ons gepassioneerde team van reisprofessionals streeft ernaar deze visie te vervullen voor elke klant die bij ons binnenstapt.

In de positie van Senior Reisadviseur ben jij de cruciale schakel tussen onze klanten en de reiservaringen van hun dromen. Je taak omvat niet enkel het boeken van rondreizen, stedentrips en verre vakanties, maar ook het coachen van een gedreven team. Met jouw coachingskwaliteiten zorg je ervoor dat jouw team excelleert en hun volle potentieel bereikt.

OVER ONS AANBOD

  • Een bruto startsalaris van € 2952,96 (afhankelijk van wat je aan ervaring meebrengt);
  • Je start je carrière met een 2-weekse training om vertrouwd te raken met onze werkwijze en bedrijfscultuur;
  • Keuze uit een TUI leaseauto of OV abonnement; 
  • Leuke extra’s zoals 40% korting op TUI reizen, IPB (indien plaats beschikbaar), korting bij diverse pretparken en een gratis doorlopende reisverzekering;
  • Een stimulerende en samenwerkingsgerichte werkomgeving waarbij je een impact maakt op het leven van reizigers;
  • Kansen voor professionele groei en ontwikkeling.


OVER DE FUNCTIE

  • Begeleiden en coachen van het team om hun kennis en prestaties te versterken en het geven van trainingen aan nieuwe teamleden om hun start te vergemakkelijken;
  • Met jouw commerciële aanpak zorg jij ervoor dat de gestelde doelen (KPI's) worden gehaald;
  • Verbeteren van operationele processen om een naadloze en efficiënte samenwerking te waarborgen;
  • Uitvoeren van baliewerkzaamheden, waaronder het op maat samenstellen van reisplannen en het reserveren van diverse reisarrangementen;
  • Coördineren van klant inspiratieavonden en open dagen om onze klanten te informeren en te enthousiasmeren over nieuwe reiservaringen.


OVER JOU

  • Je hebt een afgeronde MBO4 opleiding aangevuld met relevante werkervaring of je hebt een afgeronde opleiding HBO Toerisme en bent op zoek naar een uitdagende baan;
  • Je hebt het vermogen om een team te inspireren en te motiveren;
  • Uitstekende communicatie- en coachingsvaardigheden, samenwerken en salesgericht;
  • Creativiteit en organisatorische vaardigheden om evenementen en klantbijeenkomsten te organiseren.


Ben jij gereed om de leiding te nemen bij het vormgeven van reiservaringen en het coachen van een toegewijd team? Aarzel niet om nu te solliciteren en word een sleutelfiguur in het transformeren van de manier waarop onze klanten de wereld ontdekken!

TUI staat voor gelijke kansen voor alle individuen. We moedigen daarom personen van diverse achtergronden en ervaringsniveaus aan om te solliciteren. We kijken uit naar jouw sollicitatie en naar de kans om samen onvergetelijke reiservaringen te creëren!

Een assessment maakt onderdeel uit van de sollicitatieprocedure

Van een werkplek naar een plek om erbij te horen. Bij TUI omarmen we diversiteit, gelijkheid en inclusie, en moedigen we iedereen aan om te komen zoals je bent, want samen is ons potentieel grenzeloos.*

*Wij zetten ons in om kandidaten met een handicap of beperking te ondersteunen, dus als u ondersteuning nodig hebt, laat het ons dan weten.

job.title: Retail Assistant Manager

job.company_description: WNS, part of Capgemini, is a global Agentic AI-powered intelligent operations and transformation company. WNS combines deep industry knowledge with technology, analytics, and process expertise to co-create innovative, digitally-led transformational solutions with over 700+ clients across various industries. WNS delivers an entire spectrum of transformative solutions that entail industry-specific offerings, customer experience services, finance and accounting, human resources, procurement, and data-led analytics solutions to solve operational challenges and drive strategic growth journeys for businesses. As of June 30, 2025, WNS has 66,000+ professionals across 64 delivery centers worldwide, including facilities in the United States, the United Kingdom, Canada, Turkey, Poland, Romania, China, Costa Rica, Malaysia, the Philippines, South Africa, Sri Lanka, and India.
job.job_description:
Company Description

WNS (Part of Capgemini), is a leading Business Process Management (BPM) company. We combine our deep industry knowledge with technology and analytics expertise to co-create innovative, digital-led transformational solutions with clients across 10 industries. We enable businesses in Travel, Insurance, Banking and Financial Services, Manufacturing, Retail and Consumer Packaged Goods, Shipping and Logistics, Healthcare, and Utilities to re-imagine their digital future and transform their outcomes with operational excellence. We deliver an entire spectrum of BPM services in finance and accounting, procurement, customer interaction services and human resources leveraging collaborative models that are tailored to address the unique business challenges of each client. We co-create and execute the future vision of 400+ clients with the help of our 64,000+ employees.

Job Description

Title: Group Manager

Relevant retail Experience: 7+ years

Location: UK

Summary:

We are seeking a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in Retail Analytics to work with our client in UK who heads Loyalty and advanced analytics. The ideal candidate will combine deep technical expertise with business acumen to build and scale analytics capabilities, strengthen client partnerships, and drive practice growth.

This role requires hands-on proficiency in SQL, Power BI, Azure Databricks, along with leadership experience in supporting the offshore delivery teams, developing frameworks, and supporting account farming in a dynamic retail environment

Job Description

  • Provide strong domain knowledge and thought leadership in the retail space, supporting loyalty, customer engagement, and related workstreams
  • Independently work with the client stakeholders to understand their business challenges/ needs and define the right analytics approach to design impactful solutions
  • Manage day-to-day delivery, work hands-on with data, and guide the offshore team in executing analytics projects involving large datasets.
  • Introduce innovative ideas to build solutions in Loyalty, Promotion Effectiveness, Digital Marketing, Campaign Management, Data Insights, and related areas.
  • Work extensively with SQL, Databricks, and Power BI to deliver high-quality analytics outputs.
  • Provide technical thought leadership, coaching, and mentorship in data science while collaborating with engineering and cross-functional teams.
  • Prepare and deliver business and technical presentations, reports, and other documents for client communication and business development.
  • Communicate confidently and effectively with client stakeholders, present recommendations clearly, and drive adoption of proposed business actions
  • Support account farming in a dynamic retail environment


Qualifications

Bachelors Degree

Additional Information

Must Have skills

  • Hands-on experience with SQL, Databricks, and Power BI.
  • Strong retail domain expertise (non-banking).
  • Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder-management skills
job.title: Subject Matter Expert - Analytics ( Retail Domain)

  • Personality and experience will assist you to guide, coach and develop the team to collaboratively reach high performance and standards that drive engagement
  • Assist with developing the team to be proactive and actively investigate and resolve every question or complaint. If things don’t go quite right, you’ll have the confidence to step in and take ownership
  • As technology advances so does TUI, you’ll work with a variety of systems empowering you to enhance your knowledge to actively improve our customer digital journey
  • Monitor and report on customer feedback, spot trends, improve customer satisfaction and loyalty to grow our business
  • Assist with recruiting talent, maintaining high levels of engagement and low levels of attrition.


About You

  • Proven passion for leading by example and delivering excellent customer service
  • Confident and fun motivator with a strong drive to achieve goals and sales targets
  • Skilled at balancing multiple tasks and confident to stay motivated when challenges arise
  • Flexible to embrace change and adapt easily to new ways of working
  • Trusted mentor with a fair and inclusive management style who recognises people are our most valued asset
  • An organised individual who takes pride in their work and able to communicate effectively
  • Able to work a varied shift pattern each week in-line with shop opening and closing times.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We're committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please let us know.
job.title: Retail Assistant Manager

job.company_description: WNS, part of Capgemini, is a global Agentic AI-powered intelligent operations and transformation company. WNS combines deep industry knowledge with technology, analytics, and process expertise to co-create innovative, digitally-led transformational solutions with over 700+ clients across various industries. WNS delivers an entire spectrum of transformative solutions that entail industry-specific offerings, customer experience services, finance and accounting, human resources, procurement, and data-led analytics solutions to solve operational challenges and drive strategic growth journeys for businesses. As of June 30, 2025, WNS has 66,000+ professionals across 64 delivery centers worldwide, including facilities in the United States, the United Kingdom, Canada, Turkey, Poland, Romania, China, Costa Rica, Malaysia, the Philippines, South Africa, Sri Lanka, and India.
job.job_description:
Company Description

WNS Global Services Inc. (NYSE: WNS) is a global Business Process Management (BPM) leader. WNS offers business value to 400+global clients by combining operational excellence with deep domain expertise in key industry verticals, including Banking and Financial Services, Consulting and Professional Services, Healthcare, Insurance, Manufacturing, Retail and Consumer Packaged Goods, Shipping and Logistics, Telecommunications, Travel and Utilities. Globally, the group’s over 44,000+ Professionals serve across 60 delivery centers in 16 countries worldwide.

WNS Poland - Launched operations in August 2012 with focus on European language capabilities. Key industries served: finance & accounting, insurance, shipping & logistics, customer service and travel.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities:

  • Investment Simulation and Financial Planning: Support markets with ENSUM+(Expert Net Sales Uplift Model) modelling for Expert-related financial planning. Develop and run ad-hoc investment choice simulations to inform local decisions. Empower markets with actionable insights.
  • Meta-Analysis and Knowledge Sharing: Consolidate ENSUM+ & Expert MMM learnings from markets to inform central analyses and business-wide applications Identify Expert growth drivers and support prioritization across BMCs.
  • Data Quality and Transformation: Conduct data quality audits and transform Expert-related datasets for use in MMM and other analytics. Enable reliable and accurate data for value tracking and decision-making.
  • Data Infrastructure and Automation Support: Transition Expert tracking data to centralised systems. Support implementation of new analytics use-cases requiring data asset access and triangulation.
  • KPI vs. Market Sales Analysis: Lead end-to-end analysis triangulating Expert KPIs with market share/sales data. Deliver insights to support investor relations and executive decision-making.
  • Testing and Validation: Design and execute new test-and-learn experiments identified in the 2026-27 roadmap. Improve predictive models and business planning assumptions. Run validation of tools/models


Qualifications

Skills & Experience Required:

  • Proven statistical modelling experience in a commercial/marketing context (e.g. regression/time series, attribution, propensity, conversion, what-if)
  • Proven hands-on data capability: SQL + (Python or R), Excel (PowerQuery, VBA)
  • Ability to translate analytics/modelling outputs into business decisions (clear storytelling, recommendations to non-tech stakeholders, stakeholder engagement)
  • Comfort working across multiple data sources/systems (Enterprise data environments)
  • Minimum 5+ years of relevant experience


Nice to Haves:

  • Experience delivering analytics products (dashboards/reporting/automation OR model outputs used by non-tech stakeholders)
  • Econometrics modelling (MMM, adstock/saturation/lag etc)
  • Sales performance measurement (Sales force effectiveness, CRM activity analytics)
  • Survey analytics (e.g SPSS)
  • Power BI (or other viz tools)
  • Cloud platforms (Azure/Databricks etc)
  • Healthcare/OTC/HCP domain experience


Additional Information

What we offer

  • Work for international client
  • Bonuses based on performance
  • Benefits (you can choose from a variety of options: MultiSport card, private medical care in Luxmed, cinema tickets, vouchers to different shops, etc.)
  • Employee Support Program - mental and legal support program dedicated for WNS employees and members of their families
  • Empowering CSR initiatives - WNS supports charity events

job.job_description:
Company Description

WNS (Holdings) Limited (NYSE: WNS), is a leading Business Process Management (BPM) company. We combine our deep industry knowledge with technology and analytics expertise to co-create innovative, digital-led transformational solutions with clients across 10 industries. We enable businesses in Travel, Insurance, Banking and Financial Services, Manufacturing, Retail and Consumer Packaged Goods, Shipping and Logistics, Healthcare, and Utilities to re-imagine their digital future and transform their outcomes with operational excellence. We deliver an entire spectrum of BPM services in finance and accounting, procurement, customer interaction services and human resources leveraging collaborative models that are tailored to address the unique business challenges of each client. We co-create and execute the future vision of 400+ clients with the help of our 62,000+ employees.

Job Description

  • Responsible for developing and implementing strategies to enhance our Energy & Utilities capabilities.
  • Working with the business development and solutions teams to support solution design and bid activity
  • Creating partnership to develop new capabilities to develop new capabilities for WNS to take to market
  • Working with marketing team to help promote the E&U capabilities
  • Expertise on RPA, AI, ML & NLP Solutions, CX Management, RFI / RFP, Prospect due diligence & Transition, Creating TOM, Project Management, Digital marketing strategist, Training, and mentoring projects.
  • Implementing new age Digital solutions, Data Analytics, and Process Excellence initiatives to drive Businesses Transformation and Ops Modernisation
  • Strategic Planning and Execution: Develop and implement capability strategies aligned with the BU's overall goals and vision. Identify and address capability gaps to ensure success. Develop and manage capability frameworks and initiatives.
  • Stakeholder Management: Collaborate with leaders across the organisation to align capability initiatives with business needs. Build strong relationships with key stakeholders, including employees, managers, and external partners. Communicate the value of capability initiatives to stakeholders.
  • Data Analysis and Reporting: Collect and analyse data to track the effectiveness of capability initiatives. Prepare reports and presentations to communicate findings and recommendations to stakeholders. Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to capability development.


Qualifications

Bachelors Degree

job.job_description:
Company Description

WNS (Holdings) Limited (NYSE: WNS), is a leading Business Process Management (BPM) company. We combine our deep industry knowledge with technology and analytics expertise to co-create innovative, digital-led transformational solutions with clients across 10 industries. We enable businesses in Travel, Insurance, Banking and Financial Services, Manufacturing, Retail and Consumer Packaged Goods, Shipping and Logistics, Healthcare, and Utilities to re-imagine their digital future and transform their outcomes with operational excellence. We deliver an entire spectrum of BPM services in finance and accounting, procurement, customer interaction services and human resources leveraging collaborative models that are tailored to address the unique business challenges of each client. We co-create and execute the future vision of 400+ clients with the help of our 62,000+ employees.

Job Description

The Senior Vice President - Sales for BFS ( Banking & Financial Services) will be responsible for acquiring new clients and achieving sales targets for WNS' services in the United Kingdom. Key responsibilities include:

  • Establish and cultivate revenue-generating relationships with C-suite executives at targeted firms, while rigorously pursuing monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets.
  • Manage the entire sales process from initial client communication through contract execution, serving as the primary point of contact for all prospect and customer interactions.
  • Collaborate with WNS' presales and delivery teams to ensure proposed offerings align with customers' business and technology requirements, providing comprehensive support during initial engagement phases.
  • Conduct market research and competitive positioning analysis in conjunction with regional teams, while adhering to all WNS Sales, Human Resource, and corporate ethical policies.
  • Demonstrate exceptional communication and negotiation skills to establish credibility, trust, and long-term business relationships with senior-level executives at leading BFS firms.
  • Maintain strong sales management focus during extended sales cycles, typically ranging from six months to one year, and engage in regular travel to support lead generation, deliver presentations, and foster ongoing relationships.
  • Develop and implement strategic sales plans to expand WNS' market share and penetrate new industry verticals within the United States.
  • Lead and mentor a high-performing sales team, setting clear objectives, providing ongoing coaching, and conducting regular performance evaluations to ensure individual and team success.
  • Collaborate with marketing teams to develop targeted campaigns, create compelling sales collateral, and participate in industry events to enhance WNS' brand visibility and generate qualified leads.
  • Provide regular reports and forecasts to senior management, offering insights on market trends, competitive landscape, and recommendations for improving sales strategies and processes.


This role demands a consummate professional with a proven track record of success in selling outsourcing services, consistently exceeding client acquisition and sales revenue targets.

Qualifications

Graduate with exp in selling BPM Services to BFS industry

Additional Information

Individual Contributor who can drive Sales in Banking & Financial Services & worked in Business Outsourcing Firm.

  • Expertise in Consultative Selling & BPM/BPO Sales.
  • Domain Expertise in BFSI
  • Expertise in Large Deals & Good C Level connects within BFSI Sector
  • Proven Hunter in previous stints
job.title: Senior Vice President - Sales for BFS ( Banking & Financial Services)

job.job_description:
Company Description

WNS (Holdings) Limited (NYSE: WNS), is a leading Business Process Management (BPM) company. We combine our deep industry knowledge with technology and analytics expertise to co-create innovative, digital-led transformational solutions with clients across 10 industries. We enable businesses in Travel, Insurance, Banking and Financial Services, Manufacturing, Retail and Consumer Packaged Goods, Shipping and Logistics, Healthcare, and Utilities to re-imagine their digital future and transform their outcomes with operational excellence.We deliver an entire spectrum of BPM services in finance and accounting, procurement, customer interaction services and human resources leveraging collaborative models that are tailored to address the unique business challenges of each client. We co-create and execute the future vision of 400+ clients with the help of our 62,000+ employees.

Job Description

About the Role:

We are looking for an accomplished and growth-oriented Senior Vice President – Sales to lead our Analytics sales initiatives across the UK geography, with deep domain focus on the Manufacturing, Retail & CPG (MRC) and/or Hi-Tech Professional Services (HPS) verticals. This role is critical to shaping and executing our go-to-market strategy, nurturing C-level relationships, and driving significant revenue growth in complex enterprise environments.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Own and drive the UK sales strategy for Analytics services, focusing on the MRC and HPS domains.
  • Develop and execute strategic account plans to acquire new clients and grow existing accounts across manufacturing, retail, CPG, and hi-tech segments.
  • Partner with solutioning, delivery, and marketing teams to craft high-impact value propositions and client-centric proposals.
  • Manage the full sales cycle: from prospecting and qualification through to negotiations, deal closure, and client onboarding.
  • Actively engage in thought leadership initiatives, representing the company at industry forums, conferences, and events.
  • Build and maintain a robust pipeline aligned with quarterly and annual sales targets.
  • Provide market intelligence and domain insights to inform sales strategy and solution development.
  • Foster trusted advisor relationships with key stakeholders, including client CXOs and business heads


Qualifications

  • 15+ years of progressive sales experience in the B2B services industry, with at least 5+ years in a senior sales leadership role.
  • Proven success in selling Analytics services or solutions in the MRC (Manufacturing, Retail & CPG) and HPS (Hi-Tech Professional Services) domains.
  • Deep understanding of the UK market with a well-developed network in the target verticals.
  • Demonstrated ability to close large, complex deals and exceed multi-million dollar revenue targets.
  • Strong commercial acumen, consultative selling approach, and negotiation skills.
  • Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, particularly with CXO-level decision-makers.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Technology, or related discipline; MBA is an advantage.


Additional Information

Preferred Competencies:

  • Strong understanding of domain-led research, analytics, and digital transformation trends in MRC and HPS.
  • Ability to align customer pain points with data-driven, insight-led solutions.
  • Agile, entrepreneurial mindset with a passion for driving innovation and value.
  • Leadership experience in building and mentoring high-performance sales teams (if applicable).
job.title: Senior Vice President of Sales - Analytics

job.job_description:
Position Summary:

We are looking for an experienced and results-oriented Manager/Senior Manager in Procurement Consulting to lead procurement transformation and cost optimization initiatives across diverse industries. In this role, you will drive impactful change, deliver measurable value for clients, and strengthen procurement capabilities through strategic advisory and hands-on implementation support.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead end-to-end procurement transformation and cost optimization projects across multiple industries, ensuring delivery on time, within scope, and on budget.
  • Manage client engagements, acting as a primary point of contact for senior stakeholders and building long-term, trust-based relationships.
  • Conduct comprehensive procurement diagnostics, spend analysis, and opportunity assessments to identify value creation levers.
  • Design and implement category strategies, sourcing strategies, and supplier management frameworks.
  • Drive strategic sourcing initiatives including RFP/RFQ processes, negotiations, contract development, and supplier selection.
  • Develop and implement target operating models (TOM) for procurement functions, including governance, processes, tools, and KPIs.
  • Support digital procurement transformation initiatives, including system selection and implementation advisory.
  • Lead and mentor junior consultants and analysts, providing guidance, feedback, and professional development support.
  • Ensure high-quality deliverables, including executive presentations, business cases, savings tracking, and transformation roadmaps.
  • Monitor project performance metrics and proactively manage risks and issues.
  • Contribute to business development activities, including proposal preparation, client pitches, and thought leadership initiatives.


Qualifications:

  • Master’s degree or MBA is a plus.
  • 8–10 years of experience in procurement, strategic sourcing, supply chain consulting or procurement transformation roles.
  • Strong knowledge of procurement best practices, procurement tools, category management, supplier relationship management (SRM), and contract negotiation.
  • Proficiency in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI, Tableau). Knowledge of sourcing platforms (e.g., Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer) is a plus.
  • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with experience presenting to senior leadership.
  • Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced, client-facing environment.
  • Consulting experience required.
  • German language a strong preference.


Why Join Us?

  • Work with a diverse portfolio of clients and industries.
  • Be part of a dynamic, collaborative, and growth-oriented consulting environment.
  • Opportunities for professional development and advancement.
  • English or German lessons with a native speaker.
  • Access to the MyBenefit platform.
  • Private medical care.
job.title: Manager/Senior Manager Procurement Consulting

job.job_description:
Company Description

WNS (Holdings) Limited (NYSE: WNS), is a leading Business Process Management (BPM) company. We combine our deep industry knowledge with technology and analytics expertise to co-create innovative, digital-led transformational solutions with clients across 10 industries. We enable businesses in Travel, Insurance, Banking and Financial Services, Manufacturing, Retail and Consumer Packaged Goods, Shipping and Logistics, Healthcare, and Utilities to re-imagine their digital future and transform their outcomes with operational excellence.We deliver an entire spectrum of BPM services in finance and accounting, procurement, customer interaction services and human resources leveraging collaborative models that are tailored to address the unique business challenges of each client. We co-create and execute the future vision of 400+ clients with the help of our 63,000+ employees.

Job Description

Senior Vice President – Sales ( HI Tech & Professional Services Sales Expert) - Deal Generation, Hunting, Individual Contributor in Generating Sales Pipeline.

The SVP will be a member of sales leadership team at WNS for HPS Business Unit. This person will have overall responsibility of delivering recognized annual revenues as per a Personal Performance Plan by networking, generating leads, assessing opportunities, creatively building solutions, overseeing the bid process to create the winning proposal, through to contract closure.

Specific Responsibilities:

  • Building a sales strategy for UK/Europe region for HI Tech & Professional Services and running the sales process accordingly.
  • Refining client segments in all possible areas within the different sectors of the HPS industry
  • Develop and coordinate sales selling cycle and methodology with all constituencies like marketing, operations, finance, etc.
  • Agree on sales quota for signed revenue and sales target for billed revenue in line with knowledge services practices.
  • Developing new accounts and opening avenues for new opportunities using personal rolodex, WNS lead generation engines (website, lead generation unit), direct mailers, etc.
  • Sensitizing the management on the latest trends in the Healthcare segment and ideating on developing new businesses, products or service lines
  • Leading responses to RFIs, RFPs, RFQs for clients
  • Conducting diagnostics and opportunity assessment studies along with experts
  • Managing clients visits and accompanying them to Indian and other WNS Operation Centers as and when required
  • Analyzing and evaluating the effectiveness of sales, methods, sales costs and results
  • Develop, manage and syndicate sales budgets
  • Managing ongoing client relationships through steering committee reviews, etc.
  • Handing over sold accounts to program management


In addition to the above responsibilities, as per the need of the Business Unit Head, this role would be required to support specific HPS accounts as and when required.

Further you will perform all the duties and other functions as are consistent with your job title and any additional duties and functions as the Company may require from time to time.

Qualifications

  • A Masters degree in business or statistics preferable
  • 10 to 15 years of industry knowledge and/or sales experience out of which at least few years should have been in a senior capacity as a salesperson ideally with UK-based HI Tech or Professional Services Firms
  • In-depth knowledge of the HPS market in the UK and a thorough understanding of opportunities in off shoring processes.
  • Strong connections and contacts in the industry and experience with outsourcing and the offshoring industry.
  • A track record of initiative, creativity and motivation to surpass challenging performance goals and sales targets.
  • Excellent presentation and negotiation skills, able to create and deliver convincing arguments
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and the desire to lead sales in the (Vertical) business or financial services space in high-growth company with operations in India
  • Ability to manage sales process and forecast with precision
  • Strong interpersonal skills, able to build rapport and credibility quickly with an executive-level audience
  • Superior time management skills and strong attention to detail
  • Solid grounding in business concepts and dynamics


Additional Information

ROLE TITLE/ DESIGNATION

Designation: Senior Vice President – Sales

Role: Vertical Sales Leader

Reporting to: Chief Growth Officer

Location: Europe

Role Span: Individual Contributor

Positioning: Senior Leadership

ROLE OVERVIEW: SNAPSHOT

A key member of the BU Leadership Team who will influence and deliver tactical and strategic initiatives that will drive toward achieving the medium to long term objectives of the BU (Business Unit). This role will report into the Chief Growth Officer. You will be part of highly motivated, driven, and high performing sales team
job.title: Senior Vice President - Sales - Hi-Tech & Professional Services
